boardgame: This Is Not a Test: Eat, Prey, Live
description:
Eat, Prey, Live is a set of three playable scenarios for This Is Not a Test designed to test the mettle, strength, and survivability of your warband. Each scenario introduces a new wasteland creature or hazard and provides additional depth to the history and (de)evolution of the Tri-State Wasteland.

Eat, Prey, Live features the following:

New Scenario (Eat) – Your warband will need more than a rod and real to bring these fish home. Look out for giant mutant crabs.

New Scenario (Prey) – Mutant turkeys are on the menu, but things may get a little explosive.

New Scenario (Live) – This is playing TNT on hard mode!  Can your warband survive fall out from the fallout!
family: Game: This Is Not a Test

description:
This mid-19th century game is quite different from the asymmetric movement game of the same name. This is strictly a die-rolling game, albeit also asymmetrical.

The board is a rectangular sheet of paper showing six artillery pieces behind earthworks firing at a multi-turreted castle. The castle walls are marked with 28 combinations of 2D6, some duplicated, while four piles of rocks in front of the castle are marked with doubles 1 through 4. (The main gates are marked with doubles 5 and 6.)

Above the castle are six spaces decorated with artillery pieces, each with a single 2D6 combination, and empty squares covering the rest of the possibilities. Players roll 2D6 alternately, and to win, the attacking player must destroy all the segments of the castle before the defending player rolls the six combinations needed to destroy his guns.

The game name and publication date of 1840 are handwritten on the reverse of the game sheet.

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game: Professor Pyg & Dollotrons
description: This expansion to the Batman Miniature Game introduces Professor Pyg and his Dollotrons. This white metal kit comes ready to assemble and paint.

Collect puzzle pieces to you Pippi-puzzle. When you land on your color, grab a puzzle piece. You can also get pieces by making your best funny face, sing a Pippi song, or pantomiming animals. But beware

The object of the game is to move by a throw of the dice and get all four of your men to the "Coin Store" first for the highest extra bonus on coin values (collected via "Coin Cards" accumulated on the way there). Your men may only move into play with a roll of a one on one of the dice. If more than one man is in play, the roll may be split with each man moving the number of spaces on one die. If doubles are thrown the player gets another roll. A man can only enter the "Coin Store" with an exact number. If more than one man is in play, the roll can be split to allow a man to enter with the exact number.

The player with the highest total of values (plus the bonus), as indicated on the "Coin Cards" held by each player at the end of the game is the winner. All players must finish because lesser bonuses are awarded for both second and third place and the player who finishes first may not necessarily finish with the highest total.

Armapalabras is the most dynamic and entertaining word game for all ages.

It includes 2 games with different levels of difficulty: Basket and Word.

For the kids: Educational and fun, exercise and expand vocabulary.

For the big ones: A challenge of agility and creativity.

CONTENT
Two decks of 40 cards each, instructions of the game "basket", instructions of the game "word" and a version of practice ..

---

Armapalabras es el más dinámico y entretenido juego de armar palabras para todas las edades.

Incluye 2 juegos con distintos niveles de dificultad: Canasta y Palabra.

Para los chicos: Educativo y divertido, ejercita y amplía el vocabulario.

Para los grandes: Un desafío de agilidad y creatividad.

CONTENIDO
Dos mazos de 40 cartas cada uno, instrucciones del juego “canasta”, instrucciones del juego “palabra” y una versión de práctica..

This is a game of balance and group interaction with different levels of difficulty.

The cards indicate different positions. If you take them, you and your friends will be RAVELLED.

The game advances, you hold cards on your head and they are stepping on you; But he resists ... the first unraveling loses!

It includes the games: Ravelled and The Equilibrist.

CONTENT

A deck of "Ravelled" cards, a deck of "Equilibrist" cards, 30 "R" cards, instructions and variants of game.

---

Las cartas indican diferentes posiciones, al cumplirlas, tú y tus amigos quedan ENREDADOS.

El juego avanza, sostienes tarjetas sobre tu cabeza y te están pisando; pero resiste… ¡el primero que se desenreda pierde!

Incluye los juegos: Enredados y El Equilibrista.

Juegos de equilibrio e interacción grupal.
Distintos niveles de dificultad.

CONTENIDO

Un mazo de cartas “Enredados”, uno de “El Equilibrista”, 30 tarjetas E, instrucciones y variantes de juego.

This is a visual perception game.

There are always two equal drawings ... But only two! 
Each card has 8 items and one and only one is repeated in another card. Can you find it?

¿Los ves? ("Do you see them?") is a set of multiple and very entertaining games of concentration and visual perception  both for children and adults.

Description (Spanish):

Siempre hay dos iguales... Pero solo dos! Cada carta tiene 8 elementos, y uno y solo uno, está repetido en otra carta ¿puedes encontrarlo?

¿Los Ves? es un set de múltiples y muy entretenidos juegos de concentración y percepción visual para grandes y chicos.

Juegos de percepción visual

Can you repeat everything Pepe says and does? ... And add something else?

"Each sixth chick sat on a stick."

Snoring with closed eyes.

"She sells seashells by the seashore."

Swim butterfly style.

This is a game with special cards of memory and corporal expression.

---

¿Puedes repetir Todo lo que Pepe Dice y hace… Y agregarle algo más?

“Quiero el panqueque que queda.”

Roncar con los ojos cerrados.

“María Chucena techaba su choza.”

Nadar estilo mariposa.

Juego con cartas especiales de memoria y expresión corporal.

boardgame: 講談級 大坂の陣 (Kodan kyu Osaka no Jin)
description:
Twin games of the Osaka Winter and Summer campaigns.

Both games are easy to learn and play.
Units are activated by Chits.  Each unit without stack execute operation including both of movement and attack.
Combat is resolved by attacker's and defender's fire. The higher moral unit fires first.

2 half-size standard maps
252 counters



Issue game of Game Journal #59.大坂の陣 = Siege of Osaka
講談級

Roll all eight dice or roll them one at a time. Weave your pirate story together using the figures on each die. If you roll the red superpower symbol, you may add a special ability to your character.

Each player can tell a story just for fun, or take turns telling a story from the same dice, and vote for the best-told story. The game is designed primarily for language-learners.

Excitement, adventure and super powers: these dice bring fun into every story! Let them inspire you on a daring pirate adventure story. And with some luck, the dice even give your hero super powers!

There are no limits to your imagination ...

~~~~~

Spannung, Abenteuer und Super-Kräfte: Diese Würfel bringen Pepp in jede Geschichte! Lass dich von ihnen zu einer verwegenen Piraten-Abenteuer-Geschichte inspirieren. Und mit etwas Glück verleihen die Würfel deinem Helden sogar Super-Kräfte!

Deiner Phantasie sind keine Grenzen gesetzt ...

Description from the publisher:

The world of Tabulette was at war. Creatures infused with the very Magics of the land lay siege to the many races and kingdoms of the land. The Dwarves ran to their caves, seeking refuge from the attacks, they sealed themselves away. 
The Elves used the remaining magic they had to conjurer a protective dome around their capital, that would keep them isolated for the next 500 years. The Paurian cat people of the jungles seemingly disappeared.The Honorable Canin people went north, far north of the Great Mountains. Humanity was all that was left to fend off the creatures hell bent on  destruction. Though the Magi Elders had worked to seal each creature,almost 5 centuries later, the seals have begun to break. 
The creatures have begun resurfacing and only the greatest Champions of the proud Banners can expel them again, hopefully this time, for good....

In Tabula Rasa, 2-4 Players race through one of the crumbling seals, to wade through the waves of enemies and defeat the great threat that lies there! Players will find gear for their Champion, learn new abilities and gather loot, all while completing the quests the banners had laid out for them. Though in the end, only one though will be honored in the Hall of Glory.

On each turn, players spend their crystals to performs actions and abilities, bringing them deeper into the threats lair, or allowing them to confront and defeat different threats and enemies that appear during the game.

Players will be able to build a better pool of Crystals to go back into their bag, as each time they must draw but cannot, their discard pile of used crystals goes back into the back, gets shaken up and drawn from again. Having a better or stronger pool gives you the ability to maximize your turns!

Some crystals fuel abilities, some are bad and need to be culled, and some are blank slates that slow you down. Building a good pool will help you complete quests and defeat minions, advancing the boss's threat track.

The Boss of the dungeon or lair isn't just going to sit back either. Every round their influence on the seal is felt, their minions attempt to stop you, and of course and as the game progresses on the threat track, when it reaches it's threshold, players ALL confront the ancient threat for glory.

When the seal has been cleaned out, players count up their Glory and the winner's legend rings out through the land as they enter the Hall of Glory!

Dinotopia: First Flight is the third Dinotopia book written by James Gurney. The first edition of the book included a fold-out board game. The front cover opens up to reveal an easy-to-learn board game. By Detaching the game cards from the back of the book, players can join Gideon on his adventures, experiencing his crushing setbacks and his high-flying triumphs.

There are a set of other Orange cards to the game I think that they are used to move around the foldout game board in the James Gurney book, I found and bough a second hand copy fo the book for my son and daughter but thee were some of the blue cards that you have displayed on your site missing from the book that I bought I was unaware of this at the time, but have since seen other copies of this book avalaible for sale. they have been quite expensive as the books by this author are now very collectable.

The players have to get their trains from the main station at the center of the game board to space 50 of one of the six destination stations. Movement is determined by a standard die (1-6) and a color-die (red, white, green), which has the following meanings:
white: free track, the train moves the double number of spaces.
red: blocked track, the train misses a turn.
green: the train moves backward.
If a train gets to a rail junction, the player has to raise his red flag, to signal that the trains on the other tracks have to wait till he passed the junction and removes the flag.
The first player to reach his destination station wins the game.

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Hammers of Ragnarok
description:
The worlds of Vikings and Pirates are about to collide!

There are few crews on the high seas more feared than the Hammers of Ragnarok. These sea-faring Vikings rejoice in the violence and bloodshed in battle. The only thing better than earning valuable booty from their raids would be to die in battle.

The Hammers of Ragnarok are a complete expansion faction for Rum and Bones Second Tide with their own Heroes, Tide cards, Deckhands, and ships. Any pirate out there will be quaking in their pantaloons when they spot the Hammers’ colors flying.

This is an expansion. The Rum & Bones: Second Tide core box is required to play.

Features:


     Long Ships - The Hammers of Ragnarok have their own long ships for engaging in battle with rival pirate forces!
     Viking Heroes - Five new Viking Heroes will lead their forces to take home some shiny pirate treasure or die in glorious battle. They’re fine either way.
     High Tide - With their own Tide deck to draw on for support, the Hammers of Ragnarok will be a force to be reckoned with!

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Mercenaries Hero's Chest
description:
These Heroes make a hearty crew, for the right price.

A good pirate captain is always on the lookout for some new recruits. They’ll lose some crew to scurvy, sunken ships, and in rare cases, retirement, so having a healthy supply of replacements on hand is crucial.

The Mercenaries Heroes Chest for Rum and Bones Second Tide, adds ten new Heroes willing to join any faction for the right price. These Heroes come with their own set of skills and special attacks. Any crew would be happy to have them on board!

This is an expansion. The Rum & Bones: Second Tide core game is required to play.

Features:


     Mini Mercenaries - All ten of the included Mercenaries have their own highly detailed miniature figure and Hero Dashboards, allowing them to join any pirate crew.
     Custom Cards - Each Hero comes with their own Skill cards that can be used to customize their abilities. As the game progresses, players will be able to choose how to develop them.
Solo scenario provided for free by Mayfair Games on their website that involves establishing relationships with the Envoy Colony.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Quest: Quest for the Golden Mushrooms
description:
In this mini-expansion the goblins have captured some of the most magical mushroom folk of the land and the heroes that can rescue them will be generously rewarded!

Quest for the Golden Mushrooms adds a capture-the-flag element to Tiny Epic Quest, as well as some additional variability through the addition of Golden Grotto Map Cards!

Note: This is included in the deluxe edition of Tiny Epic Quest

Tat's All Folks! is a 2 player worker placement micro-game based on American traditional tattoo artwork. The two players are rival tattooers in a shop, filling in available spots with their signature piece to gain more clients than the other and become known as the best.

Tat's All Folks! was built for economy, of both space and price. The modular game board is made of 6 double sided cards, creating 64 possible board permutations. 2 of the cards function as score trackers, sliding along the numerical track created by the remaining 4 cards on the board. A 7th card contains all the rules for gameplay, while a final 8th card can be cut up and used as game pieces. The entire game could easily fit in your wallet!

Following standard worker placement rules, the two players will alternate placing their 3 game pieces on the board to perform listed actions and gain/lose clients over the course of multiple rounds. Each player also has a single use special move, called the "Dead Man's Switch," that when activated grants them another immediate turn, which can make or break a player's strategy. The first player to reach 9 clients wins the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: A trivia box of over 700 questions and 130 challenges about TV Series, Litterature, Movies, Comics, Mangas and Anime, and Video Games.

Each card in a deck displays three pairs of phrases. The final word or words of the first phrase of a pair is/are the same as the first word or words of the second phrase of the pair. Within a time limit, a player tries to get his teammates to guess all six phrases and all three "combolutions" of two phrases on the card. Collect a token for each guessed combolution. The first team to twenty tokens wins.

Rhino The Game® is a trick-taking card and token game for 2 to 6 players. A Rhino deck consists of 55 premium playing cards: a special set of 52 playing cards numbered 0-12 in four color suits, along with two "Big Five" Bonus cards, and one Super Safari card. Each player begins the game with their own set of five special character tokens representing the "Big Five" African mammals - rhinoceros, elephant, lion, leopard, and Cape buffalo. Each token has unique properties to influence game scoring and may only be used once during the course of the game.
    
The object of the game is to bid correctly on the exact number of tricks that a player will take (win) in each subsequent round of play. Points are awarded for a correct bid and are either zero or negative for an incorrect bid. The player with most points after all rounds have been played is declared the winner. The game is played in 13 rounds with the number of cards dealt per round varying from seven to one and back to seven. Player strategy combining with statistical probability (chance) interacts the effects of dealt cards, Bonus Cards, and the character tokens. Multiple skill levels of play and a partner option with 4 or 6 players is available. Designed for ages 14 and up. However, younger precocious children may also pick up this game rapidly especially when guided by an experienced player.

"Could you become the richest, swankiest, most successful pop star in the world? Release records! Have hits! make pots of cash!

First you have to get a music record out and battle your way to the top of the hit parade against the other players. But keep your wits about you, because you'll have to use your skill and judgement to determine when to cash in on your chart success before you tumble down pop's dastardly dumper!"

Players move around the board collecting pieces to create their records. There are four pieces to every record A,B,C and D. A and B make the song name and C and D make up the first line of the single. You have to then make up, oh and also, sing the next line for your single to make it into the charts.

Whilst moving around the board you can steal, pass and lose un-used pieces of the record. But once it's created and in the charts you start to move up the charts and cash in on the popularity. Game play moves clockwise around the board and spaces and action/question cards indicate what the players must do to move play along. create the most successful records and collect money at the right time (preferably before the records slumps) to win!

Tic Tac Whoa! consists of a deck of 17 cards that display an "X" and 17 cards that display an "O". This deck can be used to play five games on a three by three grid. The rules common to all the games are (i) that a player plays both the "X" cards and the "O" cards, (ii) that a player can score by getting three in a row of either symbol, and (iii) that same-symbol cards cannot be played on top of each other.

This optional expansion adds unique new weapons that count as multiple weapon types.

Contains

     Morning Star
     Songbow
     Long knife
     Halberd
     Swift Bo Staff
     Magic Hand Armor

Description from the publisher:

What better way to rid your village and the surrounding lands of the werewolf menace but to head up a powerful hunting party, designed to do just that? The Huntress is a one-shot wonder guaranteed to kill her target. The Mad Bomber takes out the two players closest to him if he’s killed. The Revealer has the ability to kill every single werewolf in just one night...unless he’s wrong about who’s a werewolf, in which case he dies. The Mystic Seer knows the exact role of her target. The Mentalist knows if any pair of players are on the same team or not. Not to be outdone by all the newfound power of these villagers, the Werewolves have a new wolf: the Alpha Wolf, who can turn a player into a wolf at night instead of eliminating him.

Players are aiming to get their secret wagers to earn the most points. It is played with a deck of 56 cards consisting of 2's to 7's from two packs of cards. Plus 1 King of each suit and 4 Jokers. Finally add the layout cards of 1 Ace of each suit and another Joker.
The layout is placed on the table in this order from left to right:
Club Ace, Spade Ace, Joker, Heart Ace and finally Diamond Ace

Each player is dealt 12 cards. From these he selects two cards which will be his secret 'wager'. He may choose two of the same suit or one each from two of the suits. His chosen cards are kept face-down in front of him.

The remaining 10 cards that a player has are used to influence or deter cards in the layout to get as far left as they can. Being a Knizia game the card play is quite novel and a little complex even.

When each player has used up his 10 cards, that ends the game. The secret 'wagers' are revealed. If the suits you picked are in the first 3 positions you get 3, 2, or 1 point (for 1st, 2nd and 3rd) if the black suits or Joker are in those positions. You score double points for red cards that get into the first 3 spots which reflects their 'start' at the back of the order from the Start. If a suit you bet on comes last you lose 3 points regardless of the colour.

The designer suggests that you play one hand equal to the number of players and the player at the end with the most points, wins.

Description from the designer:

War: the Board Game is an abstract wargame which captures the most difficult strategic and tactical decisions faced by generals in a war with a minimum set of rules that can be explained in less than 5 minutes. The design is effectively a deconstruction of the concept of a wargame, removing all ornament and unnecessary layers of complexity in order to retain only its essence. Despite its parsimonious nature, in War: The Board Game players face situations that can be considered direct representations of the reality in warfare. They need to protect the supply lines and headquarters, capture prisoners, flank and surround enemies, take advantage of geographical features etc.

The strategic depth of the game and the absence of in-game randomness, aims at rewarding experienced players. However, the learning curve is not steep and the millions of possible situations in the game make almost impossible to master it completely. Each games plays very differently depending on the type of strategy players attempt. Games can be very short if both opponents play very offensively or longer if they adopt a more conservative defensive strategy. As in reality, War: the Board Game can be won sometimes by a blitz attack or by attrition warfare.

Although War: the Board Game is a game with open information, the randomized initial setup and the addition of special rules for each scenario (e.g. antiquity wars, WWI, WWII, etc.) make the game replayable ad infinitum and avoid the problem of players memorizing and replicating standard initial moves and tactics. Moreover, the special scenario rules not only add thematic flavor to the game, but also introduce an different spatial element and make much more difficult to predict enemy's actions.

War: the Board Game is language independent and can be played 1 vs 1, 2 vs 2 (Allies) or 4 players (free for all). The Allies (2 vs 2) version of the game adds an extra twist and new strategic and tactical options. Allies version can be also playable by 2 or 3 players (1 vs 2) if players want to control two armies and headquarters.

Each player begins with 4 cards depicting a friend or follower. The object of the game is to be the first to get rid of those cards. Phone and notification cards are flipped one at a time from a central deck. The first player to slap the deck when two phone cards are flipped consecutively loses one of his friends or followers. A notification card requires players to compete in various battles, such as writing a humorous response to a hashtag. The winner of a battle loses one of his cards.

The card deck consists of 52 multiple-choice trivia cards and 52 bully or target cards. In rummy fashion, players draw from the deck and try to meld sets of three bully or target cards in which all the cards of a set are the same or all are different. If a trivia card is drawn, eg, "School bullies often target which type of Kids," a player may earn chips for a correct answer. Melds also grant chips, as does emptying your hand. The person with the most chips when someone empties his hand is the winner.

Journey of Existence is a three to five player card game in which players journey through a world filled with dreamy art. Each choice a player makes affects them as the game proceeds.

Key Features of the Game:

Friendly Player Interaction: Journey of Existence is a bit friendlier than most games of its type as there are no discarding or stealing mechanics. It isn't free from all chaos however as its main mechanics of sharing and passing valuable cards around can be just as scary. Strategic players will usually win the game by planning out what cards to pass and which to keep once the inevitable passing happens.

High Replay-ability: Journey of Existence is a game that has so many combinations of card plays that every game experience is unique. The core game mechanics, victory point combination possibilities, and card passing theme ensure that every game is different from the last.

Journey Goals: The game revolves around the theme of Journeying through your Existence by picking up Life Traits important to your style of play. Depending on the type of Journey you decide to have and how it relates to the other players will decide the outcome of the game.

Life Actions: In Journey of Existence your time is limited. Each turn provides 4 Life Actions which can be used to do a variety of things. To do well you will need to balance your plays and keep a close eye on what other players have done in their Journeys so that you can win the Journey Goal Cards at the end.

Description from the publisher:

Grimoire Quest allows 2-6 players to take the role of powerful wizards in a massive all out war against one another to claim the lost pages of The Master Grimoire ( spell book ) to become the Master Wizard and rule the land.

The game contains 3 decks. 
Wizard Relics: These are each players personal defense cards.
Narration Deck - Contains piece of story that is happening on the battlefield as well as a special rule for that round. 
Action Deck: The core of the game play: spells, attacks, and special cards.

The game play is simple, each player chooses which wizard they want to be ( Fire, Shadow, Earth etc. ) Each player lays their wizard's 6 relic cards face up in front of themselves. Players then draw 5 action cards and follows any instructions on the cards if applicable. The round begins by the dealer reading the top card of the Narration deck which includes a small bit of story or an event that is happening on the war field. There is an action on each narration card that sets a special rule or action for that round as well. Each player has one opportunity at the start of the round to cast one spell before the round begins. After which players then each take turn attacking the defense relics of the other wizards in an attempt to disarm one another. Once a player has all of his relics "broken", they are "defenseless" and can then be eliminated once a player attacks them with a Mortal Wound card.

The game is won by one of two methods. 

    . collect all 12 pages of the Master Grimoire, either by luck, chance, defeating a player, or special cards that allow you to take form other players etc.
    . Defeat all other players and win unopposed. 


Collect and complete Master Grimoire Page pairs to gain special immunity skills and attack boosts!

A child-friendly adaptation of Jok-R-ummy. In this version, the deck of playing cards has replaced the four suits with animals (parrot, penguin, tiger, and zebra).

Each round players receive nine playing cards and one mission card. Each mission card is unique and lists a series of cards needed to complete the mission (for example: 5 tiger cards in ascending order). On their turn, each player must draw a card and then discard a card. When a player has the cards required on their personal mission card, they reveal their mission card to the other players and layers the corresponding playing cards on the table. Players acquire a new mission card every round, or they can keep an old mission card if they did not manage to complete it in the previous round.

The game ends when a player completes four mission cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Llamas with Hats: Hungry for Hands Card Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

Llamas with Hats: Hungry for Hands is a card game where 2-6 players attempt to survive Carl's terrible llama rampage. Will you stumble out of the way in time to save your succulent hands from his dark appetite? Maybe, maybe not, that's life!

All players can die, all players can live, or any combination in between! Work together or leave your friends behind, as Carl slowly munches his way toward your tasty digits.

Comes in a metal tin that is sure to impress at your next dinner party or ball. Additionally, each copy comes with 1 random "Poketmans" card, which is also sure to impress at your next dinner party or ball.

From the rulebook back cover

More than 150 years have passed since the apocalypse that nearly destroyed the Earth. Today, the planet is a torn remnant of its former glory, ravaged by nuclear fallout and mutagens. new lifeforms-Mutants and Synthetics-challenge True Humanity for dominance, while warring factions compete for survival and supremacy, and must carve out their place in this brutal landscape, or else perish as billions before them.

Scrappers is a skirmish miniatures game set in the wastelands, where players assemble Scrapper Crews and send them out to scavenge scraps of Ancient technology and battle rival factions. Explorers, cultists, and raiders clash with mutated creatures, robotic soldiers and embittered True Humans in this wargame of salvage and survival in the ruins of the future.

Welcomes to the slums where Mob bosses reign supreme! Can you take over enough territories to be the top boss?

HOW TO PLAY
Take over territories by playing cards that represent henchmen, mercenaries and bribes. The card with the highest point value wins the battle... or bribe your way to victory!

Henchmen are your foot soldiers. While they aren't always the strongest card, they are the most loyal and cannot be bribed.

Mercenaries are the ace up your sleeve, the strongest henchman. They can do any job or take care of any problem for the right price!

The Judge and the DA cannot be bribed. With this powerful card, you can immediately seize a territory.

Use money cards to bribe henchmen and pay mercenaries. Sometimes it only takes a little cash to get what you want!

The Mob Boss are powerful cards that can defeat most other cards but must remain out of play for a turn after use. Beware, if your Mob Boss is defeated in a fight, you lose the game!

HOW TO WIN
Be the first to collect a predetermined amount of territories to win!

HOW TO PLAY 
Chroma Quarrel is a card game in which players play colored cards from two decks - each representing the player's health and attacks/play direction.

Cards are played face-down, the cards are revealed at the same time and the highest card wins. If the attacking player wins, then they deal damage. Players lose health and if the player runs out of cards from their health deck, they are eliminated out of the game. However, if their attack hand is depleted, they can spend health cards to stay in the fight!

The last player standing, wins!

Three baskets filled with plastic "tipping points" hang from near the bottom of a pole that sways with the slightest touch. Players take turns removing a tipping point from one basket and placing the point on the end of one of several spokes that jut from the pole above the baskets. The winner is the last player to place a tipping point before the pole tips over.

HOW TO PLAY
Lawbreakers is a game in which players must match the right character with the right weapon. Cards are based on popular video game characters and weapons.

Players start with three characters cards and three weapons cards in their hand.

Players deal damage by playing a weapon card from their hand. If they have a matching weapon card, they deal double damage.

The player to first lose all three characters cards, loses the game!

You’ve been there for the great debates among your friends: Which player is better than which? What political party is really causing all the issues? Which superhero is better? Is a certain band legit musically or completely overrated? 
You’ve had a few laughs and some really good debates, but so often you have just agreed to disagree. Now is the time to test your powers of persuasion. Now is the time to settle this once and for all… or for at least tonight!

The players will take turns debating various topics from serious to the zany. The players will vote on who wins the argument based on the merits of the debaters.

This is a standalone expansion to The Great Debate.  This contains all new arguments and enough vote cards for 15 players.

HOW TO PLAY
Target, Aim, Assassinate is a card game in which the player's goal is to terminate high value targets for the most points.

Every player is dealt four random cards at the start of the game. The cards type are weapons, items or targets. Using their hand of cards, players attempt to match weapons to a target. Targets can only be taken out with their designated weapons.

At the start of play, a target is revealed. Each turn, players draw a card and then has the option to attack another player, draw a second card or assassinate the face-up target.

The player who assassinates the highest value of targets wins the game.

HOW TO PLAY
Geo-Shatter is a card game in which the player stacks cards in order to create point-scoring patterns.

On each cards is a geometric shape with points at various locations on the cards. As the cards stack, more points are revealed or removed from play.

Players play a single card a turn and when the cards have run out, the players get one last chance to play a card onto the stack.

HOW TO WIN
The player with the most points when all of the cards run out, wins.

Each card in a deck displays a letter that is worth from one to three points. Nine cards are arrayed face-up in the playing area, and three more are dealt to each player. Players simultaneously try to find the word with the most points and announce the word's value. When no one can exceed a value, the player who announced it provisionally collects all the cards and keeps them if he survives a challenge to the word's validity or spelling. The nine community cards are replenished, and play continues until the deck is exhausted. The winner has the most points.

From the back of the rulebook:

Deus Vult: Burn & Loot is a new game from Fireforge Games. This book allows you to relive, on a tabletop, the battles of the medieval era using model soldiers.

This game focuses on small engagements fought between the eleventh and fourteenth centuries in both eastern and western Europe. With Burn & Loot you can simulate any kind of battle, from small clashes between border patrols or within cities, to large battles and attacks on strongholds. Several battle scenarios are included in this book so you can face a new challenge every time you play.

In this book you will also find army lists that allow you to quickly and easily assemble a force for battle; more army lists will be published on our official website, and you can download them for free.

We hope that you will enjoy playing this game as much as we enjoyed developing it. But now, it is time to prepare for war!

HOW TO PLAY
In Release Animal, there are 66 animal cards, each with an elemental affiliation: Fire, Water, Earth, Timber and Gold. Each card also has an animal type: mouse, cat, dog, wolf, tiger, elephant and dragon.

Each player is dealt a hand of cards, depending on the number of players. Players use cards to fight against the other players - depending on the type of animal or the elemental quality depends on whether they can defeat the defender.

For example, cat eats mouse, dog eats cat and mouse, etc. However, the elemental affiliation can swing the fight - a water cat can defeat a fire dog. As long as play can play a card, they may continue to discard cards.

HOW TO WIN
Player who discards all of their cards first, wins the game.

Have you ever wanted to see piggies fight? In Elemental Piggies you get to do just that!

HOW TO PLAY
This game is about beating your opponent's pigs with pigs that counter the ones in place without being able to look at the opponents card, you will have to just trust them or call them out on their lie. However if you falsely accuse someone of lying you are the one that takes the cards.

Each player gets 10 cards. On each card are a number of piggies with an elemental affiliation: water, fire, wind, void and earth. One element beats another or you can overpower a card with the number of pigs in the card.

Players place their cards in the middle of the table and try to beat the value of the card that was previously placed. If they can't beat the value, they must take the pile.

HOW TO WIN
Play cards with pigs and elements and the first to get rid of all of their cards wins!

boardgame: Burn & Loot: Wrath of the Northmen
description:
Wrath of the Northmen is the first supplement for Burn & Loot, the historical miniatures wargaming rule system for Medieval Era.

In this book you will find a brief history of the Viking, a comprehensive map showing all the Viking's raid, the rules you need to field your new Armies, plus new Scenarios and rules for a Campaign in the Viking period. The Army Lists you will find in this supplement have been extensively tested, so you and your opponent can deploy forces that are as balanced as possible.

This book also includes new Traits and Reactions wich, along with your skill as a player, let you relive the battles of the Dark Ages - especially those involving Vikings. This book covers the early medieval period in western and northern Europe (a period roughly from 650AD until 1070AD).

To play this supplement you will need a copy of the Burn & Loot wargame rules.

Sheriff of Nottingham: Merry Men contains five modules that can be added to the base game, as well as the option of including a sixth player. The five modules are:


    Deputies: In six player games, you may use two Deputies instead of a single Sheriff. Here, both deputies must decide to search bags or let people pass, and they will share the punishments and rewards. If a deputy disagrees with the other and searches a bag, they will receive the punishment or reward by themselves.



    Merry Men: Adds new illegal goods in the form of 'merry men' characters. If they are successfully smuggled in, players will reveal it and activate its special ability.



    Black Market: Adds three sets of bonus cards that display illegal goods. The first player who successfully smuggles in the indicated goods receives the bonus cards and a large reward.



    Laws: Sheriffs must draw three law cards and choose two to keep. The chosen laws are in play for the entire round.



    Special Orders: Special order cards indicate combinations of one legal and one illegal good. If players can smuggle both of those items in the same bag, they get that card, which provides them with bonus gold.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Green is the first game of the GreenGreenerGreenest set. Green, Greener and Greenest are three games that use the same set of components.

Green is a game for 3 players derived from The House of the Flying Blades and brother game of RED and BLUE, by the same designer, that uses a non-transitive capturing mechanism.

Depending on the set, you’d have:

Basic: a 6×6 board, 15 black pyramids, 20 green pyramids, 15 white pyramids.

Advanced: a 6×9 – 9×9 board (using one or both pads), 30 black pyramids, 45 green pyramids, 30 white pyramids.

Gameplay overview:

The board starts full of pyramids. Players in turn capture (mandatory) other pyramids or stacks of pyramids orthogonally so that:

- Black can only capture Green
- Green can only capture White
- White can only capture Black

The game ends when all players pass in succession. The player with the highest combined height of controlled stacks wins the game.

Please refer to the rulebook for detailed rules.

This is a Chess variant in which you'll use patterns of Rook, Knight and Bishop to command Pawns into the glorious battle to the last pawn standing.
Game is played on 5x8 chequered board with regular Chess pieces so if you have set, you're ready to go.

Designed for 2017 Two Player PnP Game Design Contest.

2-4 player, Ortus Regni variant mat.

Some might consider the heavily armored knight to be the final word in medieval combat, but the Long Bow and the Trebuchet may be the pinnacle of military technology in the High Middle Ages. Neither one was easy to acquire or operate, and both require specialized companies of skilled warriors to field effectively.

2-4 player, Ortus Regni variant mat.

You are heir to a great old Earldom! Your ancient and noble family holds many assets and retainers. You can call upon all your house’s wealth to aid your endeavors but be careful when spending your family’s carefully acquired assets. They took generations to gather make sure you spend them wisely.

2-6 player, Ortus Regni variant mat.

The border of the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms and Wales is a wild frontier, neither English nor Welsh. Marcher lords rule fiefdoms that thrive in this no-man’s land between two civilizations; where the ancient Britannic people of Wales live as uneasy neighbors of the more recent Germanic Anglo-Saxon conquerors and settlers of Britain.

2-6 player, Ortus Regni variant mat.

The offices of the Shires are the start of a more formal secular organization in the early kingdoms of England. Evolving slowing, but surely, from ancient traditional Anglo-Saxon tribal leadership organization into the great royal and bureaucratic titles that we recognize today.

A unique way to play Ortus Regni; variantmat for 2-4 players.

You are Knights of the Realm, not great Earls. But you hope to rise in stature by having that title bestowed on you and your house.

Earning the respect of the great peers of the Realm, catching their eye with your strength and guile, will secure your rise.
A Knight wins Knight-errant instantly when they possess any 3 of the unique victory conditions; as mere Knights would dare not touch the person or possessions of a recognized Earl. Status is everything in this world, and all the other great Earls of the land would immediately crush such a challenge to the hierarchy!

Strategic wargame with a game board showing a map of most of Europe with a square grid overlaid. Playing pieces are steel miniatures of different types of military units. Rules are included for three types of games. The first type of game is played with infantry units only and movement controlled by rolling dice. It is intended for younger players. The second type also uses only infantry, but with deterministic chess-like moves and no dice. The third type adds all the other types of units. The goal in all games is to capture the flag in the enemy capital or reduce the enemy army to less than half of the own army's size.

Included in the game are miniature armies for Germany and France allowing for a two-player game on a war between those two nations fought on land only. Expansion armies for 15 other European countries were sold separately as well as expansions adding navies for 12 of the nations.

The rules recommend playing with one player per major country, but that two players play Germany as a team if fighting a two-front war against both France and Russia.

Wolfe & Co is a quick tile-laying set-collection micro-game for two players.

Each of the 17 game cards is double sided, with the same fairytale character on both sides, but with a different coloured background. Players take turns placing cards on a shared tableau with their colour showing. Once the tableau is full, scores are tallied depending on which combinations of characters and/or colours lie adjacent to each player's cards. A game consists of four rounds, with the tableau shape changing each round.

boardgame: Realm-Master: A Game of Dice, Domination and Magic
description:
Now live on Kickstarter: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/639064444/realm-master-a-board-game-of-dice-domination-and-m
A wizard, a dwarf, an elf, a warrior, a squire and a princess battle it out for control of "the realm", made up of 4 regions chosen randomly. Each adventurer has a special power. A round starts with a random event and then players take it in turns rolling 6 dice, hoping to collect gold (useful for buying weapons, equipment, banners or curses), swords (for attacking opponents), hearts (for healing the damage) and half-banners (basically two of these = one victory point but one on its own is useless) There is also a blank side and a wildcard side. Players have one chance to re-roll any dice they don't like. Control of the realm enables players to gather more resources but also makes them vulnerable to attack. There can only ever be one player in the realm. There are two versions of the game: an elimination version (win by collecting 10 banners or eliminating everyone else) and a points version (players who lose all their life lose half their resources and re-spawn).

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Grim Ironjaw – Star Player
description:
The Slayer Cult is a strange but well-documented aspect of life for a Dwarf, seen as the only option for those who have committed a shameful act. Shedding their worldly possessions and symbolically greasing their hair with pig fat, these contrite Dwarfs seek only a pure death in combat against the most terrifying foes imaginable. Traditionally, they had sought their fate on the battlefield, but all of that changed when Grim Ironjaw joined the Black Mountain Blades.

Following a successful first season, Ironjaw spent several years moving from team to team, always striving to move up in the league rankings. After all, as he saw it, the most glorious death to be found on the pitch must surely be at the Blood Bowl itself, facing off against one of the NAF’s most accomplished teams. In the years since he has yet to compete in the fabled tournament, but with each passing season, his chances are greater and his legend grows ever more impressive. Who knows – maybe this is his year!

Field Grim Ironjaw as a Star Player in your Dwarf Blood Bowl team with this resin kit. He’s a particularly stocky example of Dwarf strength, with bulging muscles beneath a frankly glorious beard featuring plaited-in details (skulls! He has skulls in his beard!) His mohawk is tall, and his fist clutches a terrifying-looking blade – truly, he’s an all-crushing Slayer and a half.

This resin miniature comes as 5 components, and is supplied with a 32mm Blood Bowl base.
Turbo Drift: Ramped Up adds another obstacle card to make tracks a little more difficult, two oil slick cards, a sweet 5th player truck and 2 ramps so that you can leap into the air.

What's a dinner party without MORE GUESTS?! These have come from all over, introducing children into the game, along with a not-so-hard-to-notice Spy, Counselor, Robot and Vampire. Dinner is served!

Find Your Seats: Bonus Appétit adds six new characters, each very different than any we've seen.

The Sweet Pea that moves neighbors closer, The Counselor that flips them back over, The Twins that have different personalities on the left and right, The Spy that looks at cards, The Vampire that sucks blood and points, and The Robot that wants specific patterns.

Enter the Realm of the Incarnations. Face the very essence of Anguish, Pride, Hope and Despair. Take up sword against all four Horsemen of the Apocalypse — including Death himself. If Fortune is by your side, you may just come out alive and wealthy beyond imagining.

Welcome back to Cutthroat Caverns, the semi-cooperative game of kill-stealing and backstabbing in a deadly dungeon. Death Incarnate is an expansion dedicated to the most feared encounters in the game, the Incarnations. Here their numbers are more than doubled, new events are themed for their spheres of influence, and legendary relics are imbued with their incredible powers — one for each Incarnation since you first braved the caverns.

Embark on a quest and see whether you are cutthroat enough to become its very Incarnation.

Description from the publisher:

Frozen: The Ball at Arendelle is a board game for children based on the popular Disney cartoon Frozen. The game is an interesting and colorful memory trainer — the players have to figure out who didn't come to the ball. Everything happens in a very simple way.

At the beginning players are looking at the 6 chips for five second and trying to remember them. Then chips turn around and shuffled. Only 5 chips are opened after that and players have to say what character on the 6th chip. Guessed player take the snowflake and the winner is the first one who get 10 snowflakes.

It’s finally here! The first expansion: the Frantic Troublemaker Add-On!
36 brand new and extremely mischievous cards give the game an entirely new spin.

This includes 14 new Event Cards, to make Frantic even more unpredictable. But also 22 Special Cards, to make the game not just bigger but more eventful: We have colour switches, Inequality, Event Card protection and the ultimate catastrophic cataclysm. And of course the highlight: Mimicry – a shapeshifter which can take the form of ANY Special Card!

boardgame: Breaking Teeth: The Battle of Teruel, December, 1937
description:
“Viva la Muerte!”—Rallying cry of the Nationalist forces during much of the Spanish Civil War.

The Republican cause was in serious jeopardy after a year-and-a-half of bloody fighting. Many felt it would take a miracle to rejuvenate an army that was barely hanging on in Madrid, and reeling from repeated Nationalists blows elsewhere in Spain. As winter settled in, the need for some sort of victory in the field was increasingly necessary as France and the Soviet Union began wavering in their support, and Great Britain stepped up its “blockade” that in reality only helped the Nationalists.

One of the most direct threats to Republican survival was the salient to the south of Madrid. Any further Nationalist gains in that sector could cut-off the capital’s link to the rest of Republican-controlled Spain. With little to lose and much to win, General Enrique Lister’s 11th Division was placed in the vanguard of the Republican counter-attack. Their goal was to take the key defensive position of La Muela de Teruel—Teruel’s Tooth.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterparts? Learn and enjoy!

Each copy of Breaking Teeth is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch map with game record-keeping tracks and terrain identification.
•    63 un-mounted, double sided units.
•    One set of 12 page rules.

Units represent regiments or brigades (infantry) and battalions (armor). Each turn represents 3 days of time.

description:
With Wendepunkte you can play through a potential life in east Germany (at the time of the German Democratic Republic) and after the German reunification. You experience the pressure of the system, discrepancies in your potential biography and you need to make decisions living with its consequences.
While playing you learn about the political turning point in 1989 which changed the life for millions of people completely.

Wendepunkte is a simple, fast-paced and compelling card game. It's fun and different every time being played and it illustrates a part of German history.

The game takes place over three rounds with three turns each. During a turn, all players choose one card from their hand to play, then handing the remainder to an adjacent player. 
After each player has revealed their cards simultaneously, the next turn starts with each player then selecting another card from the deck they were passed.
All cards have different symbols on them which either allow you to play certain other cards or they forbid playing them because you're missing the right symbols on the cards you've already played.

After round two and three, all players sum up the points on their played cards and the player with the most points wins the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Forged Realms is a simultaneous tactical dueling card game that plays in about 10 minutes

Players build an army from a large pool of spells and creatures including the Border Knight, Necromancer, Siege Troll, Crypt Wolf, Goblin King and more. You then have only 5 turns to try and drive your opponent from the battle field using tactics, scouting, speed, flanking, and combat

Each turn you will place one Creature or Spell faced-down in each open Rank on the battlefield at the same time as your opponent. When both players are finished deploying, you then simultaneously reveal cards in the same Rank and resolve the spells and abilities of creatures. The newest Rank also is called a Scouting Rank where the fastest creature outmaneuvers the slower creature and immediately deals its damage to the opponent. Finally, any creatures that were revealed in earlier turns do battle with each other to try and defeat the opposing creature in that rank. Once opened, the creature here can go right through and do it's damage directly to the player

Forged Realms is a game of planning, surprise, scouting, using abilities/spells and controlling ranks to try and defeat your opponent by lowering their armies moral to 0 in only 5 turns

With 4 factions to choose from in the Core Set, you have up to 10 combinations to make your army with. Each pre-built deck can be changed and built as see fit thus giving players hundreds of deck combinations

Brushed-metal Munchkin pin, in the shape of Spyke's head.

Special rule: Each Munchkin Pin you are wearing gives you a +1 bonus in combat. After each fight you are in, win or lose, roll a d6. If you roll equal to or less than the number of Munchkin Pins you are currently wearing, remove one pin. You may not wear more than one pin of each color during any game.

You may not be able to see your Munchkin Imaginary Friend, but that doesn't mean he can't help you! Once.

Official Rule: You may tear the blister away from the backing to release your Munchkin Imaginary Friend whenever you are in combat. He will give you a +10 combat bonus for that fight, after which you can't find him again to put him back in his case. He's free! Free! FREE!!!!

The game is played on a slanted grid using three pieces to a player, representing viking longships; the goal is to cross the playing field to your opponent's starting spaces the fastest. Each turn a die is rolled, with an odd result usually resulting in moving one ship one space, and an even result meaning a lost turn. Opposing ships block but do not capture each other. Some spaces require a specific die result to enter, and other spaces are effectively forbidden, sending a piece back to the start if for some unexplained reason you should enter them.

The game is nominally for 2-4 players, representing the vikings of Sweden, Finland, Norway and Denmark, but it would not be fair to play with 3, since the board is effectively divided in half and with 4 players the two pairs interact very weakly except for the racing aspect.

There is no year of publication, but the Alga logo (or lack of one) suggests the 1940s or 1950s.

Strange things are going on in Europe. People are disappearing and followers are changing faction. The plague appears to be stronger in some countries, but nobody really knows why. At least people are warned and new alliances are made. Let’s bring an end to this plague!

This is a mini-expansion. Rattus (base game) is required to play. For the Tamerlane card, Rattus Africanus (expansion) is required to play.

Italia 1918 - A Farewell to Arms is an operational wargame which simulates the fierce fighting which pitted the troops of the Entente (Italy, France, Great-Britain and USA) against those of the Central Powers (Austria-Hungary and Germany) during the period between the Battle of Caporetto (Oct 1917) and the Villa Giusti Armistice (Nov 1918).

Two A1 maps, printed front and back, are included. The “global” one covers the terrain between the Adige River and Trieste, to allow the players to simulate the whole campaign from Caporetto onwards. The “zoom” map, with enlarged hexes, covers the terrain from Adige to the Adriatic sea in order to concentrate on the main combat zone after the Italian retreat: the Piave River, the Monte Grappa Massif, the Asiago Plateau and the Trento area & for the second one the zone of the Austro-German offensive on the Isonzo that has been immortalised under the name of the battle of Caporetto. The 2 ½ countersheets include a complete OB for all the belligerents, from tenacious Italian “Alpini” to the redoutable German “Alpenkorps”, along with Austrian “Kaiserjäger”, Hungarian “Honvéd” divisions or French “Chasseurs des Alpes”.

Basic rules allow the players to tackle 6 variously sized scenarios, from 1 to 6 hours of play, using either a part of the board, or the map in its entirety. 5 of the scenarios are designed to be played on the ‘zoom’ map, in order to take maximum advantage of the improved play experience provided by the larger hexes. Advanced rules allow the players to simulate the whole campaign; they introduce the reconstruction of destroyed units, the management of strategic reserves and the use of random events cards (50 for each side) with various effects and evocative titles such as “Rommel”, “Hemingway” or “I ragazzi del 99” (“The boys of 99”), for example. Optional rules will allow players to explore several “what-if” possibilities.
 The campaign game is played on the “global” map, beginning with the Caporetto offensive and lasts 23 to 36 turns. 
 • Boxed game
• 2 A1 double-sided maps
• 1 A1 single-sided map
• 700 counters
• Rules booklet in english
• 24 pages campaign rules booklet in english
• 1 set of 100 event cards
• 2 A3 double-sided player aids [ Allied screen, Central powers screen ]
• 3 A4 single-sided player aids [ Strategic reserves for both sides & other tracks ]
• 4 A4 double-sided player aids [ Scenario setups ]
• 2 A4 single-sided player aids [ Reinforcements & Terrains ]
• 4 dice

(from Nuts Publishing website)

description:
This a set collection / take that card game for players of all ages where each player tries to get enough pieces to build their caterpillar all the while, using other cards to prevent their opponents from finishing their caterpillars.

The goal is to build your caterpillar by collecting and playing A Head piece, a Tail piece and four body pieces before the other players.

Components
This game deck is made up of 7 Head cards, 28 Body cards, 7 Tail cards, 20 Red attack cards, and 6 Green defense cards.

To complete your caterpillar you need to have in play in front of you, one Head, four Body segments, and one Tail segment. If you have those six cards in play in front of you, you win.

Other players will try to force you to discard pieces, or even take pieces from you for their own caterpillar.

Kickin’ Your Ass is a set collection / take that card game where you try to build your Ass, while kicking other players’ Asses.
You try to collect each piece of Ass you can get your hands on. Once you have enough Ass to complete your Ass, you win. But along the way, other players are trying to kick your Ass. This of course results in you losing your Ass, or at least some of it.

Each player starts with a hand of four cards.  All cards are drawn from a common deck.  You build your Ass out of 5 cards, Tail, Back Legs, Middle, Front Legs, Head.
All the while, you play cards to Kick someone’s Ass which makes them lose pieces of their Ass. You can try to stop this by playing Get Off My Ass cards.

If you can find your ass with both hands, you can play Kickin' Your Ass!

(from publisher)

Drink shots, or get shot? That's a good question!

Welcome to the life-betting party! A bunch of desperadoes have gathered together — not to bet money, but to engage in a more primitive way to reach to the top: a drinking game. In this party, you can either be wasted or get shot. Only the fastest person can leave here alive.

Lethal Party is a speed game for 3-10 players in which you try to be quick and sharp to grab the correct card to fire your bullets at your opponents. Whoever can survive to the end is the sole winner.

Show your power as only the mighty can strike! Bring your favorite space monsters along, and fight for the universe!

Mochiju is a quick card game for 3-6 players. Evolving your monsters down the right path or striking your opponents at the right time is the key to victory! Come enjoy the fun of beating your friends!

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Grombrindal and the Black Gobbo – Star Player Duo
description:
Two new star players, Grombrindal, the white dwarf and his arch-nemesis, the Black Gobbo, are taking the pitch in Blood Bowl. Stats and rules for these two new star players are in the May 2017 issue of White Dwarf magazine as well as in a downloadable PDF.

Grombrindal’s reputation is almost mythological. Tales abound of his shrewd tactical advice on the field of battle; on the Blood Bowl pitch this translates to a mastery of team strategies and the ability to draw the very best out of the players around him.

The Black Gobbo is legendarily devious, as evidenced by his countless elaborate schemes to undermine the White Dwarf. Before he even thinks about setting foot on the pitch, it’s a given that he will have already established a web of bribery, hostage taking and other sneakiness that ensures the cooperation of the match officials.

Legendary heroes, this Star Player duo can be used in games of Blood Bowl as long as one player has a Dwarf, Halfling, Human or Norse team and the other has a Chaos Renegade, Goblin, Orc or Underworld Denizen team. They’re not hired in the same way as other Star Players – things like contract negotiations, legal issues or basic civility are pretty alien to them, honestly – and both must appear in your game.

Grombrindal is looking in fine form: his miniature features a splendid shock of white hair and a magnificent beard covering a purposeful scowl, with plenty of Dwarven iconography appearing on his kit. The Black Gobbo is appropriately robed and behatted, with sneakily studded armour beneath the robes – and a bomb. He’s clutching a bomb. That probably isn’t going to end well.

These resin miniatures come as 8 components, and are supplied with 2 32mm Blood Bowl bases.

boardgame: Sailor Moon Crystal: Truth or Bluff
description:
Are you telling the truth or bluffing? Keep your opponents guessing as you pass tiles around the table and try not to get caught being too obvious. Watch out for the Dark Kingdom villains, though, because they can mess with your plans. When one player triggers the end of the game they are defeated and the player with the most number of Success Tokens is the winner!

A hilarious party game of deception and misdirection featuring super-deformed caricatures of your favourite Sailor Moon Crystal guardians!

Game Play Details
Players each attempt to avoid running out of Character Tiles and/or filling the conditions on the selected Ending Tile that triggers the end of the game while simultaneously trying to gain the greatest number of Success Token points. The player that triggers the game’s end has been defeated while the player with the greatest number of Success Token points is the winner.

The king of the underworld Mr. Big and his gang are intimidating civilians, forcing them to flee the city. The time has come to rein in the carousing bandits.

The game is based on the classic, Mafia. Good citizens with the help of police are trying to expose the criminals and the bandits. Good citizens should find the bandits. Bandits' goal is survive till the end while citizens fighting against each other.

The mechanics of Mafia have been simplified for children, keeping the deduction part but removing the "killing" of other players.  Child friendly cards identify which team the player is on and what special powers/hidden roles they have.  As with traditional Mafia, the goal of the game is to find all the Bandits, before the good players are all exiled from the game.

A renewed version of the White Box Percival Promotional Expansion, Percival is now a Pillar Survivor.

Pillar cards are used during the design of your new campaign. They add rules that increase the depth of your campaign experience. Campaigns of Death Vol 1 (CoD) includes complete rules for campaign design that includes pillar cards.
Using Percival pillar card in your next campaign:
When you design your next campaign, if you want to include this pillar, use the Install Guide to replace a basic hunt event with the Dead Warrior Pillar hunt event card.

Pillar - Percival is packaged in a uniquely designed graphical box and includes:
1x Percival hard plastic miniature
1x Kingdom Death 30mm base
1x Pillar hunt event card
1x Secret fighting art card
1x Pillar card
1x Pillar card install guide
1x Large matte art print

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – White Speaker Promo
description:
Two gear cards and a sticker to alter the white speaker event in the rulebook. These were included with the White Speaker miniature, which is not part of the game.

Gear:

Speaker Cult Knife (Weapon, 3/6+/4, two affinities red upside, downside; deadly, sharp; while insane and not wearing armour or accessories, gain +2 evasion, +2 strength)

Bloodskin (Item, two affinities green upside, downside; When you depart you fill the bloodskin and gain two bleeding tokens. At the start of each your acts, remove 1 bleeding token)

A renewed version of the White Box Fade Promotional Expansion, Fade is now a Pillar Survivor.

Pillar cards are used during the design of your new campaign. They add rules that increase the depth of your campaign experience. Campaigns of Death Vol 1 (CoD) includes complete rules for campaign design that includes pillar cards.

When you design your next campaign, if you want to include this pillar, add the listed campaign milestone (When the settlement innovates Storytelling | Replace a basic hunt event with Baby and the Sword) with other Milestone Story Events on your settlement record sheet.

Presented in a litho-wrapped printed box, Fade includes:

When the Pillar Hunt Event Card is encountered, the survivors discover Fade's ultimate fate and can choose to brave the dangerous Harvester in order to gain the Sword of Silence, a Newborn child or possibly both.

This expansion has been said to include some interaction(s) with the upcoming Red Witches expansion.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Before The Wall Promo
description:
This White Box Promotional Expansion includes a miniature that represents Aya the Survivor wearing Vagabond Armor while wielding a Black Sword (Gormery) and a Beacon Shield (Blacksmith), this can be used to represent a female survivor in game.  Also included are two gear cards and an armor set card for complete Vagabond Armor (which is an alternate Leather Armor set).

Crafting Recipes for the gear cards are printed on the back. There is currently no way to craft the White Dragon Gauntlets, but it is speculated that this will change when the Ivory Dragon expansion is released.

In order to complete the Vagabond Armor set you will also need a copy of the 'Beyond the Wall' promotional cards.

Pieces required to complete the set
Tabard
Leather Chest or Leather Breastplate

You cannot wear head armor, but head accessories are allowed.

White Dragon Gauntlets
Hand Armor (12 Armor points)
Affinities: Red; Left Half

It is not known what a Sideswipe in the game is at this stage.
