30-60min for veteran players. Playtime increases with the number of players, but feel free to shorten the game by requiring fewer demons to win.

boardgame: One Night Ultimate Werewolf: Bonus Pack 3
description: One Night Ultimate Werewolf: Bonus Pack 3 consists of three new roles, two new player number tokens, a new artifact, and three alternative artwork cards. The three new roles consist of the Body Snatcher, who steals an opponents card and turns it into an alien version of that role; the Empath, who sees certain players indicate certain things with their eyes closed, and can use that information later on; and Nostradamus, who predicts which team will win by first looking at cards and then indicating the last card they viewed in the app, which indicates the team that Nostradamus is on.

User Summary
Are You 勇者？ (Are You Yuusha?) is a fast-paced were-wolf card game by the Japanese doujin publisher ザキさん工房 released at the Game Market 2015 Autumn convention in Japan.

In Japanese, 勇者 means 'Hero' so the game name can be translated as 'Are you a Hero?'.

Within 2 rounds only, the monster players must find and eliminate the Hero player to win, otherwise it's the Hero player win.

There is only 1 Hero player card, and there are 5 monster cards available. Each of them has different abilities.

The game plays roughly as the following:
1) Each player draws a character card. The players can only check on this character card and must keep it face down so no other player can see who they are.
2) Give two action cards to each player.
3) Before the start of the game, in order all players but one will close their eyes so one can play their power if possible.
4) Round 1 starts. All players discuss and try to figure out the other player's identity. It is of course okay to lie.
5) When time is up or everyone decided, all players put forward one card face down. 
6) The players reveal at once who they are going to play their action card against. You can play a card on yourself.
7) Depending on your character, you may reveal you identity and use your ability.
8) All the players reveal their action card and proceed to resolution of their effects. Any player whose HP reaches 0 is KO and is out for the game. They cannot display their action cards not their character card and cannot take part in the discussion until the game ends.
9) Without replenishing action cards, move proceed to Round 2 by repeating steps  4 to 8.
10) The game ends. If the Hero player is still alive he wins. If he's dead the monster players win.

Publisher Summary
【Are you 勇者？とは】
・３～４人で手軽に楽しめる人狼型ゲーム
・魔王復活の２日後までに勇者役のプレイヤーを推理して攻撃だ！
・勇者のＨＰを０にすることができればモンスターサイドの勝利！
・推理するだけでなく、キャラクターとアクションカードの能力を駆使した両陣営の攻防がアツい！

Description from the publisher:

You're a duck, in a truck, delivering packages.

Unfortunately ducks aren't very good at doing deliveries.
Crates fall of vehicles and other players can steal your cargo!
Delivery Duck is a strategic family game for 2 - 6 players.

Gameplay centres around picking up and delivering cargo. Depos are placed around a dodecagon shaped board. Each turn a player picks up cards that determine the starting location and destination of cargo. Then they place a terrain tile to help them traverse the board or stop others, before moving their vehicles.

The goal of the game is to earn 10 victory points. Cargo comes in multiple types, each with its own monetary and VP value. Strategy comes in deciding which vehicle to use considering terrain, resources on the board and other players actions. Action cards further modify the gameplay.

boardgame: Apochalyptica: A Game About Humanity, Survival, and the Atomic Bomb
description:
Description from the publisher:

Apochalyptica is an advanced board game for 4-8 players based around a group of people that find themselves facing nuclear war in the late 1940s. WWII has just ended, but any celebration to be had is cut short by news of bombs falling worldwide, with more headed their way. This ragtag group must gather as many supplies as they can before locking themselves away in a bunker for twelve months. During this time, they will have to deal with a number of trials from petty arguments among themselves, to food, water, and oxygen running out; all with the threat of fallout exposure looming around every corner.

The game is board-and-card based, but plays like your average tabletop rpg. Players have the opportunity to roleplay as their characters, dealing with interpersonal relationships within the bunker as you wait for the timer to tick down and free you. Each character has their own unique set of skills and traits to bring to the table, and players must decide how to best use them to ensure the survival of the group.

The first section is the gathering section. Based on the chosen level of gameplay difficulty (easy or normal. Hard doesn't have a gather section and instead starts directly in the bunker with a set amount of supplies) players will travel around the board to "locations" to search for supplies. To search, they roll 1d10 and compare it to a table of outcomes.
At the end of three rounds, they enter a "voting" phase to choose which characters enter the bunker with their supplies, and which must be left outside.

Section two, the "main game", is twelve rounds in the Bunker where the surviving characters must monitor the levels of food, water, and oxygen. All while trying to keep their sanity levels intact so they don't end up killing each other, or themselves. They have ways of slowing the drop of each of these supply levels, including farming, building a water purification system, monitoring a radio for information about the outside, and interacting with one another in positive ways. Each round, however, players must roll to determine events that occur on their turns that could either help or hinder their progress. It could be something as little as having a bad day and losing a few extra sanity points, to something as disastrous as a radiation leak that permanently seals off a section of the bunker from use.

User summary
ゴリ×夢中 (Gori x Muchuu) is an abstract 2-player strategy game by 楠本舗 (Kusunoki Honpo) that was released at the Game Market 2017 Spring in Odaiba, Tokyo Japan.

As the name suggests, Gori x Muchuu is themed with Gorillas and Bananas. According to the publisher it is inspired by the game Geister.

The game doesn't come in a box. The components are a grid sheet, representing the forest, and gorillas chips, which comes in 2 types: square and triangle, 4 of each.

The textbook only feature Japanese but no knowledge of Japanese is required to play the game.

You win if you either:
- Reach the banana square of your color inside your opponent's zone.
- Capture 5 of your opponent's gorilla chips
You loose if you either:
- Capture 4 or more gorilla chip of your opponent of the same type (triangle or square).
- Get your player chip captured

Publisher summary

A limited edition train card, released as part of a postcard of which 100 were printed and signed by designer Tony Boydell, with all proceeds going to Asthma UK.

The card depicts Puffing Billy, the world's oldest steam engine, currently on display in the Science Museum in London. When the owner user of the train uses it to get their third worker from the pub, they must nominate another player who can also retrieve their third worker from the pub for free.

A card game for 2-4 players based on the Dark Crystal movie.

Players share a common deck. Of these, 11 can be utilised to create a Crystal Combo (required to win the game), the others have special abilities which allow players to undertake a variety of actions (i.e. draw cards, steal cards, look at cards, switch hands …)

To start each player is dealt 3 cards. 12 cards are then spread facedown evenly over the play area – these are called Table cards and are available to be drawn (picked) by the players.

Players take turns, with each turn having 4 phases:

1.  Fate Phase 
-   Take the top 2 cards of the deck and place them face down on the play area. If there are ever 12 or less Table cards available, draw and place extra cards face down until there are at least 12 Table cards.

2.  Prophecy Phase
-   You may play 1 card to use its special ability. When you play a card either discard it or return it face down to the table.

3.  Quest Phase
-   Draw 3 Table cards and put 2 of them in your hand. Return 1 of these cards to the Table.

4.  Crystal Phase
-   Reveal the Crystal Combo if you have it to win and end the game. The combo must contain 4 cards: one of each of the following 4 types: 
I. The Gelfling or Jan or Kira card
II. The Crystal Shard or Dark Crystal card
III. The Castle of the Crystal or Sacred Chamber card
IV. The Grand Conjunction or Alignment of the Suns or Heal the Crystal card
The Prophecy card can be used as a wild card for any of the 4 types of cards needed in a combo. 
-   If not, max hand size is 5 cards. Return excess cards to the Table.

The first player to declare a Crystal Combo at the end of his turn wins the game.

This is a playing card game developed for italian band BIZARRO WELT. It is based on their Song "Bizarro-Planet der Affen". A limited amount of decks printed in professional-grade quality was sold on Christmas 2016.

The objective of the game is to draw cards from a pile and avoid cards labeled with an evil, mutant ape by neutralizing them with a "battle" card. If an ape card cannot be neutralized, the player looses. The number of battle cards in the game decreases over time. The last player still standing wins.

Additional action cards can give an individual player and advantage over others, for example:

- The "future zombie" card allows a player to look at and reshuffle the top three cards of the drawing pile.

- The "fight" card allows a player to challenge an other player to a side competition (e.g. arm wrestling, throwing darts, drinking etc.). The winner may then take one of the loosers' cards.

- The "downfall" card moves all "ape" cards in the game to the top of the drawing pile.

The game can be played by two to five players, but is most fun with four or five players.

Mage Wars: Academy – Forcemaster Expansion includes a new mage for use in both Mage Wars: Academy and Mage Wars: Arena. In this expansion set, play as a telekinetic Forcemaster competing to prove that your mind is the most powerful weapon!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Description from the publisher:

Covfefe is about the nightmare scenario of 21st Century Supervillains that take over countries from the people through a mix of technology, misinformation, and outright deception. Players are sociopathic dictators trying to dismantle the world to profit themselves and their friends, all the while trying to keep one step ahead of government investigations. The winner is the one with the most Hubris when the world is hobbled by Nuclear War, Economic Depression, or Tribalism.

All players are recently-elected populist politicians who only won their elections through corruption related to an evil empire that is hellbent on world domination. Through manipulation of the public, pointless but politically popular actions, and bald-faced lies, the players try to destroy their governments, making money from evil corporations, and stay one step ahead of government regulations and/or unpopular opinion.

The game has worker placement (we call it ‘scapegoating,’ as your worker may not come back) and worker-developing (like deck-building for workers), multi-purpose cards, and a lot of deception. Welcome to 21st Century politics!

In F*That!, the active player each round is presented with an uncomfortable or unusual situation (e.g., You've just gone to the bathroom only to discover afterward that no toilet paper is available. What's an acceptable substitute?) along with five possible solutions for this situation (e.g., fashion magazine, pile of leaves, etc.). The active player tells everyone how many of the solutions they find acceptable, then everyone else simultaneously guesses which solutions that player might pick.

Description from the publisher:

The players in the game run the last bastions of human control, corporations. Governments long ago failed after being dismantled by Emperor Trump. In A.I., corporations are trying to continue to line their pockets with the use of technology, except the technology is starting to work against that. Players are trying to profit handily while staying one step ahead of the A.I., which is starting to think of humans as a virus in the physical world.

A.I.’s key mechanism is the idea of an engine-building game with a potentially intelligent engine. In most engine-building games, players assemble an engine through the acquisition and management of resources to construct from a selection of parts. At some point in the game, the engine that will be run for the sole purpose of making victory points. The interesting decisions in these games is when to make that shift.
 In A.I., the engines have a chance to become sentient and could actually become part of a dummy player in the game that both hinders the ability of the player to progress and competes against all players to possibly win the game.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Goblin Secret Weapons
description:
Description from the publisher:

Those dastardly goblins have concocted a range of deadly – and almost certainly illegal – secret weapons to use for Blood Bowl! We can see the classic ball and chain, an actual bomb, and a chainsaw will be making their way onto the pitch via Goblin sneakiness.

In Invisible Think, players take turns racing against time to get their teammates to guess the word on the drawing card. However, in this game of "invisible ink" there is a twist - players or the entire team will also wear special red lens glasses that make the red-ink drawing invisible to the eye!

A quiz game in which players know the category and then have to bet with their cards (2 sets of 1-4 per player). The players betting the highest cards are playing the current round and trying to answer first to win the point. The questions are usually hints that are read by a non-participating player slowly, so people competing for the question can answer as soon as they think to know the answer.
The game is played in 3 rounds of 8 questions each. In round 2 and 3 the players not in the lead may steal a question once each if they are faster than the active player(s).

Publisher's blurb:

"DAY OF INFAMY is the second game of The Second World War Series (TSWW) from Diffraction Entertainment for the Pacific Theater of War.

"Day of Infamy (DOI) is the TSWW-series game covering the day that lived in infamy forever; Pearl Harbour and the first 3 years of the North Pacific and Central Pacific war.  With over 2,200 counters, 11 maps (several at 75 mile SSZ scale) and the usual TSWW horde of At Starts (Pearl Harbour, Midway, Operation M (north), the Allied counter offensive in the North Pacific, Guam, etc., this is the ideal introduction to the Blue Water Naval System that is a core component to the TSWW game system.
Featuring the usual TSWW refinements including multiple modules and scenarios, DOI is the next release in the TSWW Game series, with massive fleet actions and a very special raid from Shangri-La.

"The awesome all-new release includes the following:

"- 2,280 high-quality counters on 8 sheets.
- 11 map segments – 7 at 15 miles to the hex, 4 at 75 miles to the hex
- Fully bound rules (expanded to handle the 75 mile hexes on the strategic displays)
- OBs (Axis, Allied, At Start, Production)
- Charts (also expanded to handle the 75 mile hexes on the strategic displays)
- North and Central Pacific theatres from Dec I 1941 turn through Oct II 1943
- Numerous scenarios from Pearl Harbor, Guam and the full Campaign Game"
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: The Second World War (Diffraction Entertainment)

description:
TopATop Family is a family party game for 3-6 players, aged 4 and above. The gameplay time depends on the number of players and takes between 15 and 30 minutes. It is a simple and very dynamic game in which you will have a chance to use your dexterity, observation and quickness.

During the game, you take turns placing cards in the middle of the table and reacting appropriately to the pictures on the cards played. A player who does that better — either quicker, more accurately, or without making a mistake — than others wins that challenge and collects that card.

Marble game in which Bangaroo spin-wheel is spun and marbles are sent whirling into the various pockets of the Bangaroo.  Five different ways to play:
Bang-a-Roo Numbers Game - Highest score wins
Bang-a-Roo Word Game - Make single word from letters and score based on length of word.
Bang-a-Roo Word Tournament - Make as many words as possible with letters.
Bang-a-Roo Long Shot - Make longest word before spinner stops
Two-Way Bang-a-Roo - Make words and highest score after 5 spins wins.

From box "Four new spin, spell 'n' score games in one, each more fascinating that the next.  All for fun and fun for all.

Contents:
Bangaroo
Bangaroo Spin-wheel
5 Bangaroo Balls
Paste on letters and numbers
Bangaroo Score-pad and pencil
Bangaroo tips for hours of fun

User summary
ハリネズミくんのラブレター (Harinezumi no Rabu Reta-) in English is 'Hedgehog's Love Letter' ('Rabu Reta-' is the imported English 'love letter'). Note: the game is not published in English, so only the JP title is being used.

Hedgehogs Makoto and Yurayura have both fallen in love with Princess Harine. Now they are rivals in love as they vie for the Princess’s affection. Each hedgehog plans his itinerary for the day in order to collect as many hearts as possible — either by sending a love letter to the Princess or, oppositely, throwing poop at his rival hedgehog. The hedgehog with the most hearts at the end wins the Princess’s heart.

This 2 player game consists of plotting action cards. When all actions cards have been plotted, they will be carried out in the specific order of how they were plotted. However, your rival’s cards are also included amongst the cards you can plot. Try to collect as many heart tokens as you can with your actions, but if you collect three poop tokens, you will lose instantly.

Publisher's summary''
ハリネズミのまことくんとゆらゆらくんは、王女のハリネちゃんを好きになっちゃった！2人は恋のライバルだ！1日の予定を（勝手に）決めて、ラブレターを送ったり相手にうんこを投げつけたりして沢山ハートを集めよう！！
最終的にハートの多いハリネズミ君が、ハリネちゃんとお付き合いできるぞ！
このゲームは、アクションカードに対してカードをプロットし、すべてプロットしたらアクションを順に行っていきます。ですがプロットできるカードには相手のカードもあります。アクションによってハートトークンをたくさん手に入れましょう。うんちトークンを３つ集めると即時負けとなります。

User summary
ヘルメスの流儀 (Herumesu no ryuugi) The Style of Hermes is a Japanese card game. Gameplay is language free, so whilst the game is published in Japanese, English rules are available online.

In the region where the God Hermes reigns, all fruits produced must be offered to him. Hermes is a god of trade, he determines the true owner of the fruits
by trading. The fruits are first offered to Hermes (placed on the table), then auctioned off by the true owner.

Players have their own deck of cards which represent the fruits produced in Hermes's region. Each turn, you may draw a card from your deck and either offer it to Hermes, placing it onto the table, or keep it as your bidding resource. When you feel the time is ripe, you may instead initiate an auction for all fruits on the table.

In the end, the victory goes to the player who has the greatest total value of fruits.

Publisher's summary
ヘルメスの神域で採取された産物はヘルメスに捧げなければならない！捧げられたのちならば産物は真の所有者のものになるであろう。捧げられた産物はヘルメスの流儀で所有者が決まる。ヘルメスの流儀とは「商い」である！

プレイヤーはヘルメスの神域で採取された果物を持ちゲームをスタートします。果物はいったんはヘルメスに捧げられますが、捧げられたのちに競りによって真の所有者が決定されます。獲得した所有物の価値がもっとも大きければこのゲームに勝利することができるでしょう。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
A dark curse has fallen over the Kingdom of Sugarplum, and the Frosted Throne sits empty.  As a Dream Mage with incredible powers and powerful ambitions you must set out on a journey across the four realms to take the crown for yourself.

But it will not be easy.

Enemies and Encounters will test you along the Candy Road, dangerous Nightmares haunt your quest, other Dream Mages will do their best to sabotage your endeavor, and even if you do take the crown for yourself, you will have to fight to keep it.

Sugarplum Chronicles is a candy-themed adventure game in which the first goal is to make it to the Crown Tile before any other players.  The second, and final, goal is to be the last surviving Player.  During their turn, players will first roll to move, and decide which direction they want to move in and whether or not they want to stop at Candy Shops or Landmarks along the way.  Depending on the color of the tile that Players land on, they will either face an enemy or encounter (red), draw a nightmare or dream card (black), receive a Lemon Drop treasure (yellow), explore a Landmark (purple), or have a rest (green).

The first Player to reach the crown tile will then hunt down the other players and face off in D-20 based combat featuring edible weapons and items.

Sugarplum Chronicles includes over 45 unique enemies, 'edible' weapons and items, an exciting PVP endgame, and player interaction through spells, mercenaries, magic teddy bears, and more.

IntroductionYugo is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board.

Each player must have access to a sufficient number of pieces of their own color. There are two types of pieces: pawns and kings. There's also one special token called button.

				
				
					DefinitionsA player's group is a set of one or more orthogonally contiguous pieces of that player's color. Pawns and kings can be part of the same group. A portion of a larger group is not a group.

A liberty of a group is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to that group. A group without liberties is said to be surrounded.

A territory is a set of orthogonally contiguous empty points. A portion of a larger territory is not a territory. You own a territory if at least one point in it is adjacent to one of your pieces and no points in it are adjacent to any enemy pieces.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On their turn, a player must perform exactly one of the following actions:

a) Take the button, provided that none of the players has taken it before.

b) Pass, provided that the button is already taken.

c) Place a pawn of their color on an empty point such that the number of surrounded groups remains unchanged.

d) Place a king of their color on an empty point such that the number of surrounded enemy groups is increased, and remove all pawns from all such groups. In the resulting position, if there are any surrounded friendly groups, remove all pawns from them as well.

Play stops when both players pass consecutively. At this point, the players may agree which groups, if any, can be removed from the board without changing the scores that would result from continuing the game. If there is such an agreement, the agreed groups are removed, the game ends and the resulting position is used for scoring. If the players don't agree which groups should be removed, play is resumed. In this case, when both players pass consecutively again, the game ends and the current position, without further removals, is used for scoring.

A player's score is the number of empty points in their territories, plus the number of pieces of their color in groups with one or more liberties, plus the number of enemy kings in groups without liberties, plus half a point if they have taken the button, plus the value of komi (see below) in the case of White.

The player with the higher score wins the game.

				
				
					KomiThe komi is the whole number of points which is added to White's score at the end of the game as a compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, the first player chooses the value of komi, and then the second player chooses sides.

				
				
					NotesYugo is a Go variant inspired by Mark Steere's Redstone.

Online Play


     igGameCenter (real-time)

In Supérieur: Des Teufels Gebetbuch, designed by author Markus Heitz, who wrote the mystery thriller Des Teufels Gebetbuch (The Devil's Prayer Book), players must adopt their best poker manner to play this bluffing game in which players have a and of only a single card. Each round, they must decide whether to leave, stay, or perhaps even raise the stakes. In the end, whoever has the highest card takes the pot — as long as they don't draw the card of death, which eliminates them from the round immediately.

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague – Troy and Evil Troy
description:
2 new Survivors for Zombicide: Black Plague (including their miniatures and ID cards).

Description from the publisher:

Troy
Troy doesn’t belong in this kingdom, and he never really meant to get into the zombie-slaying business at all. But now that he’s here, now that he has no option but to swing his sword around rather than swinging around bales of hay, well, he’s going to be the best zombie-slayer there is. Maybe win himself a princess in the process, a small castle… There’s something to be said for being a hero, Troy is discovering, especially when it comes with lovely wenches and pocketfuls of gold. Of course, he has to survive all the zombies first.

Evil Troy
The necromancers have performed many experiments in their quest for the perfect evil, the perfect zombie. One of the worst things they managed was a simulacrum of a living being. This golem remembers everything the original human, Troy, knew; he has all of Troy’s skills. But he is as undead as a zombie, as angry as a raging bull, and as absolutely evil as it is possible to be. Evil Troy is a nasty piece of work, to put it shortly, intent on killing anything – human, zombie, or necromancer – that gets in his way.

boardgame: 続・リア充爆発しろ!! (Zoku Riajuu Bakuhatsu Shiro!!)
description:
User summary
続・リア充爆発しろ!! (Zoku Riajuu Bakuhatsu Shiro!!) is a Japanese card game by 楠本舗 (Kusunoki Honpo) released for the Game Market 2016 Spring in Japan.

In Japanese, リア充 (Riajuu) refers to "someone who is interested in real things and has a successful social life". It was invented in opposition to the word オタク (Otaku).

リア充爆発しろ!!, Riajuu bakuhatsu shiro!!, the title of the game, is a famous expression on the Japanese internet.

In this game, the players are split into 3 groups: Riajuu, non-Riajuu and Narcissist.
In the Debate Phase you try to get information from the other players on their identity, then in the Voting Phase you design the two players you think are Riajuu and you proceed to the Scoring Phase.
If the non-Riajuu could find the Riajuu, they get points.
If the Riajuu are not found by at least 2 person they get points.
If the Narcissist gets designated as a Riajuu, he get points.
After several rounds, the player with most points wins.

Publisher summary
とあるヲタサーの集会でリーダーが言い放った。
「この中にリア充がいるっ！」
サークルのルールは『裏切り者には死を』である。
サークルメンバーは一癖も二癖もある『スキル』を持った変人ばかり。
果たして非リア充たちはリア充を追放することができるのだろうか・・・。
artists: ヒューマンピクトグラム２．０様 (Human Pictogram 2.0-sama)

description:
User summary:
東京ダンガン ("Tokyo Dangan") is a tile-placement game by the Japanese publisher Grounding released at the Game Market 2017 Spring event in Japan.

In this game, the players are tourists visiting Tokyo.
The goal is to visit before the other players the 5 places you selected randomly. To move from one location to another, to the other, you need to make a road by placing tiles on the map and connect places. Stay alert as other players are able to rotate tiles or even remove them!

Description from the publisher:
～誰よりも！速く！東京名所を駆け抜けろ！
　弾丸（ダンガン）のスピードで！～
あなたは日帰り観光で「5箇所の目的地」を訪れるため、「東京」へとやってきました。 
しかし道が複雑でまるで迷路！　行きたい場所になかなか行けない！　残された時間も少ない！ 
迅速かつ効率よく、他の観光客より先に全ての目的地を訪れてください。東京ダンガンのスタートです。

User summary
Graduation Photo Records (グラデュエーション・フォトレコーズ！) is a photo-placement game by the Japanese publisher TRI Fol:um -Repens-. The game was released for the Game Market 2017 Kobe in Japan.

In this game, you play students about to graduate from high school. They have little time before their graduation to get ready a photo album with memories of their days as student friends.

A tabletop game where your objective is to collect photos of your youth life and create your student yearbook. Gameplay itself is simple: draw a photo, and mount them on the board. Though, each player character has hidden objectives (like which kind of photo they like, or who they are in love with), and since so, their interests should have some conflicts.

There are no special events in the game, but with many beautiful photos in a common Japanese school days settings, the nostalgic atmosphere should be appealing to many.

Gameplay
You play as one of eight character, and each character has hidden objectives. The deck of photos is like printed photos shot on film, not digital prints. Each turn, you may 'shoot a photo' (draw a photo from the deck and place it face down in front of you), 'develop/print' (turn one of your face-down photos face-up), or 'layout on board' (place your printed photo on the shared album board).

The board doesn't have a grid, you may place the photo anyway you like. You may overlap any previously placed photos with just one restriction: at least one character's face must be fully visible from the underlying photos.

When the 'endgame' photo is drawn (it is loaded somewhere near the bottom of the deck), you tally points based on your hidden objectives.

The gameplay itself has low language dependencies, but the 8 student ID cards (with the face of the character and their hidden objectives) have Japanese on them. The photo itself has some handwritten Japanese scribbles with their character names, and some objectives refer to the other characters by these names ("4pts: have 2 or more photos with Miki at the end"). Therefore, if you cannot read JP letters well, you might struggle to properly recognise the names as they are presented. So the game needs a little more than a crib-sheet or memory, you need to be able to spot these names easily.

Publisher summary
写真を撮影、現像して
隠した想いを達成しつつ
あなた達だけの卒業アルバムを作るボードゲーム
～写真を重ねて創り出す、あなた達だけの卒業アルバム～

■簡単な概要！
①写真を撮影、
②現像して、
③アルバムを編集していく
――というボードゲームです。

各プレイヤーごとに秘めた思惑を達成しつつ、
素敵な卒業アルバムを仕上げましょう！

青春が描かれた写真カードを集めて、思い出の卒業アルバムを作成するボードゲーム。
システムとしては、写真を引いてアルバム台紙に置いていくだけのシンプルな物。
ただ、それぞれが隠し持った点数目標(写真の好みや意中の相手によって差別化されている)に向かって利害関係を作っている。
特別なイベントは無いが、日本の学生が思わず「懐かしい」と思えるような透明感のあるイラストとノスタルジックな世界観が魅力的。

A card and board game for 2-6 players in which each player takes the role of a Demon Hunter.

The game board depicts Europe in the late 1800s. 20 cities are marked on the board, with each city connected to 1-4 other nearby cities.  Some cities may have an effect on die rolls or creature capabilities.

There are 3 decks: 
-   The Character Deck represents advantageous traits that the characters start the game with.
The Research Deck comprises cards aid that aid the Demon Hunters in their quest. Players acquire these during play. Card types include: Events, Spells, Weapons, Armour, Items, and Companions. 
-   The Creature Deck need to be small to fit easily on the map and represent the creatures that can be found in the various cities. Creatures have a strength and a victory point score (earned by defeating them)

Each player starts with 5 Hits. Each player draws and keeps 5 character cards.  Each player rolls 1D20 to determine their starting City. 
Randomly distribute the X top cards of the creature deck to random cities, where X = the number of players. (Max Monsters in any one city at start is 2)

Players take turns with each turn having 4 Phases:

1.  Destiny Phase
-   The current player draws the top research card from the deck and places it in his hand.

2.  Evil Phase
-   For each player roll 1D6: on a roll of 4+ flip over the top card of the creature deck and for each new creature roll 1D20.  Place the creature on the indicated City.

3.  Action Phase
-   The Current player may take 2 actions. There are 6 possible actions: 
1.  Rest Action - Roll on a healing table to heal any hit points lost.
2.  Travel Action – Roll on a movement table to move 0-2 spaces.
3.  Search Action – Roll on a search table to determine whether you can find a creature to fight.
4.  Recruit Action - Put one Weapon, Item, Armour, or Companion card into play. A character can only have a maximum of 1 armour card, 2 weapon cards and 2 companion cards in Play
5.  Research Action - Roll on a research table and draw 0-3 research cards from the deck and put them in your hand. (You may do a max of 1 research action per turn)
6.  Trade Action - If you are in the same city as 1 or more other Demon Hunters, you may trade resource cards, in hand or in play.

4.  Fate Phase 
-   Max hand size is 7 cards. Discard excess. 
-   If at the end of a turn, any city has 5 or more creatures, it is 
overrun and all players lose.

When fighting creatures roll 1D20 (The Battle Roll) and add any force bonuses you might have. If this number is equal to or greater than the strength of the creature you defeat it. Place defeated creatures in your victory pile. 
If your modified roll was less, the creature remains in play and roll on the Wound Table taking damage accordingly. If you are reduced to zero Hits skip your next turn and return to play with just 1 hit. 
If after losing a battle roll, you still have hits you may decide to either 
continue the combat or flee. If you decide to continue, make another battle roll against the same creature. If you decide to flee, you may stay in the city or move 1 space.

The game ends at the end of the turn the creature deck has been exhausted. The player with the most Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The Brotherhood Without Banners
description:
Description from the publisher:

Take up arms with outlaws in The Brotherhood Without Banners, the sixth Chapter Pack of the "Blood and Gold" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! As the cycle draws to a climactic conclusion, you have the chance to see the themes of the cycle more fully realized, with new economy cards and new support for the bestow keyword across all eight factions. Whether you're serving the Night's Watch with Donal Noye or leading the brotherhood with Beric Dondarrion, this expansion has plenty to offer, including a brand-new agenda inviting you to join the brotherhood without banners.

boardgame: Police Precinct: Richard Austria Character Board
description:
Was available through Police Precinct - Online! kickstarter campaign as well as part of a reward from the Dice Tower 2016 (Season 12) Kickstarter.

Text:

Rapport: As a trained experience Community Resource Officer you can impact situations others can't:
At any time you can turn in a donut to add or subtract a pip to any die rolled in relation to your location if any punk tokens are also present

Respect: They look up to you, so interviewing juveniles when they are away from their cohorts works well: 
At the end of your turn you may draw an investigation card from any deck if you are parked next to a lone punk token

Gangster cards for Tom, Eric, Zee and Sam.

Available from various Dice Tower events.

Tom "The Tower" Vaselini: Tom has all the special abilities of gangsters with a lower position number.

Eric "Sweet Talk" Summerino: When Eric takes and action, he may reveal the top three cards of the Gangster deck and take any or all of the revealed actions on those cards. Revealed Gangsters to to "Sleepin' with the Fishes".

Mighty Zee Garcia: Zee's '$' and 'R' are equal to the highest values present in all active Gangsters on the table.

Sammy "The Storm" Healetti: Sam has all the special abilities of other Gangsters in the same row.

boardgame: Temp Worker Assassins: Rabid Reprocessing Dept. Promo Card
description:
"Put up to 2 trashed cards on the top of a player's deck."

Available from the publisher as an event promo card.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Bonus-Box
description: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Bonus-Box, released to celebrate the fifth anniversary of Legends of Andor, includes printed versions of the legends "Die Befreiung der Mine" and "Die Eskorte des Königs", the new legends "Angriff der Barbaren" and "Der Vorbote des Feuers" (solo legend), bonus material for use with the base game, as well as a soundtrack CD.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons Dice Masters: Tomb of Annihilation
description:
Dungeons & Dragons Dice Masters is a game that utilizes WizKids Games' proprietary Dice Building Game platform in which two players take the role of warlords, collect and assemble their "party" of adventurer dice and battle in head-to-head gameplay.  Each turn, players roll the dice to see what resources they have available, buy dice, send their party members into the field, and then strike at the enemy warlord. There are multiple cards available for each type of die so players get to choose which one  to use. This lets players specialize dice to suit play style.

The name and theme of the new expansion set will be the Tomb of Annihilation, set in the Forgotten Realms.  This set will offer new adventures, monsters, and spells for the game, as well as a new “Draft Pack” format, designed for tournament play.

Each Draft Pack will include 2 Basic Action cards, 12 Character or Action cards, 24 dice that match the 12 cards, and a rules insert.  All components can be used with other Dungeons & Dragons Dice Masters sets, but the sets have been designed for better use in draft-based games. Any Dice Masters Starter Set will be required to play (for starting NPC dice and Basic Action color indicator cards and dice).

The Dungeons & Dragons Dice Masters: Tomb of Annihilation will be sold in countertop displays.  Each display includes eight Draft Packs.

The ? card is mixed in with all other cards at the start of the game. It counts as a wild and always takes on the lowest value of any card played by other players. Thus, this card can change its value several times before you discard it from play (either because you pushed the card through or because it is flushed down the drain by other players).

Included in every copy of Get The Cheese! from Stronghold Games.

The Spanish Civil War is an Axis & Allies variant that takes place in Spain from 1936 - 1939.

It is in the form of a booklet and comes with:
Two 11" by 17" game maps
One 12 page rule book
One sheet of cut out counters

In addition to this game, you will need a set of plastic military figures to represent the game units and at least on ten sided die.

description:
We’ve all heard stories of the great heroes and legends that span the worlds of fantasy, raiding dungeons, toppling strongholds, and slaying malicious monsters. We’ve all sat and listened to the tales of evil warlords and sorcerers raising armies and rancorous beasts to do their biddings. Now’s your chance to be the very source of all these stories! Jump behind the seller’s table. Rife through all the alchemical, magical, and sharp bits and goodies that make up the very foundation of the worlds of fantasy! And match them all up with the tricky characters that are willing to buy ‘em for your good ole fashion gold!

Black Market Bonanza is a living card game where Players select their special Seller Character Card and draw their own Seller's Table worth of Contraband Items to sell to Customers for precious gold coin counters. In the Contraband Deck, Players will find unique Contraband Items, some with special abilities, and tricky Sleight Of Hand Cards that be used for your own benefit or at the expense of your opponents! In the Market Deck, Players will draw the coveted Customer Encounters, the fun and random Market Events, or the dreaded Guard Encounter Cards! Beware of being thrown into Lockdown! Can't play the game much if you're stuck in a cell! All things in the Market Deck depend on your Seller's Skill Checks and lucky dice rolls, so be sure to choose your Seller wisely!

Your ultimate goal is to be the first Player to get to 10 Gold! So get those coins clanking into stacks, get all your items sold, and be the first to take control in Black Market Bonanza!

A worker placement/set collection card game about mining gold during the American Gold Rush.

Put your miners to work in the gold mines. Get as much gold as possible, but watch out – make sure you aren’t wasting time collecting Fool’s Gold! Pay to check the quality of gold in the mines, and call out your opponent for trying to pay with Fool’s Gold or just plain rocks. Collect the highest values of gold by the end of the game to win.

Finansije za kasnije ("Finance for Old Days") is a game about different financial products and how to use them. Players start with a birth and childhood, after that they have to decide would they work or study. On the half of the game they form family, and at last work for better carrier. They have different financial products, cash credits, home credits, savings, private pension fund and etc. Goal is to collect as much money for retirement. 
Game is not for sale, but for education purpose, my firm is arranging turners around the country in middle schools to teach pupils how to save money.

boardgame: Guilds of London: The Secret Guild of Guildsmen
description:
Promo card included in The Cousins' War. Acts as another guild.

"At the start of the game, put this card into play next to the guild tiles. Liverymen can be moved onto this card like a regular guild. When it resolves, the winner of this guild gets to take 1 of their liverymen that was on this card and place it [as a master] on an existing guild tile controlled by any player; then flip this card over (do not place a liveryman on it once flipped - i.e. it is not itself controlled by a master)."

How Savvy are you?

Starting with a random card between 1 and 36, players have to decide whether the next card drawn will be higher or lower to accumulate points... but this is no game of luck!

Each time you choose correctly, you accumulate a point in the 'holding area', and have the chance to choose higher or lower again from the new number, and get another point each time you guess correctly. BUT... guess incorrectly and lose all the points from that turn! The only way to keep your points is to elect to stop, at which point the next player continues from the current number.

Extra care is needed on the first guess of each of your turns too! You can elect to guess and start earning more points, but get your first guess wrong and you will lose half of all the points you have in your 'bank'!!!  Or, if its too risky, pass straight away to the next player, but this will cost you one point from your bank. If you've nothing in your bank, you must call!

Players on low points at the start of their turn, get another go if they get their first call wrong.

Counters mark which cards have already been drawn on a simple mat, so while this is a brilliant game of risk for anyone to play, it's also perfect for developing numerical and probability skills with children of all ages!

For 2-6 players, aged 8 and upwards, and a playing time of 10-40 minutes. The longer time scale relates to six players, the banter, and the liquid refreshment at hand.

More info and game session videos are available on the website at https://savvygames.co.uk

Description from the publisher:

Dusk is falling. Wind is howling. Will you survive the night? It’s possible, if only you can make it to the Safe House. Grab your friends and dash across the neighborhood. Duck in and out of rundown houses, hiding as best as you can. The house at the end of the street is almost in sight. You can make it, just keep running. You can make it. Just. Keep. Running.

In Safe House, players are attempting to cooperatively save survivors. It’s a real time game that demands good teamwork and great communication. It’s frantic fun for all participants.

Zombicide: Friends and Foes is an expansion for Zombicide: Green Horde. On the "Friends" side, it includes new Survivors and weapons to equip them, but also faithful Familiars to aid them in their quests. On the "Foes" side, the expansion contains challenging Tainted Walkers and Abomination, from which you'll want to keep your distance, 5 new treacherous tiles, and 10 new deadly Quests!

The full contents of the Zombicide: Friends and Foes expansion are:

4 Survivor figures and ID cards
6 Familiar figures
18 Tainted Walker figures
1 Tainted Abomination figure
5 double-sided tiles
17 Equipment cards
14 Zombie cards
Rulebook with 10 new Quests

description:
Version 2.0 coming 2023-2024.

In the pristine little neighborhood of Gnome Crossing, the competition for the highly coveted award of “Yard of the Month” is fierce. The residents will stop at nothing to win, including sabotage. While some gnomes will beautify and protect a garden, some gnomes will curse it, resulting in quite the eye sore.

Will you be the first to fill your garden with 10 flowers and be crowned “Yard of the Month,” or will you be the laughing stock of the neighborhood.

Yard of the Month is a family-friendly card game for 2 - 6 players. In the game, each player (neighbor) is competing to have the best flower garden. You have access to resources to either help or hinder a garden, including the garden gnomes Gnick and Gnack. While Gnick will keep your garden safe, Gnack's goal is to curse it, but that isn't anything a hammer can't handle.

Go Go Dragons! is a dragon racing gaming designed to encourage participation and social interaction, promote observation and turn taking and introduce probability and chance.

In Go Go Dragons 2-4 players select a coloured dragon and take it in turns flipping over randomised coloured discs. When a disc is revealed the dragon matching the colour of the disc is moved forward one space. Once the dragon in last place reaches the discs on the board the disc is turned over and the matching coloured dragon is moved back one space

The winner is the first dragon to cross the finish line.

Blue Mongolia is a Monopoly reimplementation with a Mongolian theme and a trivia component.

Players move around a circular board buying and selling cultural elements of Mongolia (from the Gobi desert to the Mongolian costumes to the State play of Mongolia). When a player lands on an "About Mongolia" space, they must answer a trivia question, earning money if they answer correctly and losing money if they answer incorrectly.

You pick a random slang term from the deck and everyone writes a made up but convincing definition for the word and anonymously hands it in.

One person reads all the definitions aloud, including the actual definition of the word, which is randomly mixed in.

Everyone guesses which definition is the real one and get points if they do. They usually fall for someone else's bullshit definition -- which earns its author a point.

Differences to regular balderdash would be more of a party-game for the adult crowd, adult-material.

Grab a plastic bottle (bottle not included. Additional purchase required) and start flipping with the Flip Challenge game! The game rules are simple – ace the Flip Challenges to earn points. First player to 10 points wins! Kids can practice the 34-included trick shot challenge cards solo or compete with friends! Game includes four different sized round targets and 34 bottle-shaped cards, each showing a challenge for the bottle flip. Challenges are grouped by category: some use the targets, some are physical challenges, and 6 cards challenge players to create their own trick shot. Each player has 5 chances to complete a challenge. So, grab a lucky water bottle, and go for the perfect flip!

A card game for 2 players based on the movie “Sorcerers Apprentice”. One player is the good Merlinians. The other is the evil Morganians.

Players share a common deck containing cards of seven types: 
Merlinian Sorcerers, Morganian Sorcerers, Spells, Amplifiers, Objects, Events and Defence.  Most cards have a power rating which is used during the duel (combat) phase.

The Merlinian player starts with Balthazar in play and the Grimhold which is equipped to Balthazar. The Morganian player starts with Horvath in play.

Both players draw 5 cards.

Each turn has 6 Phases:

1.  Lore Phase
-   Both players draw 2 cards from the deck.

2.  Recruit Phase
-   You may put one sorcerer into play from your hand and any number of objects. Sorcerers may give their objects to each other (max 3 objects per sorcerer). Excess objects are discarded. 
-   The Merlinian player may only recruit Merlinian Sorcerers. 
-   The Morganian player may only recruit Morganian Sorcerers.

3.  Event Phase
-   Starting with the Merlinian player, players may take turns discarding cards with event abilities to activate and immediately resolve those abilities.

4.  Encounter Phase
-   Each player randomly picks one of their sorcerers to undertake a duel.

5.  Duel Phase
-   The two Sorcerers who have encountered each other will now fight as follows.
-   Each player will make an attack combo which are then revealed simultaneously. For their attack combo each player may play one spell, one event and up to 3 amplifiers
-   Derive a power total for each sorcerer = the sorcerer’s power + equipped objects + spell + played event + amplifiers.
-   Each player may play up to one defence card. These will cause certain opposing cards to be discarded, thus changing the power totals. 
-   The sorcerer with the higher power total is the combat winner. The losing sorcerer is discarded.
-   The combat Loser may play a protection spell. This prevents the losing sorcerer from being discarded and his objects from being stolen.
-   The winner takes the losing Sorcerers objects AND looks at his opponents hand AND then steals one target card from his opponents hand.

6.  End Phase
-   Max hand size is 5 cards. Discard excess cards. 
-   Check for Victory Conditions. Record Time Markers.

The Merlinian player has 2 ways to win: 
1.  Destroy Morgana: Defeat her by 7 or more power in a Combat. 
2.  Prime Merlinian Succession: Keep Dave Stutler in play for 7 consecutive turns.

The Morganian player has 1 way to win: 
Cast the Rising Spell: Keep Morgana in play for 5 consecutive turns.

Both players may win by the following method: 
Army of Sorcerers: Have 6 more sorcerers in play than your opponent in the end phase.

A card game for 2-4 players based on survival and accomplishment in the Florida City of St. Augustine.

St. Augustine was founded in 1565 by Pedro Menendez. It is the oldest, continuously inhabited European settlement in North America. For the first 200 years of its existence, it was controlled by Spain. The town itself was always very small and poor, but it held an important strategic position. It helped defend the treasure fleets on their way back to Spain by preventing the English and French from building their own settlements along the Gulf stream.

In this game players actively recruit townsfolk, earn gold, gain military glory, and build structures while being mercilessly subjected to hurricanes, raids, disease, and hostile indians.

Each turn is equal to about a decade. The game ends after 20 turns, at which point, historically, the British take control of the city.

Each Player gets a player mat which is divided up into 7 boxes: Government, Churches, Forts, Population, Gold, Glory and Victory Points. Tokens are placed in the boxes on player mats to keep track of each players score in that category. Each player starts with 7 population, 5 gold, and 3 glory.

There are 2 Common Decks: 
1.  The Event Deck - one random event occurs each turn. 
2.  The Action Deck which comprises four suits:
-   Economic (Types: Industry, Agriculture, Trade)
-   Military (Types: Fight the French, Fight the English, Control) 
-   Population (Types: Indians, Blacks, Europeans)
-   Buildings (Types: Government, Churches, Forts)
Each suit has 3 types of card with 4 cards for each type. In addition the deck has 4 leader (wild) cards.

Each turn is divided into 7 Phases:

1.  Draw Phase
-   Each Player fills their hand to 10 action cards.

2.  Trade Phase
-   Players may trade action cards with each other and may also trade tokens on their player mats.

3.  Meld Phase
-   Players may make melds. A meld consists of 1-4 cards of the same type. One card in a meld may be a leader. One card in a meld earns 1 token. Two cards in a meld earns 3 tokens. Three cards in a meld earns 6 tokens. Four cards in a meld earns 10 tokens. 
-   You can make a max of 4 melds, one from each suit. 
-   Tokens earned go into the relevant box on the player’s mat. (e.g. tokens from a population meld go into the population box, tokens from a military meld go into the glory box).

4.  Building Phase
-   Buildings provide bonus resources. Tokens are awarded for having the highest and second highest church, government and fort scores.

5.  Event Phase
 -  Flip over the top card of the event deck. The event is resolved immediately and affects all players.

6.  Score Phase
-   Players gain Victory Points (VP) in based on their population, gold and glory scores.

7.  End Phase
-   Players may discard their action hands down to zero cards.
-   Each player loses one half his gold rounding down. 
-   Each player loses all his Glory.

The game ends after 20 turns.  The player with the most VP at the end of the game wins.

The Roots of Magic is a tabletop miniatures game of duels between competing wizards in fantasy world. Players activate their wizards to perform mundane actions such as movement as well as casting spells and using other special abilities. The goal of the game varies depending on the scenario chosen and there might be multiple paths to victory.

The game can be played with a single wizard per player or the players may decide to use additional models such as apprentices and animal companions for a larger game. Individual scenarios may also add neutral monsters to the game.

The game rules are published online and the models are sold individually. Each model has their own card listing their various abilities. Players may add also tabletop terrain to the game from their collection.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Echoes of the Past: Mythos Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Echoes of the Past is the first Mythos Pack in The Path to Carcosa cycle for Arkham Horror: The Card Game.

The King in Yellow has come to Arkham, you caught the performance, and now you find yourself drawn into the maddening web of powerful forces and sinister event that seems to surround the mysterious play. So how do you proceed? Where do you continue your investigations? How you do hold onto the tattered threads of your limited sanity? If there are ties between the play and Arkham, there's one place where you might uncover them—the Arkham Historical Society, a landmark building that holds age-old secrets even its curator doesn’t know…

Even as it introduces a new scenario through which you can explore the Society’s hidden secrets—and the many terrors with which they are associated—Echoes of the Past introduces a wealth of player cards driven by two major themes that allow you to refine your investigator’s practiced Talent and Composure.

description:
ASL JOURNAL 12 is a 40-page magazine and expansion for Advanced Squad Leader and features nine articles, the latest Q&A/errata, and 12 new scenarios printed on cardstock. Also included is a corrected scenario card for scenario 181 Gavin Take from the recent YANKS, which was missing its red text and is being replaced here.

JOURNAL 12 includes the following articles:


    The Umpire's view - Executive editors comment on this issue and coming games.
    A Dose of Antifreeze - Peter Struijf helps guide you through the decision matrix of how to defeat enemy vehicles using bypass "freeze". 
    ASL Crossfire: AP119 Konev Cross John Slotwinski and Phil Palmer discuss their playing of this Action Pack 12 scenario.
    Advanced Nuggets - Paul Sidhu brings light to some obscure and easily-overlooked rules.
    "Ghost Killers" - Andy Hershey recounts the history of the Korean Marine Corps, featured in the upcoming Korean War ASL module FORGOTTEN WAR which Andy helped design.
    Out With The Old... - Chas Argent looks at how old counter art from the Avalon Hill days is being updated for new MMP edition core modules.
    MOSSMOUSE! - Paul Sidhu is back and explains how the "Principles of War" as taught by the United States Army can be applied to your ASL play.
    Single-Hex Fire Groupology - Ken Dunn & J.R. Tracy de-mystify the Defensive First/Final Fire options available to units stacked together in the same Location.
    Debriefing This issue's errata updates.
    Scenario Analysis: AP71 Head In The Noose - Matt Shostak brings his magic analytical eye to this Action Pack 7 scenario.
    Recon: Flames in Hatten - Chas Argent previews Andrew Rogers's upcoming HASL on this German-vs-American HASL set during Operation NORDWIND in January 1945.


JOURNAL 12 also includes

12 new ASL scenarios:
J182 Belgian Blitzkrieg (German vs Belgian, 27 May 1940)
J183 A Real Barn Burner (French vs German, 31 May 1940)
J184 Dayan To Meet You (Australian vs Vichy, 8 June 1941)
J185 The Haunted Castle (German vs Russian, 22 June 1941)
J186 Castles On The Horizon (German vs British, 20 November 1941)
J187 In Deadly Combat (Russian vs German, 30 December 1941)
J188 Grab And Go (Russian vs German, 20 August 1943)
J189 Buckley's Block (Japanese vs U.S.M.C., 13 January 1944)
J190 Trial Run (Canadian vs German, 17 January 1944)
J191 Rebels Without A Pause (Slovak vs German, 5 September 1944)
J192 Taking Some Flak (Finn vs German, 28 October 1944)
J193 Raff's Rules (American vs German, 24 March 1945)

1 YANKS replacement scenario card (180-181)


2/8/17 JOURNAL 12 corrections:

• J192 Taking Some Flak: In the VC, last sentence, between "count" and "for" add "double".

• On page 31 of JOURNAL 12, the last sentence in the box of the "Single-Hex Firegroupology" article indicates that Final Fire in the DFPh might be available after having used Subsequent First Fire in the MPh. But that is not the case, since using SFF would mark a unit with a Final Fire counter, which precludes firing in the DFPh. We regret this error, which was introduced by the Editors in the editing process.

A set collection game using a board common to Monopoly and Bonanza style. Instead of buying and charging rent for spaces, the players compete to complete sets played on the board. The cards and matching spaces are based on Amish communities in a handful of US states.

Description from the publisher (translates):

10 extra cards that add a dose of cowardice and twists to your games of the famous 6 nimmt. With these new cards and two rule variants, the game will be even more spicy.

No matter their race, origin or desires, everyone has the right to be a child. Be it a knight or a magician, when we lie down to sleep the unconscious thoughts seem to transcend all realities. It is up to us, which is the connection between desire and thought, and the best children to all as persons. There are so many dreams created that are not so much that they have nicknamed our house of Factory of Dreams!

Take control of a mystical character such as Fairy, Unicorn, Gnome or Ente and build the dreams that citizens both crave in thoughts. Each character has a special ability that assists in their production and each citizen's card has a different dream to be built. During a match, it is necessary to send out items and emotions cards on the table according to what each citizen card is demanding.

How about joining that magnificent sword, a little courage, and giving the Warrior the glory of that very successful moment for him to kill the fearless Dragon ... in his dreams, of course! Can you be the best mystic in the game and be the employee of the month in the Factory of Dreams? Come and test your gifts, come and dream of us!

GAME SETUP: Each player stands at one end of the table. Your position is important because it determines whether a Desire card is valid for you or not according to the arrows of the cards. All players choose a character. Shuffle the Wishes deck (6 types) and reveal 16 cards in the center of the table placing four cards per row creating a 4x4 square. Shuffle the deck of Dreams and deal 2 cards to each player. The Dream cards are the template from which the player must form the same configuration with the cards arranged on the table.

GAMEPLAY: Fábrica dos Sonhos is divided into turns and actions. Each player on his turn performs the following mandatory actions:
World Change - You must remove a Wish Card from the table and place it in your hand, then replace that card with another one from the top of the Desire Deck or another one in your hand ( maximum of three cards in the hand). If you exceed the limit, before you pick up a new card, you must discard one of the cards in your hand.
Intercept - Choose between moving Desires Cards on the table or buying a new Dream card. The movement of Desires is simple. The movement points you have are equal to the number of players +1 . You can swap two orthogonally adjacent cards, or two cards diagonally that are on the corners of the 4x4 diagram. You can still rotate a letter anywhere. Each change of position or rotation counts as a point of movement.

GOAL: The objective of the players is to format the cards of the table according to the template of the cards of Dreams in their hands. The winner is the player who can achieve 3 dreams first.

COMPONENTS:
1 Rules Book
4 Character Sheet
20 Dreams Cards
38 Desires Cards

Game Overview:
Monarchs, Minions & Mayhem! is a Fantasy Themed Card-Driven Strategy Board Game. It has engaging gameplay that is extremely fun to play, well-balanced with innovative and unique game mechanics that will provide endless hours of entertainment with family & friends.

Number of Players: 3-8 (2-Player Variant Rules Included)
Or 3-4 (2-Player Teams)
Ages: 14+
Playing Time: 30+ Minutes
Game Components: Rulebook, 18”x18” Game Board, 108 Custom Playing Cards (poker size 2.5”x3.5”), (9) Gemstones & (1) 12-sided Dice.

Game Sets-Up in 5 minutes or less.
Simple to follow Rules & Game Play.
Replay-ability is virtually limitless. 
No two games will ever be the same.

 
Object of the Game:
Winning a round: The first player that has Zero (0) cards in their playing hand at the start of their next turn wins the round and gains 1 Gemstone.

Winning the game: After 3 rounds (Short Campaign), 6 rounds (Standard Campaign) or 9 rounds (Long Campaign) of play the player or team with the lowest casualty point score determined during the End Game Tally of the game (total casualty points minus Gemstone Point Bonus), Wins!

Setup & Dealer:
Players determine which campaign mode shall be played: Short Campaign (3 rounds), Standard Campaign (6 rounds) or Long Campaign (9 rounds). Players may opt unanimously at the end of a short or standard campaign to continue the game to the next campaign level if desired. 
 
Dealer: Either roll high (d12) or If playing a series of games, the winner of the last game is the dealer for initial set-up and the first round of play. The dealer in rounds 2 thru 9 changes to the player with the lowest total casualty points (score). Shuffle the 108 custom playing card deck and deals 1 card face down on each one of the 12 Battlefield Events.
 
Each player is then dealt 7 cards. The unused cards are placed face down as the Draw Deck and the top card is flipped face up to form the Battlefield.

Gameplay:
To start a round, the dealer shuffles the cards in the Draw Deck, Battlefield, Battlefield Event Wildcard and the Discard pile. Seven cards are then dealt out to each player from the Draw Deck, and the top card is flipped face up to form the battlefield. On the first round either roll high (d12) or if playing a series of games, the player with the highest score in the previous game flips over any one of the 12 face down cards in the Battlefield Event Area that is the Battlefield Event Wildcard that is in play (Revealed!) for the current round.

On rounds 2 thru 9 the player that “Won” the previous round and gained the Crown Jewel flips over any one of the remaining face down cards in the Battlefield Event Area, and places it in the area noted on the game board as the “Battlefield Event Wildcard”. 
 
Order of play begins with the person to the left of the dealer and proceeds in the direction determined by the card played on top of the existing card in the Battlefield.


     - If the Played card is lower rank = Play Direction Reversed to Counter Clockwise (Right).
     - Played card is higher rank = Play Direction Advances Clockwise (Left).
     - Played card is of equal rank (Stalemate) = player chooses direction of play left or right. 


If the Draw Deck is depleted, the battlefield (except the top card) is shuffled and becomes the new Draw Deck. Cards in the discard pile may only be shuffled into the Draw Deck at the beginning of each new round.

Turn Sequence: “Order of Play”
1.   Royal Decree Phase:  Player may choose to play a Monarch Card using the card’s Royal Decree.  The Royal Decree invokes any one Revealed Battlefield Event that the player chooses; if the Monarch card is played in this manner it is placed in the Discard pile and removed from play for the remainder of the round. There is no limit to the number of Monarch Cards that can be played during the players Royal Decree Phase using the Monarch Royal Decree. Regardless of whether or not a player plays a Monarch Card during the Royal Decree Phase, the player proceeds to the Battlefield Play Phase or Draw Phase of their turn.

2.  Battlefield Play Phase:  The player has several options to choose from, if available.


     - The player is not required to play a playable card in their playing hand if he/she chooses not to.



     - The player may play an Emissary or Scales of Justice card from their hand on top of any card in the Battlefield. Automatically changes faction currently in play to the faction type of the Emissary card. Continue on to the Battlefield Result Phase: The outcome of the battle is a Stalemate. After the faction change effect is resolved the rank value Emissary card is zero (0) for the next players turn.



     - The player may play a Monarch card as a Faction card from their hand on top of the card in the Battlefield. If the Monarch card either matches the faction or rank of the card in the Battlefield, continue on to the Battlefield Result Phase.



     - The player may play a Minion card as a Faction card from their hand on top of the card in the Battlefield. If the Minion card either matches the faction or rank of the card in the Battlefield, continue on to the Battlefield Result Phase.



     - The player may play a Minion card that matches the Rank of the Battlefield Event Wildcard that invokes any one of the Revealed! Battlefield Event that the player chooses. Resolve the Battlefield Event, and continue on to the Battlefield Result Phase.


3.  Draw Phase:  If the player was unable to play a card or chose not to play a card onto the Battlefield during the Battlefield Play Phase, they must draw a card from the Draw Deck.


     - If the card drawn by the player can be played as specified in sequential order of the preceding Royal Decree Phase or Battlefield Play Phase, the player may opt to do so.



     - The player is not required to play the drawn card even if it is a playable card.


4.  Pass Phase:  (end of players turn) If the player does not or cannot play a card onto the Battlefield, the player passes and play moves on to the next left (clockwise) player.

5.  Battlefield Result Phase:  (end of players turn) If the player has played a card on to the Battlefield, the Battlefield Result is determined by the card played on top of the existing card in the Battlefield.


     - Played card is lower rank = Play Direction Reversed to Counter Clockwise (Right).
     - Played card is higher rank = Play Direction Advances Clockwise (Left).
     - Played card is of equal rank (Stalemate) = player choses direction of play left or right.

boardgame: My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic – The Cooperative Adventure Game
description:
Players take on the roll of the mane six ponies from the animated series and work together to prepare for a confrontation with one of the series villains. If the ponies have made enough progress, they can thwart the villain.

Each turn the players move their ponies around the game board, which is a map of Ponyville and Equestria.  They then attempt to deal with plot issue cards by rolling dice; other ponies can help them by rolling additional dice.  Successfully dealing with the issue cards allows them to advance on their Element track.  When they advance far enough, they will gain their Element of Harmony.

Meanwhile, the villain is plotting.  The villain has a progress track which advances each turn and when new issue cards come into play.  Villain event cards will come into play as the track advances and which will cause problems for the ponies.  Once the villain track reached the final space, the confrontation begins.  Ponies then attempt to defeat the villain with a series of dice rolls.  Their success will depend largely on how many Elements of Harmony they have obtained during the game.

Each of the mane six ponies has her own special ability: Twilight Sparkle can advance Element tracks by doing research, Rarity can collect gems to help her resolve issues, Fluttershy is good with animals, Rainbow Dash's movement is faster than the others, Applejack is good at helping other ponies resolve their issues, and Pinkie Pie can help other ponies avoid setbacks.

Players can play stand-alone games or a campaign game made up of a series of games, with a different villain in each game.  The ponies can gain new abilities and even become alicorns as the campaign continues.

This is a two-player game where one player controls the 32 Peasants and one player controls the Three Guardsmen.  The guardsmen control the castle ( 3 x 3 positions) and the peasants try to gain control of the castle.  They start in the four corners of the board (each corner has 8 positions.)

To play: Peasants move first and then each player moves alternately.  Peasants move one space at a time and never jump.  Guardsmen may move one space or may jump one peasant, removing it from play. Guardsmen must jump if possible or be removed from play.  Moves can go in any direction, following the lines on the board.  Once in castle, the peasant can not leave.  The guardsmen can move in and out at will.

Peppermill's Energy Conservation Game

From Cover:
Q. Is there really an energy crisis?
A. Yes, we are using up our favorite non-renewable fossil fuels at a furious pace, but we can learn how to conserve and use solar energy for most of our energy needs.  That's what this game is all about.

From Instructions:
Energy Crisis is a fast moving, game of chance, strategy, and educational fun.  It will challenge adults and fascinate children.  It's a game the whole family will enjoy.

Game Play:
This is a role and move game where players are trying to insulate their house, convert to solar energy and conserve your energy units.  Use your energy units to pay your bills.  There are various spaces on the board where you buy solar collectors and insulation, collect energy, and pay bills.  Play continues until one player acquires 10 solar collectors or until all other players run out of energy units.

An epidemic of apocalyptic proportions has swept the globe with the bite-size edition of The Walking Dead™ Trivial Pursuit®. Featuring 600 questions that cover the characters and events included in the popular comic book series, even the most ardent fans will be challenged to fight for their survival. First person to collect six cards becomes the last survivor.

Contents:

100 cards feature 600 questions

Categories include: People, Locations, Events, Issue Numbers, The Dead and Wild Card
 1 Custom Die
 Wedge case card holder
 Instructions

Underlords is a 1-4 player fantasy game where players take on the role of the Underlords, hard done by masters of the dark who only want to make an innocent evil subterranean empire filled with minions and monsters. Alas these tranquil utopias find no peace as pesky adventurers keep turning up at the door trying to steal everything and killing every minion in sight.

In Underlords players use tile placement to build a dungeon full of rooms and caves, into which minions and monsters are placed. Each turn the underlord push their luck by sending monsters out to raid villages, towns or cities to gather plunder which is used to recruit minions and build the dungeon.

Each turn one hero arrives at the dungeon seeking treasure. Depending on how hard the Underlord raided, this could be a plucky peasant or a gristle nemesis of evil. The hero travels through the dungeon in a tower defence fashion being battled on the way by the dungeons defenders.

The game is won by the player who accrues the most magic items from fallen hero's by the end of the game.

This is a Sudanese version of Monopoly.

"With regards from The Modern Bank of Luck
The only agent in Sudan"

It is completely in Arabic, but I've had most of it translated.  The mechanics are the same, however with slightly different rule variations.  Properties focus on Arabic cities in Northern Africa and the Middle East including Khartoum, Aleppo, Benghazi and Baghdad.  You can purchase garages, restrooms or markets.

Description from the rules translated into English:
"The Bank of Luck game is one of the best games that guarantees players a good time and makes people love honest trade in the sale and purchase of cities, garages, restrooms, etc.  It is one of the most famous games played by 2 to 6 players and preferably more than 2 players."

boardgame: WarQuest: 5th Player Expansion and Experienced Units
description: WarQuest: 5th Player Expansion and Experienced Units includes everything you need to play WarQuest with more people as well as an experienced unit system and an imperial guard die for each faction! The rules are a compendium for quick reference to get you in the action quicker, too!

Metal miniature and Expansion Card for Tentacle Bento

Card Details
Discard up to three cards and then draw an equal number of cards from the school pile.

Metal miniature and Expansion Card for Tentacle Bento

Card Details
Target opponent draws two cards from the school pile.

Metal miniature and Expansion Card for Tentacle Bento

Card Details
