Description from the publisher (Translated):
Metalcorps is a game of cards and dice.
Players become metalcorps pilots ("mech" or "mazinguer z"), and must fight for control of the world.

Each Metalcorps has been created by corporations from different parts of the world; USA, Japan, Europe, ..

But being a pilot will not be easy, as your metalcorps will have 35 energy levels that represent the resistance of your machine, as well as use them to attach weapons and shields to your metalcorps and execute metalsofwares.

Description from the publisher:
METALCORPS es un juego de cartas y dados.

Los jugadores se convierten en pilotos de metalcorps ("mech" o "mazinguer z"), y deberán luchar por el control del mundo.
Cada Metalcorps ha sido creado por corporaciones de diferentes partes del mundo; USA, JAPON, EUROPA,..

Pero ser piloto no será fácil, pues tu metalcorps tendrá 35 niveles de energía que representa la resistencia de tu máquina, además de usarlos para acoplar armas y escudos a tu metalcorps y ejecutar metalsofwares.

boardgame: Railway Rivals Map II: Illinois and Indiana
description:
An expansion map for Railway Rivals and Dampfross. It has been sold at various conventions.

The map covers the states of Illinois and Indiana.

Ёжик-Потеряшка ("Lost Hedgehog") is a speed matching game. Five cards form your reference: a white horse, green frog, brown bear, yellow owl, and blue elephant. Then, you will draw a card from a 40-card deck. All the cards in the deck have two of these animals of different colors on them. Your job is to find which animal or color is not represented on the card at all, and slap that reference card. You then claim the card that was flipped over. If there is an exact match (i.e. a green frog), disregard the other rule and slap that exact reference card. You then claim the card that was flipped over.

Play proceeds until all the cards in the deck have been claimed -- most cards is the winner!

Description from the designer:

Payroll is a worker placement, resource management game that will test your ability to build a successful business. Compete with up to four players to win the public's approval and have the best reputation in the industry.

Build your company, hire employees, and delegate how they spend their time. Utilize their specific skills and find out who they work best with. Make your company more profitable as you continue to expand. Your choices will affect your company’s reputation and decide the outcome of the game!

Throughout the game you will be competing for actions on the main desk. You will have to balance how much time and money you invest to expand your business and increase your reputation. If you expand too quickly, you may not be able to pay your employees. If you focus too much on quick money, you may not reach your full potential. Finding the balance, knowing your competition, and a little luck will be the key to building your business empire!

Assailed for centuries by enemies on every side, House Liao’s Capellan Confederation faced its own oblivion time and again. Now, nearly forty years since the realm teetered on the very brink of collapse, the Confederation has returned, reclaiming the pride and unity lost when the armies of Steiner and Davion rolled across their worlds to shatter them. Led by the crafty and charismatic Sun-Tzu Liao, and embracing the spirit of Xin Sheng—rebirth—House Liao once again has become a political and military power to be reckoned with, defying those who would see them fail, and deadly to all who oppose them.

Handbook: House Liao compiles the history, politics, and cultures of the Inner Sphere’s smallest—and perhaps least understood—Great House. This sourcebook also includes game rules for use with Total Warfare, Tactical Operations and A Time of War: The BattleTech RPG, including new support vehicles, equipment, creatures, and more!

Handbook: House Liao continues the faction-based Handbook series that updates the political, economic, and historical details of the major realms in the BattleTech universe.

Era:    Civil War Era
Universe Date    3067
Timeline    2086 - 3067

Starship Awesome 3000 is a board game inspired by old-school space-flight sim games with tactical maneuvering, weapons systems that are customized during game play with card combinations, and a novel dice mechanic for resource management.

In the default scenario, players are initially competing to destroy the most Drones on the board, until one player is potentially revealed to be the "Rogue." At that point, the Rogue can winning by destroying any other Player, but any other Player can win by destroying the Rogue.

Players can also choose to play a simple dog-fight, where ships battle until one is destroyed.

You're all that stands against a legislative agenda that promises to up-end Democracy as you know it...

Work together and help each other use the most powerful tool you have to block upcoming policy votes - the Filibuster!

Filibuster is a semi-cooperative game for 3-5 players acting as Senators in the minority trying to stop a series of policies from passing by utilizing the Filibuster. Major game play mechanics include hand management, press your luck, voting and trading, negotiation, and take that!  The game is played over seven rounds, with each round consisting of an election phase to determine the group's leader, and a filibuster phase to block the policy revealed that round.

The leader is responsible for increasing the filibuster time to meet or exceed the minimum number of hours required to successfully block the policy. They also interact with the Time Deck, which is full of events that affect the players and will typically cause the end of the round. Non-leader players during a round act as supporting Senators that play assists and pass cards to the leader to help effectively block the policy and meet the Filibuster requirements for the round.

If at any time the players fail to block 4 policies, everyone loses! If all 7 rounds are completed and at least 4 policies have been successfully blocked, the Senator who earned the most influence (rewarded based on performance while leading a filibuster, as well as playing assists and passing cards) is the true winner!

So, be sure to work with your colleagues to prevent too many policies from passing through... but always look for ways to help yourself a little more than your fellow Senators so that you come out on top!

Imhotep: A New Dynasty adds five new places, fourteen market cards, seven god cards, four chariots, and 56 tiles to the Imhotep base game. God cards let players predict the progress of different buildings, with them being rewarded at the end of the game if they're correct and otherwise being punished.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Description from the designer:

Interstate is a quick-playing family game in which each player is taking a vacation or road trip through the United States via the Interstate road system.  The first player to visit all of their destinations and return home is the winner.  The game has a deck of Interstate cards which allows for interactive card play as players can help their own cause or hinder other players' travel.

Interstate is presented as an easy-to-learn basic game but also has several optional variants that can be used to spice up the basic game.  Because of the randomness and unpredictability inherent in the play of the game, as well as the variants, Interstate has excellent replay value.

Most games of Interstate are close; it is rare to have a runaway winner. The play of the cards helps to rein in anyone who gets too much of a lead over the other players.  There will always be unpleasant events that will hinder your game but basically the person who can most efficiently plan out the course of visiting their destinations will have the best chance of winning.

S-Tris is 2-player game that combines tile laying like Blokus with the use of cards.

Before play starts, players modify the usable part of the board by putting some blocks anywhere on the board. Each players is dealt a certain number of cards, depending on the amount of players.

On his turn a player plays a card, either a square or a helper card. In case of a square card he puts the corresponding piece on the board as long as it fits somewhere. In case of a helper card he carries out the special function. Some cards, like The Bomb, allow you to remove a piece first for instance.

The player who gets rid of his cards first or with the least points on his cards left after all players have passed on their turn, wins.

Level Up!, an expansion for the Bubblee Pop base game, brings two additional families of bubblees and two new powers into the game, giving you a great way to customize your games and «pop» more than ever!

Box Battles: Cold War Evolution is a game of tactical conflict between the forces of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) and the Warsaw Pact (WARPAC) during the escalating Cold War. Whether you are a historian, a devout fan of the military channel or simply fascinated by this game genre, you too can now be a miniatures collector with the convenience of terrain, play rules and playing aids, all in one box. Assembled for 1/285 scale miniatures games, included within are:
NATO Forces:
12 M113 Armored Personnel Carriers
12 Infantry Squads
1 HQ Platoon (6 vehicles)
5 M60A1 Tanks

WARPAC Forces:
10 BMP-1 IFV
10 Infantry Squads
2 Towed 120mm Mortars
4 T-62 Tanks
Game play terrain surface 24"x36"
Rule booklet
8 Vehicle Information Cards
12 Command Cards
3 Dice (d10, d12)
1 Tape Ruler, 2 ruler card playing aids
50 marker counters
2 Large buildings, 4 small buildings, 2 sandbag emplacements and 10 cubic inches of foliage terrain

A chess variant using cards. Basic two player chess rules board and pieces apply modified by the introduction of a common deck of cards.  The deck includes a range of alternative plays that players can elect to use rather than normal movement for that turn.  Examples are: move 3 pieces, move pawn like a rook, switch two of your opponent’s pieces, move bishop to any empty space etc.

The deck is placed beside the chessboard and four cards turned face up.  One of these forms the start of the discard pile, one is available for use by the black player, one for use by only the white player, and one (the free card) is available for use by both players.

On your turn you can move as normal or you can play either the free card or your colour card.  If you play a card it goes to the discard and is replaced by the top card of the deck.  If you make a normal move, you may cause the free card or your colour card to be discarded and summarily replaced by the top card of the deck.  
If the deck runs out, shuffle the discard and draw from it.

As in normal chess the winner is the player to checkmate his opponent’s King.

Play the classic mobile game "Snake" using dice & a pencil!  No old mobile phone or battery-life required!  Make the longest snake you can, picking up power-ups along the way to supercharge your snake.

Solo game:
You will use up to 6 custom 'snake' dice to determine how your snake moves around the 11*11 grid, eating power-ups that give you powers to manipulate your dice rolls, and even using one-time actions to portal through walls or tunnel under blockades!  But be careful - every time you gain a power-up, more blockades appear on the grid.  And one of the snake dice faces is a Blockade face that can't easily be manipulated and causes more blockades to appear!

How much of the board can you traverse and how many power-ups can you unlock before you inevitably crash?...

Multiplayer game:
You can now also play the game with 4 players, all manoeuvring their own snakes around the same shared board, vying for powerups and all trying to avoid the blockades AND each other!  Points are acquired similarly to the solo game, but you get additional bonuses for being the first to reach level 4 of each powerup track, and the last snake standing gains a bonus 10VP!  You can also try your luck on the larger 15*15 grid, giving more room to snake around (and a slightly longer game) - especially good with the max 4 players!

Description from the publisher:

War of the Cross is a four to seven player diplomatic strategy game that tells the story of the last decade of a terrible war that raged across Théah for thirty years. You must muster all your skills to command one of seven Nations to victory in a game of shifting politics, dangerous alliances and tactical skill. Can you lead armies, navies and Heroes to win the day and bring an end to the bloodiest war in Théah’s history?

War of the Cross plays in turns consisting of several phases. Players go through the phases together until one Nation has enough Victory Points to end the War and declare victory, negotiating with other players, giving secret orders to units, then making alliances and revealing those orders to claim territory and win conflicts.

The Bloodstone is an evil and powerful relic that enchants any who possess it. We pick up our story with an interesting cast of characters from all over the realm. They all have very different stories about how they became entrapped in the arena. Nightly, they fight to the death only to be resurrected and made to do it all over again at the sorceress' whim.

In Bloodstone, you control one of the characters and use a custom dice mechanism to determine your attacks and movements, using your miniature to move around the arena and engage in combat. You hope someday to escape this damned place, but tonight...you fight!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Sabres and Smoke: The War of 1812
description:
Description from the publisher:

Sabres and Smoke: The War of 1812 is a 2-player, turn-based light strategy board game set during the War of 1812. From Trenton in the Michigan Territory, to Fort George and Queenston Heights in Upper Canada, to the shores of Baltimore, Maryland, players will be able to pit British and Canadian infantry, militia, artillery, Native Warriors and naval units against their American counterparts. Across 16 exciting, historically accurate scenarios, both players will determine the fate of North America, with a game that is simple to both set up and learn, yet provides an incredibly rich, entertaining gaming experience. The game is for ages 10 and up.

Created by Eric Sheppard, the game is the result of extensive research that included explorations of several of the battle fields featured in the game. Additional scenarios to augment the 16 included in the base game will be forthcoming later in the year.

The game includes:

- 3 fully-mounted game boards (2 land, 1 coastal).

- 44 double-sided terrain hexes, illustrated to provide depth and dimension to the battlefield.

- 250 military units, including Native Warriors British, Canadian and American infantry, militia, artillery, naval units, and specialized unit markers.

- 100 card stands, allowing all units to ‘stand to’. No need to move stacks around the board.

- 8 battle dice (4 red, 4 blue) with custom-printed icon stickers.

- 32-page Rule/Scenario Book with all the information to set and play the game, quickly and easily. Book also includes 16 scenarios with full-colour set up maps.

- 52 Battle Cards, to add richness and variability to the gameplay.

Publisher's summary
再会の約束 (Saikai no Yakusoku) in English would mean 'Promise of Reunion'. The game is #3 of the five games in the Ars Combinatoria system, and takes up to three years to play.

Players are given one card each, and write down the same time and place they will meet next time. Then seal a tally impression between each card and its neighboring cards.

At the specified time, all players who have come to the specified place wins the game. But they taste a better victory if opponents don't come to the place.

Any number of players can play this game as long as there are enough cards.

プレイヤーは次に会う時間と場所をカードに記入し、割印をします。
その時間と場所に来ることのできた人が勝ち。
しかし、相手が現れなければ勝利は苦いものとなります。
引き分けを楽しむゲームです。
カードがあれば、同時に何人でもプレイできます。

Publisher's summary
幸せな時間 (Shiawase na Jikan) in English is 'Time of Happiness'. This is game #2 in the Ars Combinatoria system.

Each player has a deck of six cards which are "Good thing in your past", "Very good thing in your past", "Good thing in your present", "Very good thing in your present", "Good thing in your future", and "Very good thing in your future".

Players are given a theme, think about their past that relates to the theme, and chooses "Good" or "Very good" thing in the past card according to what they have thought. Do the same for their present and future.

During the game, your opponents asks several questions about what you thought, then guesses the cards you chose. Opponents win the game if they guessed your cards correctly.

Any number of players can play this game as long as there are enough decks of cards.

各自6枚のカードを持ちます。
カードには「過去のいいこと」「過去のとてもいいこと」
「現在のいいこと」「現在のとてもいいこと」
「未来のいいこと」「未来のとてもいいこと」と書かれています。
回答者が、過去・現在・未来の3つのタームでどちらのカードを選んだか
他の人はそれを当てることができれば勝ちです。
勝敗よりも会話を楽しむためのゲームです。
カードがあれば何人でも同時にプレイできます。
"This copies the other Twist(s). If that wouldn't change anything, replace the other Twist on the game board."
"After any players Research, each player with any cards in hand simultaneously passes the card left to the next such player. (Players with no cards in hand are skipped.)"
"After playing an INVENTION, Gain 1 per card in your hand."

A dice and card game of civilization building for 2-4+ players.

Each player has a set of 8 six-sided dice and share 6 common decks of cards:
-    The Leader, Wonder, Culture and Resources decks each have cards with a Victory Points (VP) value between 1 and 25, but may score additional VP in combination with other cards (from the same or different decks) or have other special abilities.  
-     Disaster Deck.  These cards have a VP value between -1 and -25 and have negative effects such as losing leaders or wonders, starvation or being attacked by barbarians.  Players should do their best to avoid acquiring disasters. 
-     Conquest Deck.  These cards have a VP value between 1 and 25 but no special abilities.

Each turn is divided into phases:
1.    All players roll their dice simultaneously. 
2.    Players may re-roll some, all, or none of their dice. 
3.    Players may a second time re-roll some, all, or none of their dice. 
4.    The player who rolled the most ones gets the top Leader card. 
5.    The player who rolled the most twos gets the top Wonder card. 
6.    The player who rolled the most threes gets the top Conquest card. 
7.    The player who rolled the most fours gets the top Resource card. 
8.    The player who rolled the most fives gets the top Culture card. 
9.    The player who rolled the most sixes gets the top Disaster card. 
If there is a tie in any category, the tied players roll one die each. Highest roll wins the tiebreaker.
Players put the cards they have won in front of themselves, visible to all.

The game ends after 20 turns. Each card has a Victory Point (VP) value.  Each player adds up the points of all their cards to get a VP total. The player with the most VP at the end of the game wins.

A card and dice game for 2-5 players.  Each player controls 1 town in Alaska and develops his town in order to score the most points.

Players share a common deck comprising resource (investment) cards and event cards.  The deck comes in three parts.  All the “early” cards are shuffled to make the top third of the decks. All the “late” cards are shuffled to make the bottom third of the deck.

There are 13 resource card types:  Indians, Mines, Fish, Wood, Ships, Ground, Air, Government, Military, Oil, Agriculture, Service, and Tourism.  Resource cards have an associated cost, score and revenue value.

Each player starts with 2 dollars.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 2 phases:

1.   Revenue Phase
-    Gain 1 dollar for free. 
-    Roll 1D6 or 2D6.  Gain revenue from every one of your resources whose score matches your roll. 
-    If your roll did not match any of your cards gain a second dollar for free.  
-    Dollars normally come from the Bank. If, however a resource is marked “ticket”, then 1 dollar comes from an opponent instead of the Bank.

2.   Investment Phase
-    The top 3 cards of the deck are turned face up. 
-    You may buy one card for its face value. 
-    You may buy a second card for double its cost. 
-    You may buy the third card for triple its cost. 
-    You may buy a fourth card for quadruple the cost and so on. 
-    Cards not bought are set aside and can be bought later by any player on their turn in addition to and/or instead of the top 3 that are always revealed.  
-    If the card is an event card it affects the player who draws them. Event cards, once drawn and played, are removed from the game.  If an event card allows a free buy, it must be of a card set aside or drawn this turn; if there are no candidates, it has no effect.

The game ends when there are no more cards left to draw.  All players in order may ten spend the last of their dollars. Scoring is undertaken as follows:
-    Score 1 VP for every card you own.
-    Score 2 VP for every Government and Military Card.  
-    Score 3 VP for every Oil and Tourism Card. 
-    Score 2 VP if you have the most cards of a type (check all 13 types)
The player with the most VP at the end of the game wins.

An abstract skirmish level squad combat board & card game for 2 players, based on the X-Com video games. One player plays X-Com units, the other the Aliens.

Use a large hex-map as the playing area. Every hex has 2 ratings: terrain and elevation.  These affect defence ratings and line of sight. Terrain is selected at the discretion of the players.

Players use figures, miniatures or chits to represent units. Each unit represents a single soldier or alien. 
-    X-Com units come in a variety of types: civilian, military, squaddie, specialist, officer, hero or SHIV robot.  Each has a hit value between 1 and 4, cost between 1 and 4, and can, through card play, undertake actions from one or more tables according to their type.
-    Alien units come in 15 different types.  Each has a hit value between 1 and 5, a cost between 1 and 5 and can, through card play, undertake actions from a table that is unique to their type

Players share a common action deck.  Cards are of a number of type: Move, Attack, Defence or Special.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 3 Phases:

1.   Tactical Phase
-    Draw 5 cards (or 4 if you only have 2 units left or 3 if you only have 1 unit left)

2.   Move and Fire Phase
-    Your units may individually move and fire in either order as you decide.  All of your units get one free move of 1 hex. In addition to the free move, you can play one move card on one unit (per turn) to move it again a number of spaces as stated on the card.  Normally only one unit is allowed per space.  Exceptions include units being carried such as a soldier carrying a wounded comrade or a captured alien or flying units passing over or hovering over other units
-    Play an attack card to have a unit attack.  The attack card has a number which is the range of the attack.  All attacks do a base of 1 hit of damage. A unit reduced to 0 hits is killed. 
-    Your opponent may play defence cards to negate damage.  A defence effect negates a base of 1 hit of damage.

3.   End Phase
-    Max hand size = 5 cards (4 if you only have 2 units, or 3 if you only have one unit left).  Discard excess cards.

The winner of the game is the first to achieve complete destruction of all opposing units.

Background:
War started in the year 2200, it was inevitable... metal combined with technology had turned into humanity’s worst enemy.
In the present day some cities still survive, protected by huge metallic walls, labyrinths and gates to prepare a first line of defense before any threats reach the inner parts of the city.
Build and pilot your Mech armor, it’s the only thing you can really trust to stay alive when venturing into the dangerous wasteland.
Money is worthless nowadays, but search the wasteland for scrap metal, cables, and electronics, you may even find weapons and lost tech from the Mech Wars. Whatever you find in the wasteland can be used to upgrade your weapons and armor, or as currency in the cities.
Pirates, mercenaries and psychos attack the cities in an attempt to conquer them, to loot and steal as much scrap metal and technology as possibly can.
Intro:
The Last Mechs is a cooperative exploration game for 1 to 4 players, a dungeon crawler set in a post-apocalyptic future. Includes a campaign mode that will guide you through the adventure with a comic book illustrated by David Daza. 
Aside from the campaign, every hero will have their own short comic that narrates their personal stories, which you can unveil by searching for clues in the cities.
With highly detailed pre-assembled miniatures ready to play.
You can choose from 4 different heroes: Paul “The Legend” a former army man turned mercenary, Karl the mechanic with his D-Sherpa droid always by his side, Lorna and her large modified cargo Mech, and Gork the smuggler.
The Last Mechs includes some RPG elements, since the heroes attributes include Mech piloting, repairing, exploring, and communications, and they can learn new skills by spending experience points.
There are different phases in TLM:
A travel phase, when one of the heroes must guide the group while trying to avoid the grave dangers that plague the wasteland.
A search phase when you may look for scrap, electronics and cables (the most valued resources), or even find standard or lost tech weapons and armor that you can repair. Additionally, different adventures and events unrelated to the campaign may trigger during your journey.
By the end of the travel phase, if you haven’t reached a city, you must set camp on the dangerous wasteland and take shifts to guard the camp.
During the guard phase, the heroes will decide how to split the guard rounds and will solve events in the dark of the wasteland. Also, the mechanic can repair the Mechs and the droid can build ammunitions.
Your party will earn fame the longer you stay in the wasteland, which will give you benefits in the cities, but the danger levels will increase the longer you venture into the wasteland, making it harder for your team to survive.
The few cities that still stand have metallic labyrinths that protect them from enemies, which you must pass through to access the cities.
In the cities you will have access to: (depending on the city)
1- Canteen: Find clues about your story and rumors.
2- Doctor: Heal your heroes.
3- Mech garage: Repair and upgrade your Mechs and equipment.
4- Tattoo parlor: To get temporary boosts for your skills.
5-Supplies: Purchase weapons and ammo.

https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/juegorama/last-mechs

A game for 2-4+ players with a restaurant building theme.

An 8x8 grid represents a city.  There is a set of 64 counters to be placed on the spaces of the board representing 16 districts (of 6 different types) and 48 restaurants (6 each of 8 different types).  A space on the board may only ever contain 1 district or 1 restaurant.

To start the 16 districts are distributed to the board such that each row gets 2 random districts randomly placed. All restaurant counters are in a pile called the build pile which is shuffled and placed face down next to the board.

Each player gets a set of 20 tokens, 10 coins, 4 random restaurant counters and 4 cards from a common card deck. Cards are of three types: Number, Income and Improvement.

Players take turns with each turn comprising has 6 phases:

1.   Investment Phase
-    The active player receives 1 Coin. 
-    If you have the most restaurants of a given type (for each type) get one extra coin.

2.   Recruit Phase
-    The active player draws 1 card and puts it in his hand.

3.   Build Phase
-    You may build one restaurant. This costs 10 coins and the restaurant must come from your restaurant pool. 
-    When a restaurant is built, draw a replacement counter from the build pile. 
-    Place the restaurant counter on an open space on the board with one of your tokens on top of it.  When building a restaurant, you must also discard a number card and the restaurant must be placed on a row or column matching that number. 
-    You may not build a restaurant adjacent to another of the same type.

4.   Improvement Phase
-    You may play one Improvement card. This costs 5 Coins. 
-    Place improvements face up on the table in front of you. 
-    You can have a maximum of 5 improvements.

5.   Income Phase
-    You must either play an income card or initiate a random income sequence. 
-    When playing a restaurant income card all restaurants of that type earn income. 
They gain 1 coin for every adjacent (orthogonal or diagonal) district.  When playing a district income card all restaurants adjacent to that type of district earn 1 coin each. 
All players earn income, not just the active player. 
-   To initiate a random income sequence roll 2D8 to get a random space on the board. If that space is a restaurant, that restaurant earns income for each adjacent district. If that space is a district, each adjacent restaurant earns 1 coin. 
-   If you have the only restaurants of a given type on the board they gain 1 star.  All restaurants start with a base rating of 1 star.  Certain Improvements and bonuses increase the number of stars. When a restaurant receives income multiply by its star rating to get the number of coins it earns.

End Phase
Discard cards in your hand in excess of 4.

The game ends immediately when one of the following happens: 
-    Any player runs out of tokens to use.
-    All restaurants of a single type are built.
-    Players go through the deck twice. 
The player with the most coins at the end of the game wins.

Description from the Included Instructions:

Object of the Game
Be the first player to get rid of all of your cards in each round and score points for the cards your opponents are left holding. Points in rounds accumulate and the first player to reach 500 points wins.

Crash-Up Rule — Accidents happen! If you pull this card from the draw pile — CRASH! IMMEDIATELY combine the cards in your hand with the cards from the hand of the next player in order. Shuffle the cards well, and then deal them back between the two of you starting with the other player. Put the Crash Up Rule card on the discard pile, and since it's a wild card, pick a new color that resumes play. If this card is turned up at the beginning of play, the person to the left of the dealer chooses the color that begins play.

Contents
112 Cards as follows: 
19 Blue cards - 0 to 9 
19 Green cards - 0 to 9 
19 Red cards - 0 to 9 
19 Yellow cards - 0 to 9 
8 Draw Two cards - 2 each in blue, green, red and yellow 
8 Reverse cards - 2 each in blue, green, red and yellow 
8 Skip cards - 2 each in blue, green, red and yellow 
4 Wild cards 
4 Wild Draw Four cards 
4 Crash-Up Rule Cards

Description from the publisher: (inconsistent with cards provided in the pack and the included instructions)

It's the matching game you love, now featuring your favorite iconic characters from Cars 3! Players race to get rid of all their cards by matching a card in their hand with the current card shown on top of the deck. There are two customizable cards and a special new wild card: the Smokey rule card which lets you look "under the hood" of any other player! Look at their cards, combine them with yours, and then deal them back to yourself and the other player! Score points by being the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First player or team to 500 wins. When you're down to one card, don't forget to yell "UNO!"

description:
Vegas Wits & Wagers, an expansion to Wits & Wagers Party, has a huge playmat that takes over the room at parties and large gatherings.  The additional betting sections are designed to create fun banter and bring the high-rolling spirit of Vegas into your living room.

The playmat is double-sided. One side has the original Wits & Wagers mat, allowing you to play the original Wits & Wagers with your Wits & Wagers Party edition.  The other side has 3 additional sections:

1) Players can do low risk bets on "Red" or "Black".
2) Players can do high-risk bets on specific players.
3) There is a section which keeps track of the question # and allows for increasing payouts to the player whose guess is closest each round.

The Vegas Wits & Wagers expansion contains a huge double sided playmat made of neoprene, 2 neoprene mats to close out betting options for fewer players, 7 answer boards, an additional dry-erase pen, some extra poker chip tokens, and a rules booklet.

Description from the publisher:

A Card Crafting Game of Nature’s Power! The campaign to restore Gaia's realm rages on and other druidic clans combating the curse have joined the ranks. And it’s not just druids who have heeded the call; other creatures have joined the fight, eager for a day when all are free from the accursed blight.

The Mana Storm expansion adds new advancement and vale cards to the base Mystic Vale game, giving players more card crafting options and exciting new possibilities for combos. New leader cards grant abilities that can be upgraded to become even more powerful! Amulets feature unique effects and greater variety over regular mana tokens.

Evil extends its reach and spills into the Streets of Arkham! Players get hours of exciting gameplay filled with horror and hardships that incorporate the core game and build upon the Arkham Horror canon.

From the campus of Miskatonic University to the gang-run speakeasies and storefronts, investigators face threats on both the supernatural and worldly fronts. Only with their wits, grit, and the occasional opportunity to unlock hidden potential can they overcome the threats to the city- and their lives.

This expansion unlocks three new digital scenarios, new digital mythos events, and a new digital puzzle. It introduces a new type of card, Elixirs, and a new type of token, Improvements. The expansion also includes new tiles, monsters, investigators, and new cards to further expand your investigations.

boardgame: Rudolph the Red Nosed Reindeer: Christmas Journey
description:
You've been chosen by Santa to help Rudolph, Hermey the Elf, and Yukon Cornelius find all the lost Misfit Toys. Christmas can't start until this courageous trio returns all the toys to the North Pole, so they can be delivered to a lucky boy or girl. Gather your friends as you all journey through an icy winter wonderland on your quest to the Island of Misfit Toys and back to ol' Saint Nick's workshop. Who will be the first to help Santa?.

Players take turns rolling dice to move around the game board, playing out actions such as sliding up or down the North Pole, picking up a Rudolph & Hermey card, swapping character placement, or entering into a Reindeer Games competition. In order to determine the winner, the first 2 players to reach the finish square on the game board compete in a final Reindeer Games competition. This competition will be best 2 out of 3, with each player taking turns rolling the die, highest roll wins the round.

Game includes game board, 4 character pieces, 48 Rudolph cards, one 8-sided die, and rules.
For 2-4 players, ages 8+.

We wanted to create a game that kids could play but had hidden depth that would keep adults engaged.  The result was an an easy-to-learn but surprisingly strategic card game that is a cross between Uno, War, and with some Texas Holdem sprinkled in.

Players take turns drawing and playing cards to win battles (and cards).  Some special action cards allow players to steal cards, change their character, void other player's cards and more.

During battle everyone plays one more "reinforcement" card just for themselves except for Holograms who play two. Monsters count claws, Robots count lasers, and holograms count monster, claws, or lasers (whichever has fewer in the middle).

Add up your totals and the highest number wins. The battle winner takes all the cards in the middle and their reinforcement card and sets them aside. This is their point pile and counts toward winning the game. The battle losers keep their reinforcement card.

Keep playing until someone runs out of cards and whomever has the most cards wins the game

The Way of Kings is an epic miniatures based game designed by Matt Riddle and Ben Pinchback under license from Brandon Sanderson, the author of The Way of Kings novels.

The game will be a Euro/American hybrid. 60-120 min big box game. 2-4 Players, with custom dice and minis, custom player decks, player mats, etc.

Minis included will be: Dalinar, Sadeas, Sebarial, Roion, Adolin, Generic Shardbearer, Parshendi, Parshendi Shard Bearer and oversized Chasm Fiend

Description from the designer:

A game for quick-thinking, problem-solving, profit-seeking creative types... and YOU!

When it’s your turn, you play the role of a rich and crazy investor. And even crazy investors know that the way to make money is to identify a problem and solve it. The good news is that you have been hearing a lot lately about a problem that needs solving. The bad news is that you have no idea how to solve it. That is why you have gathered a think tank of innovators together (yes, these are the other players).

Choose one card from each pile to reveal the problem. Now that everyone knows what the problem is, it is up to the innovators to figure out why it exists and how to fix it.

In two minutes or less, each Innovator must draw a prototype for a new, creative product to solve the problem – without using words. When time is up, they will give you the prototypes to review.

Choose two that you would like to learn more about. The Innovators that drew them get a chance to “pitch” their product to you.  After hearing the pitches, choose one product to invest in. The Innovator who came up with the product wins the round. Hooray! Give them in investment chip so they can keep track of how many rounds they win – because it matters.

Now, it is the player to your left’s turn to the be the Investor and you are the Innovator. And so on. The person who wins has the most investment tokens when you’re tired of playing is the Innovator of the Year. Yes, they should totally put that on their resume!

This Gold Mining themed game is a great learning tool for young players and it can be a great family game.  The primary objective is to roll out and rid your hand of cards before your opponents and collect gold and WIN.  As you collect the gold tokens you will collect Prospector level badges.

Ways to win: Reach the Expert Level Badge-Rookie, Gold Finger, Elite and Expert
             Most gold collected after 5 rounds.

Each player will be given a determined number of gold nuggets depending on number or players.  4 players 25 nuggets each.  Game play starts with each player in the game will 'dig' or ante a gold nugget and place it in the middle of the table.  This gold becomes the pot.  The players will be given a three card hand and they will be given the option of rolling one di or two dice to roll out their hand of matching card and dice values.  EX 1=1, 7=7 11=11.

There are some features such as 'THE GOLDEN ROLL' If a player rolls two sixes they will automatically win the pot.  Another feature is if a player rolls'FOOL'S GOLD' which is two ones they are automatically out of that hand.

Each round begins with the players deciding if they are 'digging' ante in or folding.

The player with the most gold or the player who reaches Expert Prospector WINS. Prospector Levels are determined by number of players playing.  Happy Rolling!

You are an item soul master. You take responsible for the sale of old goods, but in addition, you also accept the commission of others, through the information provided by customers to gradually repair the relationship damage between the owner and the items.
Occasionally there will be an old thing to come to your shop; these old things will be the identity of the soul in your shop, for your shop to bring a different scene.

A card game for 2-4 players in which players are Athenian generals (Strategos) in the 5th century accumulating influence and prestige.

Players share a common set of cards of 8 different types:  Proposal (gain prestige one time), Events (multiple effects), Associates (gain prestige every turn), Prosecutions (remove associates & strategoi), Popularity (temporary influence gain), Reaction (multiple effects), Victory (gain influence) and Defeat (lose influence)

Each player starts the game controlling one leader. There are 16 different leaders available and each has a unique special ability.  If your leader is ever killed or removed from play, you must start over but keep any prestige already earned. Each player starts with 10 Influence and 10 Prestige.

Each round has 6 phases:

1.   Enemy Phase
-    Roll 1D6: if even Persia is the enemy; if odd Sparta is the enemy this round.

2.   Availability Phase
-    Flip over the top 5 + X cards of the deck where X is the number of players.  These are placed face up in the middle of the table. These cards are known as the available cards.

3.   Turn Order Phase
Players roll high 1D6 to determine turn order this round.

4.   Action Phase
-    Players take turns (in turn order) selecting one available card until all cards have been selected. 
--- If a player selects a Popularity or Reaction card, they put that card in their hand. These are retained until needed to influence votes, or  negate an event.
--- If a player selects an Associate, that associate becomes theirs and is placed face up in front of them. 
--- If a player picks a Proposal or a Prosecution then a vote is conducted immediately to see which player wins it. 
--- If a player picks an Event card resolve it immediately. 
--- If a player selects a Victory or Defeat, it becomes theirs and is placed face up in front of them.

5.   Prestige Phase
-    Associates earn prestige for their leaders.

6.   End Phase
-    Max hand size is 4 cards discard excess cards.

The game ends when there are no cards left in the deck. At the end of the game, the player with the most prestige points wins.

First decide who will begin as the Narrator. All other players blindly draw three white Story Cards. The Narrator draws a scarlet Starter Card and reads it aloud to begin the storyline.

Each player then submits a Story Card face-down. Shuffling before reading, the Narrator selects a favorite to continue the storyline and reads it aloud.

If your card is selected to continue the storyline, re-up to thee white Story Cards with the option of swapping out one or both of your remaining cards. If your card is not selected, tough s#*t, you'll have to make do with only your remaining cards.

Repeat this for (at least) one more round. The card selected to complete the storyline on the final round (providing its fantastic conclusion) earns the scarlet Starter Card as a point. Congratulations, you've climaxed!

Pass the Narrator role to the next player. All other players re-up to three new white Story Cards and continue.

The first player to have a threesome wins! Collect three points with scarlet Starter Cards, that is.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Go bravely for the masquerade, Cinderella. Board game special edition
description: Players play the main role of Cinderella, came to this gorgeous masquerade, in order to win the eyes of the crowd and won the dance prince or princess's honor, and start a dance challenge with other opponents!

Noble Armada is a tactical, miniatures based, space fleet battle game designed to deliver a game experience that balances deep tactics with cinematic action.

Players create fleets for one of the five Royal Houses from the Fading Suns setting and wage war using a ruleset that focuses on fleet command. Careful positioning of ships will enable a fleet to unleash devastating weapon barrages. Targeted attacks and flanking actions can sever lines of communication, hindering an opposing commander’s ability to issue orders to their fleet. Boarding actions allow you to capture opposing ships, and add their resources to your own fleet.

Format: 6" x 9", Spiral-bound Softcover, 288 B&W Pages

boardgame: The Contender: Heavy Lies the Crown Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

15 cards using real quotes from politicians on both sides of the aisle during President Donald Trump's first 100 days in office. Featuring cards inspired by Trump, Former FBI Director James Comey, Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, Vladimir Putin, and Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In a ruthless, dystopian future, agents from all walks of life toil in the shadows on behalf of the Corporations. These colossal entities wage an eternal covert war against one another for control of the megacities. One in particular, Tau City, is coveted above all others and it is here that death, intrigue, and scandals are just another part of the daily grind.

Take control of a group of undercover agents in Syndicate: Tau City, each with their own powers that can be combined into devastating combos, and deliver the city into the hands of your corporate masters in this game of hidden movement and area control.

Description from the publisher:

Augment The Contender with cards based on the wildest primary season in American political history. Fresh quotes from Bernie Sanders, Marco Rubio, Hillary Clinton, Jeb Bush, Martin O’Malley, Ben Carson, Ted Cruz and so much Donald Trump you’ll need to build a big beautiful wall to keep yourself away from it.

91 New Argument cards
9 New Topic cards
For a total of 100 new cards!

Made in the United States of America by patriots who give a damn!

boardgame: The Contender: Politically Incorrect Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Learn how to cuss like a real American with 50 new cards of political filth, straight from the apple pie holes of real presidential candidates.

This expansion deck contains 45 politically incorrect Argument cards cataloging gaffes, rants, and political no-nos from Richard Nixon, George W. Bush, Thomas Jefferson, Pat Buchanan, Bill Clinton, Ronald Regan, Lyndon B. Johnson, and many more. Plus five Topic cards: Adultery, Genocide, Abortion, Your Child, and Prostitution.

The Politically Incorrect expansion pack has blue face cards so that they can easily be identified and removed for politically correct game play.

boardgame: The Contender: Television & Movie President Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Quotes from famous television and movie presidents including Air Force One, West Wing, House of Cards, 24, Veep, Battlestar Galactica and Dr. Strangelove.

And one extra one from Dave, who wasn't a president but rather a willing patsy in a conspiracy to replace the president with a presidential impersonator.

11 Argument cards in total.

Description from the publisher:

14 card mini-expansion inspired by the smash hit musical and using real quotes from real historical figures themselves. Including Alexander Hamilton, George Washington, Aaron Burr, Angelica Schuyler, Marquis de Lafayette, and Thomas Jefferson.

BONUS! A card commemorating the Bowling Green Massacre as described by White House counsel to the president Kellyanne Conway.

boardgame: The Contender: Battle for Las Vegas Debate Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

From the dunes of the desert comes a showdown that will mercifully signal the final furlough of the 2016 election. This 12-card expansion will include quotes from Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton taken from their debate on October 19th in Las Vegas.

BONUS! Three cards added! Including two topic cards! Now 15 total including "I'll keep you in suspense", "Such a nasty woman" and "We have some bad hombres here."

boardgame: The Contender: "Battle for St. Louis" Debate Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

It's the televised argument so nice they did it twice! Actually, there will be three of them. But all of these cards will come from the rematch of the most watched debate of all time. Will Donald Trump sniff out a better performance? Can Hillary Clinton shimmy her way to victory?

Either way, all the memorable barbs, gaffes, and insults will be memorialized in this 12 card expansion! 11 argument cards and 1 topic card.

WARNING! Contains one extra EXPLICIT Card.

boardgame: The Contender: "Battle for New York" Debate Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

All of the best lines from the September 26th Presidential Debate between Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton. 12 cards including one topic card.

boardgame: The Contender: "All I Do Is Win" Victory Speech Mini Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Jimmy Carter, Richard Nixon, George W. Bush, Bill Clinton, George H.W. Bush, Ronald Reagan and more!

Includes a quote from President Elect Donald Trump comparing his future Chief of Staff to a horse. Because... well... #2106 I guess.

boardgame: Black Knight (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
A print and play scenario for T.I.M.E Stories:

The Black Knight satellite has been orbiting the Earth for years. Recent events and new information has convinced the government that it is time to bring it down for study. Safely tucked away at Edwards Air Force Base (more commonly known as Area 51), they try to unlock its secrets - but there are some problems ...

BEFORE YOU PRINT (a note from the designer):

This is a long scenario, 208 cards. I tried a smaller size to save ink and paper, but it didn't work with a couple different scenes.

After several conversations with other players, I would like to emphasize this point: There are several extremely difficult codes and puzzles. The puzzles are not technically needed to win, but solving them will help immensely. You will also miss part of the story if they aren't solved. If you are intent on solving them then plan on spending just as much time on the codes/puzzles as actually playing the game. Also, I have not offered any hints or clues on the forums. This scenario was created to be hard.

You will need a device with a QR reader.

There is no time loop--it's do or die.

There is no combat. Challenges, yes; combat, no.

It is playable with 2, 3, or 4 players (it will even work solo, except for one card that is not totally necessary).

It is hard, but family-friendly in all other ways.

It is long, and there is no good stopping point. Be prepared to use the save option in the box, finish in one long session, or have a table where it can be left out.

Some duplex printers work better than others. You may want to try hand feeding paper for better alignment. A tedious job, but it often helps.

Please be sure your printer is set to 100% not "scale to fit." If the cards appear too small this is likely the problem.

boardgame: MaGCK: The Matrix Game Construction Kit
description: MaGCK contains everything needed to play and design Engle "matrix games" — free-form narrative games that encourage player discussion. The kit includes rules, hundreds of customizable tokens and stickers, disks (to mark status or conditions), isomorphic map tiles, various tracking mats (phases, turns, time, die roll modifiers), dice, and links to additional online resources. MaGCK also contains rules and briefing materials for two games: ISIS CRISIS (a conflict simulation of contemporary Iraq) and A RECKONING OF VULTURES (coup-plotting and political skullduggery in the fictional Republic of Matrixia). Matrix games are already used within the national security and foreign policy communities for training and analysis. With MaGCK the user can develop their own games on a wide range of topics, whether for professional use or hobby gaming.

boardgame: Imperium Chronicles: Grunts and Grav Tanks – Mech Assault
description:
Description from the publisher:

Grunts and Grav Tanks: Mech Assault (G&GT:MA) is the first major expansion for the Grunts and Grav Tanks tactical wargame. It includes a brand new faction called the Talion Republic (allied to the Magna Supremacy) and new units (mechs and attack craft). The expansion also includes a completely new battle map (30" x 22.5") that can be used alone or in conjunction with the map from the original game.

Features

     A new faction (Talion Republic)
     New units including mechs and attack craft
     A new full-color map (30" x 22.5”, divided into 9 sections)
     500+ unit counters (full-strength & damaged sides) & markers (minefields, bridges, and craters)
     Six new scenarios including a grand campaign using the new battle map and the one from the original game.
     Straight-forward rules for fast gameplay

A game for 2-4 players about the modes of death and character types in the plays of Shakespeare.

There are two decks of cards:
-     Character Deck.  These represent the characters within the play.  Each character card has a special ability which are generally activated by die rolls.
-     Death Deck.  These represents the methods by which characters die within the play.  Each death card has a rating of 1 to 6.

To start roll 1D6 +3. This will give a random number between 4 and 9.  This is the number of characters who will die before the play is over.

Each player is dealt 5 random character cards. These are kept face up on the table in front of the player.

Players take turns with each turn having 3 acts:

1.   First Act: 
-    Draw 2 death cards. You max hand size is 5 cards. Discard excess cards.

2.   Second Act: 
-    Place 1 death card on an opponent’s character card. A character may have any number of death cards stacked on him.

3.   Third Act: 
-    Roll 1D6. This is the Death Die. If the rolled number matches the number on any death card on an opposing character, then the character is killed. If there are multiple characters that can be killed, pick just one. A killed character and any death cards on it are discarded. 
-    The death roll will often activate the special abilities of one or more characters. The attacked player may respond first with the reaction of one of his characters. Play of reactions of other characters proceeds in a clockwise direction.

The game ends when the pre-determined number of deaths is reached. The winner is the player having the most living characters left at the end of the game.

A card game for 2-4+ players with a Tarzan theme. The game involves playing cards to complete the storyline for an adventure involving Tarzan.  The form of the adventure is described as a Tarzantra and has the form:

My name is Tarzan, (Title)
I have great (Attribute)
And am skilled at (Ability)
I like to wrestle (Animal)
Me and my friend (Ally)
Journeyed to (Locale)
Where with (Object)
We executed a daring (Action) 
And defeated the (Foe)

Players share a common deck.  The deck has 9 suits of 9 cards each: Title, Attribute, Ability, Animal, Ally, Locale, Object, Action, and Foe.  Players are dealt 5 cards each at the start of the game with the remaining cards being spread out face down in the middle of the table.

In a sing-song fashion the leader says the first line of the Tarzantra.  The first player to his left gets to play a Title card from his hand if he has one.  If he does, then he keeps the card face up in front of him in his personal victory pile and he draws a face down card and puts it into his hand.  (He must also say the name of the card to complete the line of the poem)

If he does not have a Title card, he must look at one face down card on the table. He may keep it, or leave it and put a different face down card into his hand.  If he had to draw a card, the next player to his left gets a chance to play a Title card to complete the line. This continues until someone plays a Title card.

Once someone does, the leader speaks the second line of the Tarzantra. The player who played the Title card gets the first chance to finish the second line with an Attribute card. This continues line by line, until the full Tarzantra is completed.

When the Tarzantra is complete, the player to the leader’s right becomes the new leader and starts a new Tarzantra.

The game ends when 5 Tarzantras have been completed (or 3 for a short game, or 7 for a long game).  The player with the most cards in their victory pile at game end wins.

The Devil’s Beach depicts the invasion of Omaha Beach and the fighting across the Normandy countryside on June 6, 1944. Player’s control the American and German forces that fought in the battle, in a bid to uphold history, or to change it.

UPDATE:

This game has been redeveloped by the new LnLP staff improving the counters, maps, rules, and scenarios in the game.

If you have purchased Omaha: The Battle for the Beach at the current LnLP Store, we will provide you a FREE Print and Play edition that you can be download from your order history from the LnLP Store.

Print On Demand Edition: The Print On Demand Edition includes full color printed, manual, player aid cards, larger maps, and counters printed on Sticker sheets and chit board to mount them on.  Once you received your game you will be all ready to play. The Print and Play edition is included for free with our Print On Demand Edition.

Print and Play Edition: The Print and Play Edition is available as a downloadable Print and Play files that can be printed in both US Letter and A4 paper sizes. Our Print and Play Edition includes individual files for the manual, player aid cards, counters and smaller multi-part maps for home printers and larger map files that can be printed at a local print shop. You may select to purchase only Print and Play Edition and reduce the price of the game under the Available Options on the product page.

description:
No Honor in Surrender is a squad-level tactical game simulating skirmishes in several critical battles fought between the US and Japanese military forces in the South Pacific in WWII. Players control squads of Japanese and American forces that fought in these battles.  The counters represent single characters (Leaders), Squads of men, Weapon Teams, Support Weapons, tanks, and planes as well as machine guns, flamethrowers, tanks, and planes.

Complexity: 5 out of 10

Solitaire: 7 out of 10

Ages: 12+

Players: 1-2

Time: 30-60 Minutes

boardgame: Against All Odds: Battle for the Golan Heights
description:
Against the Odds: Battle for the Golan Heights is a game simulating the Syrian attack on the Golan Heights during the opening stages of the Yom Kippur War, between October 6-9, 1973. The Israeli forces were extensively outnumbered in the opening thrust but in the end, they held their ground, blunting the Syrian thrust and forcing them to withdraw.  The withdrawal was a timely break for the Israeli’s, as unbeknownst to the Syrians, the Israeli defenses were on the brink of collapse. Against the Odds: Battle for the Golan Heights concentrates on a small segment of that desperate battle through both historic and variant scenarios.

Complexity: 4 out of 10

Solitaire: 7 out of 10

Ages: 12+

Players: 1-2

Time: 30-60 Minutes

A solitaire game for the decktet.

Kingdom is facing the biggest threat ever! Dark, ancient forces have returned and are unleashing their evil and terror causing destruction and mayhem. You are Jacynth's most famous adventurer given an important task - you must retrieve 6 pieces of an ancient artifact and bring it to the place where the ritual will be performed in order to destroy the source of this evil. Its minions will try to stop you along the way.

On your turn you roll five six-sided dice in order to defeat enemies or overcome dificult terrain ahead of you. Every turn there are three cards in your path that can be one of three types: enemy, location or event. When you roll the dice you can assign pairs of dice with total value that matches exactly the rank of the card in order to overcome it. Undefeated enemies deal you damage and so do the locations that are in your path more then one turn due to fatigue. Unresolved events make you lose resources that you can gain when defeating enemies. You can also spend your resources to travel easier through locations. When you make it through the whole deck (there are no more cards in your path) and you have one of each type of resource you win the game!

Exégèse is a game as well as a game generator based on interpretation mechanics.

It is made of 80 cards that contain images and symbols. They lead the players to create their very own games in a short time while playing against the game or against each other.
On your own or with friends, discover a new approach to playing and designing card games, board games and games in general.
While the cards tell if and how you play together against the game or against each other, use Sketch cards and Inspiration cards to find the game mechanics that suit you best!
Discuss with the other players and find out what the new game will look like this time.

A card game for 2-6 players based on the Syrian Civil War from 2011.

There are 6 factions, one of which is secretly assigned to each player, and an event deck of 37 card representing nations, organisations and activities which may have a positive or negative effect on one or more of the factions.

To start six faction cards (one for each faction) are placed face up in the middle of the table and 10 Victory Tokens (VT) placed on each card. Using a duplicate set of faction cards – one is secretly assigned to each player and remaining faction cards removed from play.

Players then take turns to draw 2 event cards from the top of the deck. One card is played and the other discarded.  Played events will cause some factions to gain VT and others to lose VT.  The played event is also discarded after its effects are taken.  Cards are discarded face down.

The game ends when the event deck has been run through twice. The player with the faction with the highest number of VT at the end of the game is the winner.

Welcome to King of Thieves, a non-collectible bluffing card game of tomb robbing in which competing thieves gamble their reputation to steal treasure from heavily guarded tombs!

Aim of the Game
In King of Thieves players represent enterprising tomb-robbers battling to be crowned king of all the thieves! Thieves gamble their reputation with every expedition into the dangerous tombs that litter the land of Al-Shirkuh, but this gamble is worth the risk as great riches can be found. However, every thief must be wary as every tomb has a guardian to protect its riches; either a dangerous creature, or a fiendish trap to snare the unwary!

How do you win?
The game ends when one player has managed to collect 10 Treasure tokens from plundering tombs. If all tombs have been plundered the game ends and the player with the most Treasure tokens wins. If there is a tie for Treasure tokens then the player with the most Treasure and Reputation wins the game.

Each player receives a mini board with 9 pictures, corresponding to 36 tiles. The tiles are placed faced down in a draw pile. Players take turns in drawing tiles, and the player who has the corresponding picture on his board recieves the tile. The player who first fills his mini board with tiles wins.

The game can also be played as a version of memory. The tiles are placed faced down on the table, and players take turn choosing tiles and turning them face-up. If a player chooses a tile with a picture corresponding to his board, he places it there and continues his turn. If the tile corresponds to a picture on another players board, he puts it back face-down and lets next player take his turn. The player who first fills his mini board wins.

During setup each player receives travel cards, corresponding to destinations in Sweden, and a pawn. Om his turn, the player moves 1-2 steps (on road or railway) or checks in for a flight. The goal is to travel from southest-northest Sweden (or vice versa), while visiting destinations in the travel cards. Om his way the player can also choose to visit destinations with souvenir cards. After visiting all destinations in his travel cards, the player moves to the final destination, where he is given three questions about Sweden.

After all players have finished, final points are calculated: Every travel card is worth 5 PTS, souvenir cards are 2 PTS each, and correct answers to the questions in the are 1 PTS each. The player with most points win.

boardgame: Arcane Academy: Bow of Spell Mastery
description:
A single card expansion for Arcane Academy it consists of a card titled: Bow of Spell Mastery.
Its ability is:
"Each time you complete a Theorem, gain 1 Shard".

This is a promotional pack of cards released for El Alamein. 
It contains 5 cards:
- P01 - Supply Officer,
- P03 - British Commanding Office,
- P05 - Brave Veteran
- P07 - Desert Rats
- P08 - Arriving At Tobruk

Description from the publisher:

A semi co-operative board game for two to five players, Ocean in the Sky tells the tale of a village in a wondrous post-apocalyptic world where the world is a barren desert because most of the waters in rivers, lakes and oceans has been sucked into the atmosphere and created a huge ocean that surrounds the world.

Now, great flying submarines traverse the skies and dive deep in the ocean in the sky, as the protagonists of the Village vye for power and prestige in their quest to expand and protect their communal holdings.

All the while, however, great mad gods come forth from this strange Ocean, spreading destruction and desolation, abominations that need to be stopped together.

The core of the game is its colorful Characters and their mighty Submarines, aided by Techs and other conquered Villages as they protect their homeland from the terrible Mad Gods. Amass Victory Points by defeating the Mad Gods as well as keeping the goals of your loyal Characters in mind.

A new set of rules to play the age-old game for two players, using a chess board and all of the chess pieces. Warlord chess is designed to be simple and easy to learn, but offering a strategic depth and its main goal is to be played with nothing but the typical chess set you can find everywhere.

Players use pawns as Farmers, creating patterns in order to bring forth more powerful units such as Knights, Bishops, and ultimately the King and Queen to win the game- but there's more than one way to win the game!

An advanced version of Warlord Chess, designed by Cyborg Centaur Games, Wizard Chess adds the element of a classic deck of cards. Like Warlord Chess, it is a new set of rules for playing chess for two players, using a chess set.

Players place pawns to act as Farmers, trying to form patterns which are used to recruit higher value pawns; but Bishops now act as Mages, and allow you to draw cards in your Hand of Magic. You can use the various combinations of cards like two pairs, three pairs etc. to cast mighty spells that alter the battlefield, adding a new depth to the rules of Warlord Chess.

—description from the publisher

The Game of the Sixty Second Screen play

With a deck of 53 illustrated movie cliches, Players will take turns pitching competing movies. A third player- the Producer, judges which pitch wins. That winner goes on to Produce the next round. And so forth, until the deck runs out or people need a nap. Whomever has the most winning pitch cards at the end of the night is the big winner. Or, whoever has the most fun.

One minute to plan it, and one minute to pitch it- Pitch, Please!

This game from cartoonist/humorist Carolyn Main is a improvisational party game, taking players to the movies- inside their own minds.

Pitch, Please! It's the big screen, in a lil' package.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Self-Replicating Robots Promo Card
description:
Self-Replicating Robots

Blue card

Cost: 7

Requirements: 2 Science tags

Action: Reveal and place a SPACE OR BUILDING card here from hand, and place 2 resources on it, OR double the resources on a card here

Effect: Cards may be played as if from hand with the cost (reduced by the number of resources on it)

Promo card 210
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Stratego Legends takes the familiar game play of Stratego and expands it into something new. In addition to ranks, all pieces now have powers as well. Some fly, some teleport. Some perform better in the marshes or in the mountains. Others have powerful death curses that provide long-lasting effects to the battle. There are over 200 different pieces in the universe, each with its special function.

Stratego Legends is an "optionally collectible" game.

Stratego Legends: Kralc's Horde is a booster pack containing 15 grey army playing pieces.

A card game for 2-4 players with a general Medieval historical theme.

Players share a common deck of 60 cards of 4 card types: Character, Holding, Group and Event.  Cards have a points value between 1 and 5 and 1 of 2 types of abilities (cards used in combination with each other or cards that initiate an event/activity).  Abilities activate as soon as the card is played.

Each player starts with 2 cards and then take turns to draw 1 card from the deck and play 1 card from their hand.  When a card is played it is placed face up on the table in front of the player.  If the card has a non-combo ability (e.g. discard 1 group, steal character, draw 1 card etc.) this gets used immediately. All the cards in front of you are known as your Fief.

When the last card is drawn the discard is shuffled and cards drawn from it (cards only ever go into the discard pile when another card sends them there).  When there is no deck or discard left, players continue taking turns, playing one card at a time from their hands until all cards are played.  At this point scoring is undertaken.

Cards score their basic points value but if they are combo cards may score extra points if the player holds the right combination of cards. The final scores are based on all the cards in your Fief and the combos they make. The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game.

A card game for 2-4 players with a Fantasy theme.

Players share a common deck of 60 cards of 5 card types: Character, Holding, Group, Event and Thing.  Some event cards are designated as Spells.  Cards have a points value between 1 and 4 and 1 of 2 types of abilities (cards used in combination with each other or cards that have a special ability).  Abilities activate as soon as the card is played.

Each player starts with 2 cards and then take turns to draw 1 card from the deck and play 1 card from their hand.  When a card is played it is placed face up on the table in front of the player.  If the card has a non-combo ability (e.g. discard 1 group, steal character, draw 1 card etc.) this gets used immediately. All the cards in front of you are known as your Fief.

When the last card is drawn the discard is shuffled and cards drawn from it (cards only ever go into the discard pile when another card sends them there).  When there is no deck or discard left, players continue taking turns, playing one card at a time from their hands until all cards are played.  At this point scoring is undertaken.

Cards score their basic points value but if they are combo cards may score extra points if the player holds the right combination of cards. The final scores are based on all the cards in your Fief and the combos they make. The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game.

Description from the publisher:

Cook, Serve, Survive! 
As a Chef, face a Zombie horde and make the best meals in order to bait the Zombies towards your opponent.

Feeding Zombies is a "cooking survival game" for 1 to 2 players ages 10 and up. It's a quick but challenging filler game offering 3 different game modes.

You play as a Chef facing a horde of Zombies invading your Kitchen, to survive this apocalypse you will have to gather every bit of food you can get your hands onto and throw it towards your opponent in order to attract the Zombies to her instead of you! Make special meals and unleash more effective attacks, arm yourself in case the Zombies get too close and choose carefully your play style.

How to play:

Each player starts with 5 random food cards, on your turn you may choose 2 of the following actions. 
Each action may be repeated, but you may not target the same Zombie card twice per turn:

1. Attract 1 Zombie by playing the required Food cards. Then refill the empty spot with a new card.
2. Trade 2 Food Cards for 1 Menu card from the display. Apply the Menu’s  effect or take the card into your hand for a later use.  
3. Play a Menu Card or a Knife. 
4. Discard any number of cards from your hand.

Then complete your hand by drawing Food cards as you must always finish your turn with a total of 5 cards in your hand.

When a Zombie reaches the fourth row of your opponent's side, she is bit. 
If you are playing the classical game mode you have won the game and your opponent is gently devoured!

boardgame: Zombicide: Green Horde Special Guest Box – Sean A. Murray
description:
This Special Guest box features characters based on the art of the boardgame Gateway: Uprising by CMON and Fish Wizard Games.

Includes miniatures and character/spawn cards for 3 survivors (Cardinal Birmbauer, Kendra, Tak "Spellbones" TrooBlood) and 1 necromancer (Grand Inquisitor Innsmouth).

Description from the designer:

Battle to grow your Cucumber in an action filled card game of strategy, betrayal and chaotic climaxes! Each copy of Cucumber Sandwich supports up to 4 players engaging in a frantic battle to grow their Cucumber Card to 12 inches first to win the round. Additional decks allow up to another 4 players to join in the frolics

Players are dealt 4 cards from the main deck and a Cucumber card to begin. On their turn they must draw and play a card. However, beware... players will be waiting to Fondle you senseless at every opportunity! Not to mention the impact of a Shrivel or the disappointment of a Dickochet.

Those of you that like to party whilst you game you'll be happy to know that we have included some extra rules for such occasions.

BOMBER COMMAND is a module in the battle over Britain game series, that adds multi-crew bombers to the system. This module does not contain rules, it only comes with game components (specifically, the Bomber Command counter sheet (32 planes), Dogfight Display, Reference Card, Battle over Britain counter sheet (32 planes), and 4-page folder)

SLAM is a tabletop simulation of the full-contact, high-flying sport of slamball. In SLAM, each minute of the game will feature an exchange and a highlight. The exchange streamlines back-and-forth play producing a baseline score for each team. The highlight then focuses in on the talents and play style of the individual players.

SLAM is primarily intended as a solitaire gaming experience, although it can be enjoyed by two, with each person coaching their own team. A single game should take about 20 minutes once you are familiar with the game.

This house is pretty haunted. Looks like you better start cleaning up the joint by getting rid of ghosts. But wait Aren't you a ghost? In that case, get these ghost hunters outta here! I don't
know, you all look the same to me.

Cause of Death: Ghost is a 4-9 player hidden role drafting card game. The object of the game is to have the most points by the end of the game. Points are earned at the end of each round by accomplishing the goal(s) of the character you drafted. The game ends after the final round. Play as many rounds as there are players

"The Executive Trivia Game". Game consists of six decks of cards, a game board, and four colored markers. The decks are shuffled together, then the players take turns answering questions from the category of their choice (people, places, or things). If the player answers correctly, they move the number of spaces (1-4) indicated at the top of the card. There the player must answer a question in the category dictated by the color of the square they have landed on. The same player continues until they answer incorrectly. Play proceeds clockwise.

Some squares are marked "Knockoff". If a player lands on one of these, they pick the category for their question. If they answer correctly, they may either go again, or "knockoff" an opponent by sending them back to start. The player knocked off now has a turn.

Players win by reaching the end square by exact count of spaces indicated by the card, and correctly answering a question in a category chosen by their opponent. If a player on the end square answers incorrectly, they remain there, and are eligible to be knocked-off by an opponent.

boardgame: Blank Marry Kill: Kickstarter Exclusive Cards

A dice and record keeping game for 2-4+ players with a Civilization building theme.

Each Civilization has 14 stats: Cities, Infrastructure, Ideas, Victory, Points, Population, Stability, Territories, Food, Resources, Culture, Military, Gold, Zeal and Wonders. These stats can go up and sometimes down with each player keeping track of the development of his civilization on a record sheet.

There are 13 common tracks, one of which (the Pre-History track) is used in the pre-game, the other 12 during the main game. Most tracks are 13 spaces long, including the start space, and circular in nature so that players can move their markers round them in a continuous loop. The 13 tracks are:
Pre-History Track (for pre-game only)
Knowledge Track
Military Track
Cultural Track
Agriculture Track
Industry Track
Building Track
Wonder Track
Religion Track
Government Track
Leader Track
Trade Track
Naval Track
There are also several charts which are referenced from various points on the tracks.

Players start the game with 5 points in Population, Territory, Food, Stability, Zeal, Military, and Culture. They also receive additional starting stats through the pre-game phase.

During the pre-game phase players place one pawn each on the start space of the Pre-History track which has 23 spaces (including the start).  Players take turns rolling the die and moving their pawns forward along the track. When a player lands on a space they get the indicated boosts to their stats. They also put a marker on the space. If a player lands on a space that already has a marker they move to any space of their choice. When every space on the Pre-History Track has been marked the pre-game ends.

During the main game turns are conducted over a series of 16 phases, with players taking turns within each phase.  During most phases each player will roll one die and move along the indicated track (i.e. during the leader phase, players move on the leader track). Players gets the stat bonuses as described in the space they land on. Certain phases allow players to increase population size in exchange for resources, trade with other players, or declare war on other players in an attempt to steal territory from them.

The game ends when anyone reaches 20 or more territories. If a player ever goes to zero territory or population he automatically loses.  At the end of the game, the player with the most Victory Points wins.

description:
Game of speed and strategie.
