description: Cacao: Diamante contains four expansion modules for the Cacao base game: The gem mines, the tree of life, the Emperor's favor, and the new workers. All modules can be played individually or combined with others, whether from this expansion or the Cacao: Chocolatl expansion. With the "Big Game" variant, you can even play with all modules of both expansions and the base game.

A card game based on the Borrowers, the tiny people that live hidden in our houses and borrow only what they need.

Players share a common deck comprising 10 cards of each of 7 card types: Places, Threats, Escapes, Friends, Foods, Objects, and Tools. Players start with a hand of 7 cards.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 3 phases:

1.   Draw Phase
-    Draw Cards from the deck to fill your hand to 7 cards.

2.   Action Phase
-    You may discard 1 card for its effect: 
•    Places = Discard 1 more card and draw 2 cards
•    Threats = Opponent discards 1 random card from hand 
•    Escapes = Steal 1 random card from an opponent’s hand
•    Friends = Give this card to opponent and draw 2 cards
•    Foods = Draw 1 card
•    Objects = Put any card from the discard into your hand
•    Tools = Discard hand and draw 7 new cards

3.   Meld Phase
-    You may make a meld of 7 cards of one of the following 10 types:
•    Borrower’s Feast: 3 Friends + 4 Foods
•    Dangerous Journey: 3 Places + 2 Escape + 2 Threats
•    Monty Haul: 3 Objects + 2 Tools + 2 Foods
•    Friendly Visit: 2 Friends + 3 Places + 2 Escapes
•    Great Adventure: 1 of each Type
•    Tricks of the Trade: 3 Tools + 4 Escapes
•    Home and Hearth: 4 Objects + 3 Foods
•    Full House: 4 Friends + 3 Objects
•    A Big Scary World: 4 Threats + 3 Places
•    Weapons of Choice: 4 Tools + 3 Threats
-    You cannot make a meld of a type already made this game. 
-    If you complete a meld discard your hand and draw 7 new cards. 
-    If you don’t have a meld, you must discard your hand down to 6 cards.

The winner is the first player to complete 3 melds.

The object of the game is for the Jammie Dodgers to try and move from the start line to a Jammie Heart on the finish line without losing their lives to Danger Dodger.

Before the Jammie Dodgers move, Danger Dodger secretly records which square he will move to.

The Jammie Dodgers move and then Danger Dodger moves.
If Danger Dodger lands on a Jammie Dodger the Jammie Dodger loses a life.

The game ends when everyone has had a turn as Danger Dodger.

The winner is the player with most points.

User summary

Object of the Game

To complete the course in the shortest time possible by taking from the move pack as few cards as possible. Any number of people may play but this is an against the clock race and each player races in turn with normally only one skier on the course at any one time.

Preparation

Set up the board, draw for order of play by cutting the shuffled pack of move cards and the highest card takes the first turn. The Slalom is a two-legged race where the fastest time on aggregate is the winner and the second leg is run in reverse order to the first.

Start of the Game

Shuffle the move and start cards and take the top start card and then 14 move cards. Any number from 0 to 9 of these 14 move cards may be discarded by playing them face downwards and incurring a small penalty of additions to the time of 0.01 secs for each card discarded. This decision to discard (or not)a number of move cards can only be made before the start and from the initial 14 cards dealt. The further  move cards which will be required may be taken from the top of the pack at anytime but all cards taken add one second each to the time whether played or not.

The run commences by playing one of the move cards and so as the first bend is a left hand turn just 2 spaces from the start then a black 2 followed by any black card will make the first turn a perfect one.

The time display board should be maintained after each move allowing for standard course time (marked on the race board), start-move-error-skill cards and the last decimal place is determined by the value of the move card last played.

After reaching the final turn line a player may cross the finishing line by playing any red card of value 2 or greater or a red 1 move card followed by any card.

Finish

After crossing the finishing line a check should be made of all cards in play to confirm the display time.

Expansion found in Plato magazine 97

This expansion adds 14 new characters to the game.

Description from the publisher:

Beer Madness is a small add-on that adds some possibility of interaction between players.

It contains:
1 new beer reviewer,
2 newa Market tiles,
1 new Beer type tile.

1 new type of beer
1 new character
2 new tiles

Four small caterpillars set off on a big journey to the prettiest and biggest flower in the meadow. The luck of the dice decides on how far each caterpillar gets to it. After having arrived on the big flower, the correct pair of wings must be found yet, in order to transform the small caterpillars into a beautiful butterfly.

Develops: Colour recognition and color matching, Fine motor skills, memory, Knowledge of different types of flowers and development stages (caterpillar- butterfly)

Description from the publisher:

The Luftwaffe Raketentruppe Command Squad is a special unit of field officers whose skills are invaluable. They are equipped with assault rifles and a rocket launcher, and their presence on the battlefield can change the entire course of battle, thanks to their precious skills. A wise general should take care to protect these experienced officers.

This squad comes with its own Dust 1947 unit card. Like all regular Dust 1947 miniatures, it comes already assembled and primed.

Description from the publisher:

The Luftwaffe has of course access to the latest generation of jet powered aircraft. Many are even fielded before the trials are over: if there's an operational need, then the plane goes on mission! The Hexe is such an aircraft, equipped with some of the latest systems available. Its nose radar makes the plane very accurate, especially coupled with the underbelly gliding bomb. It allows to attack large targets from a safe distance, a new nightmare for the enemies of the Axis.

This miniature comes with its own Dust 1947 unit card. Like all regular Dust 1947 miniatures, it comes already assembled and primed.

Using the Monster templates, each player tries to draw his or her own Doodle Monster using the Monster Card as a guide. In each round, one body part can be added and is decorated with a pattern. The winner is the player who succeeds in drawing his or her monster first.

Have you already met Julius the Dragon? He lives with his friends, nimble little Filine the Mouse and Paula the wise owl, in a big old castle where lots of thrilling stories are hidden
away, just waiting for you to discover them.

Julius and Filine are feeling excited as they sit in front of the old fireplace in the castle. Paula has promised them a big surprise. In the castle library she has found an old book about
monsters. She says the word “monsters” in a mysterious whisper. Julius’ and Filine’s eyes open wide. “These monsters are everywhere in the castle. They are friendly and playful. They can change their colours and their patterns”, wise owl Paula goes on. “Yippee! Let’s go on a big monster hunt!” they all shout.

Have you ever seen a monster? Try to collect as many shapes and patterns as you can for your own special Doodle Monster.

boardgame: Russia Falling: The Coming Civil War
description:
The game enables two players to play the first year of a hypothesized near-future post-Putin civil war in Russia. The map covers the core area of the Russian ethnic heartland, from St. Petersburg in the north to Tula in the south, and from Smolensk in the west to Nizhny Novgorod in the east, at 16 miles per hex.

The game appeared in issue #85 of the Paper Wars magazine, in spring 2017.

Description from the publisher:

Since many consider green one of the most important colors in the rainbow, no fruit-themed game would be complete without the addition of limes — and these green fruits add just the right twist to the gameplay found in Fabled Fruit.

Explore twenty new locations, the exciting gambling tokens, and the mysterious camouflage coat in Fabled Fruit: The Lime Expansion!

description: This set contains: Tark Mees Taskus, Tark Mees Taskus 2 and Tark Laps Taskus. All three decks may be played separately or shuffled with each other.

Description from the publisher:

Power Grid: Fabled Expansion offers completely new experiences for both the Power Grid and Power Grid deluxe base games through the new Fable Game system introduced in Fabled Fruit. With two presorted Fable Decks, the players are confronted with exciting challenges in different campaigns on the maps of USA, Germany, Europe or North America. Each campaign is played in three consecutive games, and in each game the players reveal Fabled Cards as their conditions are met. These Fabled Cards add new rules to the gameplay.

With two players, the "Against the Trust" rules are in effect.

description:
Description from the publisher:

whatchamaCARDit  is a fast moving card game that rewards strategic play, a sharp mind and a little bit of sneakiness, but doesn’t require strategy or a sharp mind to have a great time.

Collect Single and Combo Collection Cards to be the first player to spell whatchamaCARDit while using CAPTUREit, REQUESTit and REVEALit Cards against other players to improve a hand, but watch out for the BLOCKit Card.

Win on one’s own turn, or on another player’s turn.  Just be the first player to collect/spell and shout whatchamaCARDit.

Description from the publisher:

With the Bicorne English Civil War Catalogue and Rules the reader is given a very simple and enjoyable insight into the English Civil War. A compellingly playable set of fast play rules provide entertainment and challenge for both the novice and veteran players alike, entwined with glorious pictures of the Bicorne English Civil War miniature figure range. The rules have been extensively developed from the author's and friends' house rules. These were purposefully designed to provide a fast, fun and challenging game for wargamers of all abilities, whilst encouraging the education of the readers into the troops and the realities of warfare in the 17th Century. There are many parts to the book in addition to the rules, including an introduction to the Civil War written by Linda Doyle, and a section detailing troop types and uniforms. This has all been designed alongside the catalogue of Bicorne troops where the reader is encouraged to collect and paint an army of model soldiers, with a view to playing enjoyable wargames with friends. To this end there is a section on how to build up your army if you are new to the period, as well as a couple of scenarios to give the reader a good idea of what can be achieved with this fun to play set of rules.

Description from the publisher:

INTRODUCTION:
Skjuub is a combinatorial game for two players. The name comes from the playing pieces, which are 'skewed cubes'. Skjuubs are 'directional', meaning that their orientation is relevant to gameplay.
Skjuub is in a sense a 'logical successor' to the Series: Tixel of games (Tix, Tixel and Regatta). The form factor is different but the 'gameplay structure' is very similar.

SETUP
Skjuub is played on a 6x6 square grid. Each player has her own pool of 11 pieces, differentiated by color.

A PLAYER'S TURN
On a turn, a player can either place a new skjuub or move one of her own skjuubs already on the board. The orientation of a skjuub (which side the 'overhang' is facing) is decided upon when placing it on the board. A skjuub can be active or inactive and only active skjuubs may be moved.
A skjuub is inactive if at least one of its sides is fully connected to a similarly shaped side of another piece (regardless of color).

NORMAL MOVES; Placement
When placing a new skjuub on the board you are not allowed make one or more opponent skjuubs inactive. A new skjuub may be placed next to one or more already inactive skjuubs in a way in which it becomes inactive itself as well. Such placement does not result in the player getting a bonus move (see section 'BONUS MOVES').

NORMAL MOVES; Sliding and tumbling
A move can be either a slide (in which the orientation of the skjuub remains the same) or a tumble (in which in which the orientation of the skjuub does change - the overhang 'switches' to the opposite side).
In a single move a skjuub may be slid as many squares as wanted (horizontally or vertically along its row or column) but may be tumbled only once (to an adjacent square from where it started the tumble, either in the direction of the overhang or in the opposite direction).
When placing, sliding or tumbling, other skjuubs that are on the board may not be 'disturbed'; their locations and orientations must remain the same.

BONUS MOVES
Chaining moves is important as this temporarily unlocks additional move types (besides placing, sliding and tumbling). If a skjuub that is slid or tumbled is made inactive at its end position - i.e., it's sacrificed - the player gets a bonus move. As a bonus move she can do one of the following:

        - one of the normal moves; place, slide or tumble one of her other skjuubs. These slides and tumbles (but not placements) can themselves result in additional bonus moves.
        - pick up one of her other skjuubs from the board (not the one skjuub that was just sacrificed!) and place it back in her pool of available skjuubs.
        - turn one of her active skjuubs a quarter turn clockwise or anticlockwise. The skjuub that is turned must also still be active after the turn (it may not be sacrificed by the turn).
        - pass the bonus move.


When turning a skjuub, other skjuubs that are on the board may not be 'disturbed'. When picking up a skjuub, other skjuubs may temporarily be moved aside to make picking up the skjuub easier but they must be returned to their original locations and orientations afterwards.
One or more other skjuubs can also become inactive by a skjuub being sacrificed. Skjuubs may also be sacrificed adjacent to other skjuubs that are already inactive though (regardless of color).

NO BACKTRACKING
Players are not allowed to undo their previous turn by playing it 'in reverse'. If the previous turn had one or more bonus moves, only the very last bonus move may not be played in reverse.
The main point of the rule is to prevent players from tumbling or sliding one and the same skjuub back and forth in consecutive turns. Therefor it is allowed to pick up a skjuub that was placed in the previous turn. Players are also allowed to alternate between sliding and tumbling the same skjuub repeatedly.
There is one exception to the rule: Backtracking is allowed if it captures an opponent piece (and the piece that does the backtracking becomes inactive itself too).

ENDING THE GAME
A player loses the game if, at the beginning of her turn, she does not have at least one active skjuub on the board that is both able and allowed to slide and/or tumble.

Description from the publisher:

More Advanced Tobruk System adventures for the 101st Airborne in Normandy with eight scenarios featuring The Screaming Eagles on D-Day and the days that follow.
This is a Deluxe Map version, featuring the largest hexes ever in an ATS product - no more stacking!

This set contains:

     8 scenarios on four cards
     a giant hex map using multiple linking cardstock boards
     all of the combat counters and AFV data cards required to play these scenarios
     the Battlefield Walkaround with terrain notes and special module rules.

A card game for 2+ players based on the video game Heroes of Might & Magic III.

Players compete in 3 categories: Might, Magic and Gold. Most cards increase your score in one of these 3 categories. At the end of the game, the player with the highest score in the most categories is the winner.

There are seven Decks:
•   Hero Deck - Heroes are of 2 types: Might Heroes and Magic Heroes. Each Hero also belongs to 1 of the 8 town types. Each player starts with 1 randomly drawn Hero. 
•   Build Deck - These are building located in towns within your Kingdom. Some indicate affiliation with 1 of the 8 town types. Your kingdom may contain multiple Town Affiliations. 
•   Explore Deck - Locations, Structures, and items found within your Kingdom, but outside of your towns. 
•   Creature Deck - Each creature belongs to 1 of the 8 town types. 
•   Spell Deck - Spells belong to 1 of 4 types: Air, Earth, Fire, and Water. 
•   Skill Deck - Advanced Knowledge known to your Heroes. 
•   Artefact Deck - Magical Objects of great Power. 
Cards may have attributes as follows: Ranged, Defence, Quick, Burst, Incapacitate, Reinforce, Buff

All cards owned and controlled by a player are placed face up on the table in front of them. This is each player’s Kingdom.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 4 Phases: 
1.  Build Phase - Draw 1 Card from the Build Deck and add it to your Kingdom. 
2.  Creature Phase - Draw 1 Card from the Creature Deck and add it to your Kingdom. 
3.  Recruit Phase - Roll once on the recruit table and draw 1 card from the Hero, Skill, Spell or Artefact deck, dependant on the die roll.
4.  Explore Phase - Draw 1 Card from the Explore Deck and add it to your Kingdom.

The game ends when every player has completed 7 turns.

The player with the most Ranged cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Defence cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Quick cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Incapacitate cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Burst cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Reinforce cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5. 
The player with the most Buff cards gets Might +5 and Magic +5.

At the end of the game, the player with the highest score in the most categories (Might, Magic and Gold) is the winner.

Description from the publisher:

The classic block stacking game with an alcoholic twist. 54 blocks in total, each block has 1 of 36 different rules and games written on the bottom. Attempt to pull a block from the tower without knocking it over, then play the game or follow the rule described on the bottom of the block. Included are the classics: story time, moose, make a rule. In combination with some newer rules: god, most likely to, nicknames. 36 different rules and games in all. Will you be the last one standing?

boardgame: Horse & Musket II: Sport of Kings
description:
Description from the publisher:

Horse and Musket: Sport of Kings is the first expansion to Horse & Musket: Dawn of an Era. This volume will cover warfare from 1721-1748, when linear musket combat approached its apogee. Large armies of well drilled infantry used volley fire and bayonet charges in set piece battles. Horsemen were used almost exclusively for charges, and artillery was increasingly mobile. Great commanders such as Nader Shah and Maurice de Saxe left their stamp on military history. The period also saw the emergence of Frederick II of Prussia, known to most as Frederick the Great.

The battles include...

Gulnabad - March 8, 1722  – Zenith of the the Hotak Dynasty

Mehmandost - September 29, 1729 – Safavid Restoration

Samarra - July 19, 1733 – The Fate of Baghdad

San Pietro - June 29, 1734 – The Blurred Line Between Bold and Reckless

Guastalla - September 19, 1734 – The Day the World Lost

Ogoula Tchetoka - March 25, 1736 – Father Senat’s Hymn

Ackia - May 26, 1736 – Bienville’s Blunder

Karnal - February 24, 1739 – Death Knell of the Mughal Empire

Grocka - July 22, 1739 – The Treaty of Belgrade

Stavuchany - August 17, 1739 – Stănileşti Avenged

Fort San Lazaro - April 20, 1741 – “To Admiral Vernon, the scourge of Spain”

Mollwitz - April 14, 1741 – “We'll retreat over the bodies of our enemies.”

Chotusitz - May 17, 1742 – Frederick II's First Victory

Fontenoy - May 11, 1745 – “Gentlemen of the French Guards, fire.”

Hohenfriedberg - June 4, 1745 – Der Hohenfriedberger Marsch

Prestonpans - September 21, 1745 – Accidental Cannae

Soor - September 30, 1745 – “God is against us.”

Kesselsdorf - December 15, 1745 – “O Lord God, let me not be disgraced in my old days.”

Culloden - April 16, 1746 – Tragedy on the Moors

Lauffeld - July 2, 1747 – Ligonier’s Charge

Immortals: Queenie 1 – New Tribes is a mini-expansion that adds two additional tribe choices to the Immortals base game: Reptiles from the Dark Realm  and Gnomes from the Light Realm.

To use them, mix the new tribe tokens with the tribe tokens of the base game.

description:
This is a fast, fun and addictive card game

Main Goal: you need to discard all your cards as soon as possible. Loses the one who has the cards on hand.

Game Setup: Shuffle your deck thoroughly and deal three face-down cards in a row to each player. Then deal the same number of cards face up to cover each card that is laying face-down. Now you have six cards before each player: three face-down and three face-up covering them.

You shouldn’t pick that cards up until you shed all the cards from your hand.

Deal each player three cards one more time, that will be player's hands. Place the deck of cards on the table within reach of every player.

Gameplay: the starting player is the one who attended a party recently, closer to the day of the game then other players. Then players are taking turns clockwise. The player lays any card on the table (to start a pick up pile) and then draws card from the deck so they have at least three cards in their hand (unless the deck has run out of cards or they already have three or more cards in their hand). The next player must lay a card equal to or higher in value than the one at the top of the pick up pile. For example: the first player lays 5, the next player can lay 5, or 6, 7, 8, 9, 10. On top of 10 player can lay 10 or 0 (the Poop) or any of the special cards. If a player has no card to cover the the previous card, they must pick up the pick-up pile (put it in their hand) and end their turn, and the next player starts a new round with any card.

A player can lay some cards of the same value in one turn. If a player is able to complete a set in turn (like four-of-a-kind 8*8*8*8) they can do so clearing (burning) and removing the entire pile from play. The player who clears may play another card after.
Special cards (could be placed over any other card):

MegaPug - when played, clears the pick up pile instantly. The player who plays it starts the next round with any card, game is played to increase (the next card may have same or higher value)

BreakDancer - changes the rules from increase to decrease (so the next card may have same or lower value). When played the second time, rules changes back from decrease to increase, and so on.

Poop (0) - resets the game. The next player can lay a card of any value from 0 to 10, game is played to increase.

Game Ending: when the deck runs out, you must play all cards from your hand. When there’s no cards in your hand, you have to play those cards that are laying the face-up in front of you, one by one. Then you play your “Blind” cards, that are laying face-down, one by one - no peeking!
If you have no suitable card - take the pick-up pile in your hand and only when you shed them all you can return to play your cards on the table.

When you have discarded all your cards - congratulations! You banished all your Pug-party animals! So you can chill out watching yours less lucky playmates.

The Pug Party is an entertaining game designed for players from 6 years old. Duration: 10-30 minutes. Simple rules, a dynamic game process, illustrations of a talented artist of Ukraine – this is the formula for the success of the game Pug Party. The first edition of the game was sold out for six months. Because everyone loves funny parties and everyone loves pugs: children, youth and adults are just delighted with these funny little animals. We take an incendiary party, add cute pugs, and get the most explosive and cutest Ukrainian game!

User summary:

You have a farm in South Africa, your worker is Oom Sakkie, but somehow, he is doing everything wrong. Work out how you can run your farm best.

Every player gets a player aid, which is your farm. Every player gets 1630 Rand to start with. Shuffle all cards and place them in the middle of the table.

First, a player draws a card and decide if he want to buy the goods, livestock or other supplies. If the card is an action, the player has to take the action immediately and win or lose some money.

The player who first fills the 10 spaces on their farm with the appropriate goods wins the game.

description:
Mouthpiece game similar to Speak Out and Watch Yo Mouth!.
There are 2500 phrases, 20 mouthpieces and sand timer.

Player must say a phrase with a mouthpiece until somebody guesses it or timer runs out. If one phrase is guessed but there are still time left He/She may continue.

The player or team who collected the most cards wins the game and becomes the Gob Master

Mouthpiece game similar to Speak Out and Watch Yo Mouth!.
There are 2500 phrases, 20 mouthpieces and sand timer.
This game includes red deck for adults only.

Player must say a phrase with a mouthpiece until somebody guesses it or timer runs out. If one phrase is guessed but there are still time left He/She may continue.

The player or team who collected the most cards wins the game and becomes the Gob Master

Description from the publisher:

Did your family play the heck out of the Watch Ya’ Mouth base game, so your kids recite our funny phrases by heart? No problem! Watch Ya Mouth Family Expansion Pack #2 replenishes your repertoire with plenty of fresh phrases for extended kid-friendly gameplay.

Watch Ya’ Mouth, The Original Mouthguard Party Game, is authentic and hilarious. Often imitated, never duplicated.
3-10 players
Hampered by cheek retractors, teams attempt to read and interpret silly phrases, like:
A belligerent bison is on the boardwalk!
Mother baboon and her fluffy babies.
Playful porpoises surfing waves.
Watch Ya Mouth Family Expansion Pack #1 comes with 155 family-safe, family-fun phrase cards and 6 blanks (161 total cards). Our cards are strong, thick, and durable for years of sticky-fingered quality time.

From the publisher´s website:

With Watch Ya’ Mouth Throwdown Edition, 4-10 players face off in our mouthy mashup of all your favorite bar games, relay race activities, and acting, singing, and drawing throwdowns —with some social media twists.

Description from the publisher:

Have you been praying for more Watch Ya’ Mouth fun? Well, hallelujah, the time has come! Introducing Watch Ya’ Mouth Bible Expansion Pack #1. Not only does this pack bring friends and family together to play but the citations at the bottom of most cards will be sure to start conversations about all things Bible. It’s about to get holy up in here!
Watch Ya’ Mouth, The Authentic Mouthguard Party Game. Often imitated, never duplicated.

3-10 players
Hampered by mouthpieces, teams attempt to read and interpret phrases, like:
Flame of fire (Ex 3:2)
Psalms
Breastplate of righteousness (Eph 6:14)

Watch Ya’ Mouth Bible Expansion Pack #1 comes with 200 family friendly phrase cards, including 5 blank cards to write in your own phrases. Our cards are strong, thick, and durable for years of sticky-fingered quality time.

Description from the designer:

Pitch your ideas to tycoons and sabotage the competition!

Pitch your best idea with the most outstanding innovation to a panel of investors, but watch out as others are looking to sabotage your idea in favor of their own. Become the top dog as you craft together winning combinations!

Pitch your ideas to the Tycoon in hopes your amazing idea is picked!

Combine crazy ideas with creative innovations like an Arcade where everyone has a magic wand.

Sabotage your competition to make sure the Tycoon picks your ideas, like it smells like baby vomit.

Be careful though, your competition is out to sabotage you as well.

Have a blast with a multiple players and thousands of combinations.

Each player starts with two of each cards - 2 idea cards, innovation cards, and sabotage cards.

You put together your best Idea with an innovation to pitch to the business tycoon. When the cards are placed face down in front of the tycoon you then place a sabotage card down on any other player’s idea/innovation combo. The tycoon picks the best despite the sabotage.

Play for a few rounds. The player with the most picks from the tycoon wins.

The tycoon rotates every turn clockwise around the players circle.

Give it your all as you give your Best Pitch!

Description from the designer:

Use teamwork to help everyone stay alive or lookout for yourself and forget your family and friends

You've just been locked in a bunker holding out to survive a disaster outside. You and the people you're with must do whatever it takes to survive, but only one of you will be able to live.

Use teamwork to help each other stay alive or lookout for yourself and forget your family and friends.

If everyone dies before the Survive card is drawn, you all lose, but once the survive card is drawn, the last player alive wins!

Use abilities like healing others as the doctor or stealing things as the thief.

Keep secrets to yourself like you can see others players cards when you die.

Or be a team player and let everyone know what you can do.

Work as a team to help each other because if everyone dies before the Survive Card is drawn, everyone loses.

Once the survive card is drawn.. then it’s every man for himself. The last player standing wins the game.

Keep secrets to yourself like you can see others players cards when you die.

Players take turns drawing event cards and play out that event.

Play events like deciding which team member should be killed, or if the engineer should fix the pipes today.

Make friends as you work to survive, then stab them in the back to ensure you live.

The game is simple, Just Survive The Game.

Stay Off My Lawn is a short (15-20 minute) card game designed for all ages, kids and adult. Players compete to have the best lawn, while trashing their opponent's lawn. When the parade comes, the best lawn wins the trophy!

Each turn players place a beneficial card from their hand into their own yard or a harmful card into their opponent's yard and then draw a card from the center deck. Each player has one "Clean Up" card that may be played at any time, but only once per game.

The game ends when the parade arrives and the lawns are judged. Players each total are points and relevant bonuses. The player with the most points wins!

boardgame: The Princess Bride: 30th Anniversary Celebration Pack
description: The Princess Bride: 30th Anniversary Celebration Pack features cards for use in three separate Princess Bride games: I Hate to Kill You, A Battle of Wits, and Prepare to Die!
Farlight: Launch Pack adds ten new cards featuring new missions and ship parts to the Farlight base game.

From the Kickstarter campaign page:

The Attraction Pack includes 8 attraction cards achieved by unlocking stretch goals. The Attraction Pack was provided exclusively to Kickstarter backers for a limited time and will eventually be available in the BGG Store and the publisher's website.

Bring the Trailer Park home...to your kitchen table. This print and play tabletop war-game pits two or more players against each other in an attempt to raise the most cash before time runs out. 
Players each have a set of goals (scams) they are trying to accomplish, while trying to prevent the opposing team(s) from doing the same. Alternatively, players can choose to simply start a trailer park brawl, and try to be the only team with characters left at the end of the game.
Each character has unique abilities to choose from, and any character can work with any other - if you so choose. So make your team of characters, and get ready to rule the trailer park. 
Printable characters are provided as part of the game, but players are encouraged to use the miniatures from the Trailer Park Boys board game as well.

Fantasy Realms: Jester is a promotional card for Fantasy Realms that was initially packaged with Game Trade Magazine #210. Here's the text:

Bonus: +3 for each other card with an odd base strength value. OR +50 if entire hand has odd base strength values.

Super Wits is a team-based version of Wits and Wagers pitting a team of heroes against a team of villains.

The standard Wits & Wager rules apply: 

    Everyone writes a estimate to a question. 
    The estimates are arranged from smallest to largest.
    Players bet on any estimate (their own or someone else's)


Additionally, each player on the team has a unique power that gets activated if they bet on their headquarters (their own answer).  The superpowers interact with each other in synergistic ways, providing a fun puzzle each turn as your team discusses under time pressure, the best place to move their Superhero/Supervillain and sidekicks.

Super Wits take 25 minutes.  Additional heroes and villains will be available in the future, allowing people to tailor their team, or expand the player count to 8 players.

Say Anything Uncensored plays just like the classic party game Say Anything, but it does not have any of the benign "we might be playing  at church with a bunch of people we don't know very well" questions.

description:
Your empire has grown vast and powerful, reaching beyond every edge of the galaxy. Now you must ensure the ties that bind your planets do not falter.

Assert your dominance in the political arena. Use your clout to advance your agendas. As your borders expand farther and farther, do not let the core of your empire fall into oblivion!

Oblivion is an expansion of Eminent Domain, and cannot be played without the base game. Its focus is on the political aspects of running a space empire, adding a new Politics role and global agendas that affect all players. We recommend that you play the base game several times before incorporating this expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – UNSC Phoenix Class Support Ship

Fans of Halo will undoubtedly recognise the unmistakable profile of the Phoenix-class Colonial Support Vessel, the most famous of which is known by the name Spirit of Fire. Replete with its Pelican Boarding Craft our version of the Phoenix-class brings UNSC players a new Large size model with which to assemble their fleets.

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – Covenant CPV-Class Heavy Destroyer
description:
Description from the publisher:

As the war progressed the crews of the CPV's found a newfound purpose as executioners and heralds, wielding excavation beams with deft hands and claws to carve meaningful sigils into cleansed worlds and leading battle groups against remnant UNSC forces judged unworthy of meeting the fleet's Supreme Commander. Veteran crews also found a new currency of honor in using their well-practiced skills to assist the artifact hunting fleets that scoured former human worlds for Forerunner relics and war trophies, with many turning quasi-mercenary and offering their services to Ministry leaders eager to one-up each other with treasures and baubles recovered from the glassed ruins.

Each book tackles a specific time period of history. This book covers the Roman Invasion of Britain in the First Century. Simple rules are provided written by Andy Callan, noted designer. The rules can be used with other miniatures.

The miniatures are drawn and coloured by Peter Dennis, the well known face behind Osprey books, many illustrations and Martin Wallace's games. The buyer photocopies or scans the images provided, folds and cuts them out to make armies.

description:
Expansion for sakura arms dueling card game, this expansion adds 2 new machine/invention themed megami:
Kururu(クルル) and thallya(サリヤ).
Kururu is the megami of innovation and madness. She does not care what sacrifices it takes to complete her machines. Her mechanic requires you to have played cards(not discard) in order to complete the required components(the card type determines what components it counts as). She is the hardest megami to learn , and will remain that way according to the designer.
Thallya is a megami who was formerly a human from another land/country. She rides a motorcycle and fights with a "sword-whip". Her mechanics revolve around her "fuel", an extra resource that many of your cards require to use, or can make use of. She also has the ability to gain unique basic actions via transforming(utilizing her S4 special card)While she has a high skill cap she is easier to approach than Kururu.
This expansion adds more difficult mechanics than prior expansions, according to the publisher it will likely remain the most mechanically difficult expansion.

Sakura Arms is a Japanese dueling living card game made for 2 players, in which players build 10 card decks from a limited pool in order to reduce the opponents life to zero.

“You can’t lose hope. Without hope, then you have nothing.”

Based on the hit novel, After Everyone’s Dead, this game pits a team of heroes against a hoard of zombies. Choose your side in the fight, a hero trying to get to the safe house or a zombie trying to turn all humans into flesh eating zombies like themselves!

Ever want to take out a hoard of your friends who turned into zombies?

Ever find yourself as a zombie trying to kill your friends?

In After Everyone’s Dead the game, now you can!

Two teams, one human one zombie battle against each other.

Customize the board however you want! With thousands of board layout combinations, every game is a new adventure!

Play on a small layout with a few friends for a short fun game, or a larger layout for a more open experience.

Use weapons like guns, hammers, and rockets to take out your enemies

Be careful though because a human might switch teams if they die, turning into a blood thirsty zombie!

To win, the humans must get the key and make their way to the safe house. For the Zombies to win all they have to do is eat every human brain.

So do you have what it takes to survive a zombie outbreak? Or do you have what it takes to devour your friends?

Description from the designer:

Build your custom robot and battle your enemies!

Build your robot just the way you like it! Use swords, lasers, rockets, and countless other weapons to destroy your opponents. This turn based strategy card game will put your wit, luck, and strategy skills to the test.

Ever wish you had your own robot to fight in combat against other robots? Well not any more with Circuit Destroyer.

Build your own robots with customized a head, arms, legs, and cores, each with powerful abilities.

Use weapons like swords, lasers, shields, and rockets to blow up your opponents robot.

Players must protect their robots core, for once that’s destroyed the player loses and the games over.

Boost your robots power using Software cards and Hack cards, sending an E.M.P. blast at an attacking robot, or doubling your damage this turn.

Circuit Destroyer comes in a variety of packs for you to collect.

Each pack comes with an array of part and at least 1 rare card.

Circuit Destroyer is a game for a beginning strategist or an expert tactician.

Description from the desginer:

Be the best Stick Figure Fighter in all the land.

Battle for the glory of being the greatest Stick Figure Fighter! Equip your Stick Figure Fighter with weapons, armor, and power cards. Fight other stick figures to prove that you are the most powerful Stick Figure Fighter. Show no mercy, using swords, shields, super armor, or your stick arms to vanquish your opponents.

Take turns challenging other Stick Figure Fighters in head to head combat, until you are the last stick standing.

Battle for the glory of being the greatest stick figure in the world.

Show no mercy to your opponent using swords, shields, armor, shoes, helmets, and an array of power ups in battle.

As you play equip more and more armor with unique abilities to your stick figures.

Use armor that boosts your attack, helmets that shoot lasers, bow and arrows to pin your opened, a giant sword to cut through your stick enemies, and shoes that let you dodge attacks.

Every turn, you must battle another stick figure in stick combat to the death.

The last stick figure standing is the winner, declaring themselves to be the greatest Stick figure fighter.

Description from the designer:

Fight to rule the kingdom!

True King is a strategy game designed to either work as a team with other players or lead the kingdom all on your own.

Move your units to steal the rival kingdom’s flag and bring it back to your kingdom unharmed. Travel perilous terrain arranged by the Kings of each kingdom.

Use strategy and teamwork to topple your opponent and become the one True King.

Be a fearless King, a courageous Princess, a valiant Knight, a powerful Wizard, or a cunning Archer and rule over the land.

As king of the land, it’s time to rule with an iron fist.

Command your forces to capture the nearby kingdom’s flag, forcing their surrender.

In this turn based strategy game us the kingdom’s King, Knight, Wizard, Princess, and Archer to move through the land to your opponent’s kingdom.

Take turns with your opponent laying out the land with magical traps and powerups.

Use special character powers like the wizard’s ability to teleport a player anywhere they wish or the knights ability to block opponent’s movement.

Play head to head against a rival or play with a group or people, each controlling one of the characters in the kingdom, taking turns moving.

To win the game and control all the kingdoms the player’s must capture the enemy’s flag and bring it back to their own base.

Watch out for magical traps like holding spells, teleporters, and power voiding areas as you traverse the map!

Use your wits to prove you have what it takes to be the one, True King.

Description from the designer:

Urbs is a quick card drafting game, where players develop and expand their cities placing city blocks cards over three seasons. Each card adds a prominent place to your city from one of six categories: Progress, Commerce, Leisure, Tourism, Residences and Industry. In each game a different combination of scoring cards is drawn, encouraging players to look for different ways to develop their cities.

description:
During the Kickstarter for Spires they offered a Deluxe Content expansion pack. It was only available in a few of the pledge levels.

Content:

8 New Scroll cards  (the graphic in the Kickstarter said 7)
1 Rule card

English Summary:
We can simulate a series of security incident response　with playing "Malware Containtment(Malcon)"
As a member of CSIRT, players  investigate and contain terminals infected with malware in the organization.

Players need to investigate the terminal card on the field and remove two specific cards.
Players have special abilities according to job title cards, and each will investigate the terminal.
Players can not talk each other.
However, if pleyers are in the same field you can exchange information.

Japanese Summary:
「Malware Containment(Malcon)」をプレイすることで、セキュリティインシデント対応の一連の流れを疑似体験することができます。
プレイヤーはCSIRTの一員として、マルウェアに感染している端末を調査し、封じ込めていきます。

プレイヤーは協力して、フィールド上の端末カードを調査し、特定の２枚のカードを取り除くことを目的とします。プレイヤーは、初めに配られる役職カードに応じた特殊能力を持ち、各自で端末を調査していきます。プレイ中、プレイヤーは会話できませんが、同じフィールドにいる場合は情報を交換することができます。

Description from the publisher:

Smack Talk Showdown is the party game where smack talking your opponents is not only encouraged, it's necessary!  The game is all about the art of the wrestling promo. Two players assume the guise of professional wrestlers and go head-to-head for verbal supremacy while a third player dictates the action as a Producer. Everyone not competing in the current turn (which we call a Segment) is the Audience and gets to choose the victor of the round!

When a round starts, each Wrestler draws one name card from each of the two Name card decks. This is their wrestling persona. The Producer then draws a Segment card. This tells the Wrestlers where they are and what they are fighting about. The Producer then draws three Smack cards to play on either Wrestler during their promo. These cards dictate the action and can be played on either Wrestler during their promos.Each Wrestler has 45 seconds to cut their promo. After which, the Audience votes for the victor. If only three people are playing, the Producer decides.

boardgame: Sight words challenge: Grade 4 – Mountain bike mania
description:
This is an educational mountain bike themed word recognition game.
Players move around a board landing on words that they must recognize by sight.  They must then try to use it in a sentence on their game cards.  First to complete eight sentences wins.

Description from the publisher:

Festival of Masks provides a new way to play Argent, as well as a new resource to race for during play rounds. Masks provide continuous benefits for an entire round of play, much like some Bell Tower Cards, but the round doesn't end when they're all taken, and you can only have one. Use these masks as the engine for your key strategies, to reduce costs, improve efficiency, and harass opponents.

This expansion includes the three cards from the Kickstarter Promo Pack #1

Jutlandia: Juego de las Flotas

Jutland: Game of Fleets

This is a game produced in Mexico in 1942.  Unfortunately the version I found does not have a set of rules.  Through research I found it was published in Mexico in 1942.  
It consists of a set of battleships, cruisers, destroyers, submarines, Base Flags, Air Bases and coastal fortification for two players.  It also includes a large map with the ability to designate each hex with a letter and a number.

boardgame: An Unnecessarily Long Story (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
This is a fan made expansion for T.I.M.E Stories.

It is a fantasy style, a bit similar to PoD but quite different in terms of era and feelings. It is more of fairy tales settings.

boardgame: The Isle of the Petrified Giants (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
This scenario takes place in the 17th Century, on the Easter Islands. 
A mysterious source of energy has appeared which must be gotten rid of ...

News from the outpost is worrying. The Leviathans, these terrifying sea monsters, are converging towards the border and threatening the Kingdom. Will the Allies and the conscripts, mandated by each of the Guilds, contain them? Exploring is now dangerous: fighting is not easy, and fleeing can be even more dangerous — but the opportunity is unique to prove your worth and use this influence to gain access to the throne.

In Abyss: Leviathan, the threat track is replaced by the border board on which Leviathan cards will be placed. When you explore the depths, if the revealed card is a monster, you can fight a Leviathan on the border. Some new lords and some allies will help you fight, using their power. The player who has killed the greatest number of sea monsters takes the statue and wins 5 extra points at the end of the game — and if you do not fight, you may get injured...
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary
エンデの建国者 (The Founders of ENDE) is a board game with a theme of island development. The game board is a geographical map of an island called Ende. Players try to score victory points by constructing buildings and/or winning battles on the island.

At the beginning of the game, each player has a different set of four action cards, which varies by player order. Each round, they take turns playing action card until all players have taken three actions. At the end of round, they pass their set of cards to the player to the left.

By performing an action, players may construct/upgrade a building, gain resources, raise military power, initiate a battle, etc. There are plenty of player interactions, such as limited nubmer of buildings in a territory and initiating a battle with a specific player. But battle is not always necessary to win the game.

A player can win immediately by upgrading a building into the castle, but it requires much military power. Strategy selection (winning by castle, battles, buildings, or their hybrid) and action planning with consideration of rotating sets of cards are keys to winning the game.

開拓しつつ、資源を獲得し、建物を建てて、勝利点を得ていくタイプのゲームです。
基本的にはリソースマネジメントです。
資源獲得をして建物を建てて、建物の効果で方向性が定まります。

インタラクションは、豊富にあります。
建築や目的地カードの早い者勝ちという要素や、特定のプレイヤーに戦争/内戦を仕掛けるという直接攻撃もあります。ただし、直接攻撃を強制される類のゲームではありません。

城によるの勝利条件と、戦争/内戦による牽制と、カードセットの巡りによる計画性がこのゲームのポイントです。
publishers: イマジンゲームズ (imagine GAMES)

description:
A baseball game played with a single deck of 56 cards depicting different actions, either defensive or offensive, happening during a baseball game. Even with Expo in its name, the game is not officially affiliated with the Montreal Expos.

According to the rulebook, the game can be played 3 different ways:

Game 1: Simply shuffle the deck and flip cards to "simulate" a nine innings game.

Game 2: Two players, each simultaneously completing a half-inning,  alternate drawing and playing cards to either accumulante runs or give 3 outs to their opponents. This is the recommended rule set in the rule book.

Game 3: A variant of game 2 but played with 4 peoples, in partnerships.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: World of Happy is a roll-and-move game given away at McDonald's restaurants.  In the game, players roll a die (made by folding a punched-out section of the game board into a cube) and move to the next matching symbol on a track.  One side of the die causes loss of a turn, and some space move the player forward or allow skipping of part of the track.  The first player to reach the "World of Happy" at the end of the track wins.

Description from the publisher:

The girl who oversaw morning!

You have to get to the school quickly to avoid obstacles to school!
돌진소녀 ("Rush Girl") is a real-time game that requires fast pace and awareness.


     All cards are mixed well and divided into 7 cards.
         At this time, players should not look at the cards they have received.
    
     Place a 'safe' card in the reach of everyone.
     Pick one of the remaining cards at random and put the safe card
         Leave it aside. (In case of 5-player play, one card is left.)
    
     Take the card you received at the same time in your hand.
     The game is played in real time.
         Check the last obstacle!
         Check the card in your hand!
         If you can fix it, shout out the next obstacle!
             Only the first card you put on the old card is accepted.

Polar
     Square board, 9x9 or 11x11 or 13x13
     Black and white pieces


				
				
					RulesPieces are placed on empty intersections of the board. Connections are orthogonal only.

The board is initially empty.

One of the players takes two pieces (black and white) and places them on any arbitrary adjacent spaces. The other player may now decide on which color to play.

Black starts, then players take turns placing one of their pieces on an empty space according to the following rules.

Connected pieces form a group, and same-colored connected pieces within a group are called a section. A group may never consist of more than two sections.

Player may only pass if they have no moves left. They must play again in a successive turn when moves are available again.

The game ends when both players have to pass.

				
				
					ScoreGroups with one or equally sized sections score zero points. For any other group, the player holding the majority section gets the triangular number section points: 
T(n) = n*(n+1)/2

The player with the highest total score wins. Games can end in a draw.

Description from the designer:

Super Sumo is simple party game for 2 to 4 players.

This dexterity game can be played on a table or directly on the floor.

There are 2 teams : the Blue Sumos and the Red Sumos.

Each team controls 4 sumos (4 circular coaters).

In this game, each team is trying to control The golden cookie.

The golden cookie (a tiny token) is put on the center of the playing area at the beginning of the game.

Players throw their sumos in order to be as close as possible to the golden cookie.

When all the sumos have been launched, the round is over. The team which controls the golden cookie (the closest sumo) wins 1 point.

The first team with 3 points wins the game.

The arabic word "Azzelij" means "small polished stone" and is known as the historical origin of the world "Tile".

In Azzelij, two to four players compete to build a beautiful tile panel, trying to get more points with your own colors.

Each player have 12 tile pieces at their stock pile that are used do form circle patterns, like an artist trying to get the best part of the pannel! At first round, each player get 3 tiles from their pile and than draw one each subsequent round, maintaining 3 options to play at almost all time. In your turn, you must place one tile (considering that you cant extend the panel mor than 7x7 tiles away) and optionally rotate 90º clockwise another placed tile (only if the rotated tile is not forming a circle pattern yet). The turns goes until the 7x7 panel is created.

You score one point creating circle patterns with absolute majority of your colour and 3 points if the circle created has only your color. You also score bonus points (1 for each circle) if the scored circle is created only by your pieces.

Ar fisrt glance, Azzelij seems to be a simple tile placement, but as the game flows players faces lots of hard decisions to make: May I get a point or avoid another player to get a better position? May I close the 7 piece limit to stop opponents to grow at one edge or stay creating my pattern? May I risk to get a full-colored circle of mine or get two or more majority-only circle points?

As the game flows, the decisions ar harder to take and the panel gets its 7x7 piece formation, that increases the difficulty and fun!

Becoming a king is hard, but being a king is even harder. You need warriors to protect your kingdom, merchants to keep your treasury liquid and heralds which proclaim your popularity across the kingdom. Luckily, your best mate has agreed to take over the hard graft so that you concentrate becoming a glory chieftain.

Isle of Skye: Journeyman, the first big expansion to Isle of Skye, contains new personal player boards indicating your progress in terms of strength, prosperity and popularity. As each progress step has requirements to be met, the personal player boards affect tile selling and placement. However, in order to claim the next level of development and gain potential rewards, it’s not sufficient to place the corresponding tiles only. A new pawn (the journeyman) needs to travel the kingdom and "activate" the tiles. In addition, four new scoring tiles are contained respecting the new challenges of Isle of Skye: Journeyman.

What's Pushido?
It's a pitching game for 2 players. You're a good sumo fighting against evil sumos!

What's in the bag?

+  6 tiny wooden disks (1 blue sumo et 5 red sumos).

+ 12 tiny stickers (2 blue sumos and 10 red sumos).

+  1 rules card (English and French).

+ 1 Zip bag.

How to play?

+  Put all the tokens randomly in the center of the table.

+ The youngest player starts, he pitches the blue sumo with one of his fingers.

+  He tries to push one red sumo out of the table.

+ If he succeed, he wins 1 point and plays again.

+  If he doesn't, the second player plays.

+ In turns, the game continues until there is a winner.

How to win?

+  If a player pushes the blue sumo out of the table, he loses the game.

+ If a player has 3 points and the blue sumo is on the table, he wins the game.

Earth! Air! Fire! Water! Danger? Zeta City has seen its share of heroes, but none the likes of these. Elemental heroes join the fray, harnessing the very forces of nature against an all new bevy of criminal geniuses, sentient websites, and opinionated baddies!

But wait! There's more! Not only does this expansion for Heroes Wanted add a brand new hero type to your already near-infinite game play options, it also provides even more content for the other hero types in the game. Players will now have bonus cards unique to their hero, as well as never-before-seen epic actions! And the fun doesn't stop there! Elements of Danger also introduces improved basic actions to make your hero even more unique!

Be a hero. Save the day. Make the team!

A cooperative party game where players hold elastic bands and tangle the group by stepping over or under the bands. The goal of the game is to untangle the group so that everyone is facing the middle.

King & Crown is a modular microgame for 2-5 players. With only 5 cards per deck, King & Crown offers five different games utilizing the same cards, though some variants require each player to have their own deck of cards. From dueling to bluffing, to social deduction, there’s something in here for everyone. And with the small size and few components, it’s great to take along and play while you’re in line waiting for other things.

Description from the publisher:

The battles to control the Nine Worlds take on a new dimension with the introduction of hidden objectives to fulfill. At the same time, in their efforts to conquer the Nine Worlds, the lords of each world turn to powerful artifacts and potent treasures to gain the advantage. Can you still conquer the Nine Worlds?

This expansion for Nine Worlds contains 30 cards:
12 Objective Cards, 12 Treasure Cards, 6 Victory Point Cards. (used in the games featuring Treasure Cards)

Players can decide if they wish to play with both Objectives and Treasures or just use one set for their game.

Players may use 1 or two Treasures in the game which each have an unique power they can use to give them the edge.

Objectives are hidden goals players can score bonus points for achieving.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Tragic Events
description:
Flash Point: Fire Rescue - Tragic Events is an expansion for Flash Point: Fire Rescue, a cooperative game for 1-6 players that plays in 45 minutes.

Tragic Events includes the Fire & Event decks that are designed to replace the Hot Spot mechanic in the Experienced game rules and add exciting new "events" to make each game of Flash Point even more unique. This expansion also includes three specialists, each with their own mini: Strategist, Suppression Specialist, and Fire Prevention Specialist.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Marvel fans assemble for the action-packed, picture-trivia card game 
Pictopia: Marvel Cinematic Universe Edition'. Test your knowledge of your favorite Marvel movies with two hundred questions! Answer trivia about Earth's Mightiest Heroes, the greatest Guardians of the Galaxy, and all Realms in between! Earn tokens for each correct answer! For a daring twist, risk what you've earned for the chance to win big!

boardgame: Bears vs Babies: Exclusive Backer Card
description:
You are so marvelous.
If I could, I would give you 5 stars.
I would grind you up
into a paste.
I would put that paste on a hoagie
and take slow, thoughtful bites.
I would relish you
as a submarine sandwich.
Because you're great
and you backed our Kickstarter.
You made our game a reality.
Thank you
-Bears vs Babies

P.S. Inside this sleeve is your very own Exclusive Backer Card. You can use it in place of the Bear Heads in the game, or use it to add an extra player.
This promotional card for Bye-Bye Black Sheep is a wild card that can be used to represent any set card.

An exclusive setting card for Dark Tales.

During the Solstice Faire, a terrible scuffle broke out between the Villagers and the people of the castle because of an enchanted elixir. In order to preserve the reputation of the realm, the Margrave decides to investigate...

Released in Spielbox issue 2017/7.

It must be very exciting to get across the Alps riding an elephant like Hannibal, to conquer the greater part of the world like Napoleon or to subdue Napoleon like Kutusov. Still in is more interesting to be able - like a mighty general - to command one's own modern army, to purchase arms, tanks, and soldiers and fight battles against the armed forces of an imaginary country. The game can be played by two or more players, more partners can overcome the enemy with allied forces led by mutual chosen commanders.

There are neither children nor adults who not be taken by a victory, that is due to their own skillful, cunning strategy and their clever quick decisions.

Thunee is a traditional South African Indian trick-taking card game for 4 players in two partnerships. Using a 24-card deck (J, 9, A, 10, K, Q of each suit), players compete to score 12 "balls" to win. The game features a unique card ranking where the Jack is highest and the 9 is second, trump calling based on partial information (4 of 6 cards), and several distinctive mechanics including Jodies (King-Queen declarations that can be bluffed), Thunee calls (winning all 6 hands), Double, and Khanack. Originating among Indian indentured labourers in KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa from the 1860s onwards, Thunee has been played for over 150 years and remains the most popular card game in the South African Indian community.

The Stone King is a campaign expansion to Fantasy Defense, adding new units, scenarios and rules that twist the battle in new directions. You can't just open the box and play with everything immediately; instead, the Stone King Campaign is divided into eight hidden chapters, each building on the last. Each chapter is printed with the unlock condition that must be met before players can open it.

Inside each chapter, you'll always find the same two types of cards: The story card reveals the world of Fantasy Defense, with notes and snippets that drive the narrative forward; the rules card introduces new mechanisms and concepts, so you can play with all the components you've just unlocked. However, Fantasy Defense isn't a legacy game! When it's all finished, you can return to the original version and play that. You could also put back all cards in their original envelope and start from the beginning — or you can spice things up, mixing and matching different elements as you wish.

The Stone King provides a more varied and challenging experience for those players who have figured out the basic gameplay of Fantasy Defense.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: A card-driven game about getting your meerkats to safety. Some cards will move one of your meerkats forward, other cards will cause the eagle to strike. If the eagle strikes when your meerkats  are on top of the mound, you have to be quick to get the meerkats to safety.

With the Dream Home: 156 Sunny Street expansion, you can spice up your next game of Dream Home with more helpers, tools, décor, rooms, and two new ways to play! With the "Family and Friends" mode you can invite friends and relatives to stay over at your house, and with "Construction Plans" you score additional points by following specific construction requirements.

Dream Home: 156 Sunny Street includes components that allow you to play Dream Home with up to six players, while also including a solitaire version for when you're home alone.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Deutscher Spielepreis 2017 Goodie Box is a product to support the Deutscher Spielepreis, the second biggest German board game prize, for which thousands of players vote each year between May and July for their favorite game. This box contains a small expansion for four of the top ten games of the Deutscher Spielepreis 2017: A Feast for Odin, First Class, Great Western Trail, and Ice Cool, as well as a bonus expansion for Word Slam.

Contains:

     ICECOOL: Brian Gomez Promo
     A Feast for Odin: New Special Tiles
     First Class: Call for Tenders
     Great Western Trail: The Eleventh Building Tile
     Word Slam: DSP-Edition
New York Slice: 2017 Specials is a set of nine special promotional tiles for use with the New York Slice base game.

From the makers of Dinner's Ready!, Happy Hour! is the first card game to help get you buzzed, challenging players to collect and deliver cocktails based on actual recipes! Not only is Happy Hour! fun and competitive, the more you play, the more familiar you'll become with cocktails!


    Drinking IS NOT a required element of playing the Happy Hour! card game, but we do have suggested drinking rules for full enjoyment!*


Happy Hour! is easy to set up and fun to play!

Happy Hour! is a game that fundamentally parallels the bartending experience of assembling mixed drink orders as quickly as possible.

Overview of gameplay: Players are dealt a hand of INGREDIENT CARDS, containing alcohols, mixers, and toppings.

INGREDIENT CARDS: There are four kinds of alcohol ingredients: vodka, whiskey, gin, and tequila!
There are a handful of MIXERS, including triple sec, sweet & sour, club soda, tonic water, ginger beer and vermouth!
The TOPPING cards consist of garnish (orange/lime/lemon/olive), cherry, and mint!
There are also ACTION CARDS which can help or hinder your drink-making progress, including spilled drinks, restock the bar, "scratch that," make it a double, and wild cards!

THE BAR: A row of ingredient cards available during each turn.
THE COUNTER: A row of recipe cards that must be fulfilled.

Using these cards in combination, players can create any one of the 21 drink RECIPE CARDS. Drinks range on a scale of 4-7 points, depending on quantity of ingredients required.

The first "bartender" to collect 21 points wins!

Happy Hour! is a fun card game for 2-4 adults, with rounds typically requiring 10-40 minutes.

LazerKnight is a solo chess variant with a razor sharp edge. You are the LazerKnight! Your whole regiment was slaughtered in a brutal ambush and you are the sole survivor. Sworn to avenge your dead comrades, you sneak through the enemies' territory to defeat them single-handedly. Or at least take as many of them with you as you can!

In this game you take on the role of a single knight chess piece which is going against a full set of opposing chess pieces. Your goal is to take out the enemy king and stay alive. To pull this off against all odds, you have the ability to hide out in black squares, gathering strategy tokens, which allow you to every once in a while break from your cover and strike out with multiple moves at once! But don't wait too long, because the enemy is not standing still and will try to encircle you, until its checkmate for you.

LazerKnight is a 1-page-rule ready to play solo chess game, specifically designed for the Boardgamegeek Solitaire Print and Play Contest. All you need to play is a chess board, chess pieces, 2 eight-sided dice and 5 tokens.

Description from the publisher:

Foreman Ben oversees a large construction site, making sure that all machines and vehicles are at the right place at the right time. But watch out! Sometimes Ben has to run to the men's room. Who is the fastest worker, carrying out the most tasks for Ben?

Caution, Under Construction! is a high-speed construction-site card game.

Everyone wants to hold the coveted DaCart Rally trophy — but the race track is tricky and constantly changing, so the drivers must try to remember the best route without getting trapped in a dead end! Who can steer their zooming racer as quickly as possible over the pit stop and across the right finish line?

Rally Run! is a tricky memory matching game.

—description from the back of the box

The Frozen City harbours many secrets, and not all of them are ancient. While most adventurers who brave the dangers seek wealth and lost magic, some journey into Frostgrave for more personal reasons…

This expansion for Frostgrave consists of 40 Ulterior Motive cards, which add variety, depth, and new tactical challenges to wargames in the Frozen City. Each card presents the player with a specific task to accomplish and offers rewards if they succeed. Some of these missions must be revealed to all of the players, others must be kept secret. Will your wizard seek to slay a great demon? Rescue a desperate captive? Bring retribution to an enemy? All wizards seek power, but what are their ulterior motives?

"The test is about to begin. Gather the correct combination of food items as they move on the conveyor belt and deliver it to the organic test subjects. Execute the right subroutines to serve them what would keep their squishy meat components operating within their optimal parameters. The robot model that performs the best will be selected for mass production. Ensure that it is you."

Robo Diner is a fast-paced family card game featuring set collection and simultaneous action selection mechanics for 2-4 players ages 12 and up. Players start the game with a robot waiter character card that is placed in front of a specific section of the conveyor belt. When a round begins, players will secretly choose whether or not they will move the belt by one step.

Food items on the belt can be manipulated with the use of power cards. Players pick up the card that is located in front of their character in order to fulfill customer orders. At the end of the game, the winner is the player that has managed to please customers the most by grabbing the right cards from the belt, completing orders, and avoiding mistakes.

10 cut-n-play tiles for Carcassonne in the new art style introduced in 2014 that apparently were hidden on the bottom of the gift box "Hans im Päckchen" given at out Essen 2014.

There are 10 tiles (that have to be cut out from a sheet) and no meeples (the players have to use some substitutes). If you glue them on blanc tiles, you could use it as an expansion.

Players explore what fetishes their partner might be into while trying to guess what they will actually be. The base set of Come Together has 142 cards with different sex acts/fetishes on them. Four cards are set out, then players secretly select which card they are most interested in. Then players guess which card their partner chose. Unchosen cards are discarded and replaced, and a secret selection happens again. If players match on the same card twice in a row, they win and probably want to try out whatever the winning match is.

The game is very much not for kids. But with values rooted in sex positive feminism and content that supports a wide variety of sexual interests and gender identities, the game is also very much not like most other "couples" games on the market.

Gotham City is plagued by its most famous villains: Joker, Riddler, Penguin, Mr Freeze, Killer Croc and Man bat. Each player takes on the role of Batman, in a race to defeat these villains and save the city.

Players roll dice and move around the board collecting tokens and fighting villains, in order to collect transport tokens. these are in the form of the Batmobile, Bat Boat, and Bat Cycle, each of which allows the player to advance to the next level of the board. The first player to defeat a villain on level 4 is the winner.

Promo card.

     Available at Gen Con 2016.
     Available in the Unfair Kickstarter 2016.
     Available in the Unfair ABDW expansion Kickstarter 2018.
     Planned to be available in future crowdfunding campaigns.


Shuffle this card into the relevant deck(s) during setup and proceed as normal.
Add no more than one copy of any sneak peek or promo card.

Gen Con (Attraction - Sideshow)
Park limits do not apply to this attraction.
If this attraction ever closes, move it to the next park in turn order then reopen it.
Guests step: Gain 1 extra coin for each guest visiting your park.

There's epic loot to be had, if only you can sneak it past the dragon.

Yokohama: Promo Tiles is a punch sheet of two unique promos for Yokohama.

Promo #1: Achievement Reward Tokens

Setup: Randomly choose 4 achievement reward tokens to place near the achievement cards. Return the rest to the box.

Rules: When completing all 3 achievements, collect one of the achievement rewards and keep it face up in front of you. You may count achievement reward flags toward obtaining foreign agents and scoring for country icons at the end of the game. You may use achievement reward free agents like any other foreign agent during your free action phase.

Promo #2: Free Agent 5-power Tokens

Setup: Mix these free agent tokens into the supply of 5-power tokens and setup the game as normal.

Rules: When you collect a free agent token, keep it face up in front of you. You may use it like any other foreign agent during your free action phase.

One of the over 50 implementations of the Box Quiz series, this one with the theme 'Travel'.

The game consists of a tin box with 50 questions and answers within a category, here about travel. It is a small and simple quiz game, where players simply answer the different questions.

One of the over 50 implementations of the Box Quiz series, this one with the theme 'Love'.

The game consists of a tin box with 50 questions and answers within a category, here about love. It is a small and simple quiz game, where players simply answer the different questions.

This is a collection of games teaching single-unit multiplication equations (from 2 to 5). The game contains 42 cards, half of which have 5 numbers (products) on them, the other half have 5 equations. One of the equations on an equations card will match one of the numbers on a number card.

In one of the games, players find the matching equation and number on a pair of cards (similar to Catch the Match). In other games, players find matches between two products, or two equations.

Discover the secret life of Santa in Ice Dice! Practice basic arithmetic operations: roll the dice and use the values of the dice and either addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division to obtain a new number and move the corresponding token from one board to another. Collect the best combination of tokens on your player board to win!

boardgame: Not Alone: The Yellow Artefact Promo Cards
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Yellow Artefact promo cards are composed of 1 survival card, 1 hunt card and 4 new place (#10) cards.

The survival and the hunt cards may be added to their respective stack.

The new place card is a new version of the artefact and replaces the one of the base game.

Two promo Spirits for Spirit Island, printed as part of the initial Kickstarter. Includes two Spirit Mats and eight Unique Power cards. When in print, it is available directly from Greater Than Games.

Heart of the Wildfire is a joyous Spirit of fire, destruction, and (maybe eventually) renewal. Mechanically, it's high-complexity, extreme-offense, and blights the land as it spreads.

Serpent Slumbering Beneath the Island is a slow-to-wake primal Spirit of the deep, deep earth. Mechanically, it's high-complexity and slow-developing with huge late-game payoffs.

Both spirits were released separately in Germany as: 

     Spirit Island: Seele des Flächenbrands
     Spirit Island: Unter der Insel schlummernde Schlange
