The object of the game is to study a card for 10 seconds and then answer a question based on the roll of a die. If the question is answered correctly, the card is kept, and the person with the most number of cards after 5 or 10 minutes is the winner.

A party of heroic adventurers arrive at the entrance of the dungeon armor shining, weapons bristling, and trusty Torch Carriers in tow. The great doors open reluctantly then slam shut with a boom once the group is inside. A lock clicks into place locking the group in the darkness. The Torch Carriers light up their torches and shoulder their packs. The heroes quickly scan the entry and seeing nothing threatening sheath their weapons and stride toward the single arched doorway on the opposite side of the room. At the midpoint of the room the floor begins to glow as inscribed runes and markings fade into view. With a soundless flash the heroes are vaporized leaving behind a small cloud of cinders drifting through the air like dying fireflies.

Once the Torch Carriers get past the stunned silence of their hero's’ demise, they decide to complete the task set before the heroes, find the dungeon exit and return home!

You play the part of the Torch Carriers. Unlike most dungeon crawlers, your character is not an epic hero trained in unique skills, covered in protective armor and armed to the teeth with custom weapons. The Torch Carriers have a pack of basic supplies and a torch. In order to survive the dungeon, complete the task, and escape you will have to use what you can find as you go.

Torch Carriers is a “diceless” dungeon-crawling party-based adventure game for 1-6 players. The Chapter-based story system ensures that no two games will ever be alike.

Built on Wrectangle Games “Card Chaos” Gameplay Engine, Torch Carriers allows you to play world-class RPG adventures dictated by the cards you draw rather than the dice you roll.

Players are English merchants circa 1590 - 1600’s financing marine voyages to seek treasure in India and SE Asia.

Each player may have a max of 4 expeditions in play at one time. Each expedition must have one or more (to a maximum of 4) ships.   Expeditions can also have trade goods or silver to trade for cargo. They can also have gifts to give to foreign rulers, and great Captains to improve their chance of success. Expeditions are created using cards drawn from an “expedition” deck of 60 cards.

Each player has an expedition mat divided into 4 sections numbered 1 to 4. These correspond to each of the 4 possible expeditions the player may have going at the same time.  The expedition and treasure cards of a particular expedition are placed in its own section.

Play is conducted on an expedition track that is 40 spaces long. There is a start space (England) and an end space (Pacific Ocean).  The spaces in between represent locations or event that may a have a positive, negative or no effect on the expedition. When starting out, an expedition moves towards the end space.  When reaching the End space or any point before then, the expedition may turn back. When an expedition finally returns, its treasures go into the expedition’s profit pile, and all its expedition cards are discarded.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 3 phases:

Expedition Phase

     Draw 2 cards from the expedition deck and discard 1 of them.  Add the card to your expedition pile. 


Launch Phase

     If your expedition pile contains at least 1 ship, you may launch an expedition. 
     If your expedition pile has 8 cards you must launch, unless you don’t have a ship, in which case you must discard your entire expedition pile, and gain 1 treasure. 
     Place an expedition counter onto the start space of the expedition track and place all the expedition cards from the pile into the corresponding section of your expedition mat. 


Action Phase

     In order of expedition number, move your expeditions along the track by rolling 1D6 and moving the expedition that number of spaces. Each space offers one possibility, helpful, or hurtful: trade, resupply, hardship, storm, capture ship, gift, cotton or naught. Spaces are resolved accordingly.


The game ends after 2 hours, or when the treasure deck runs out. The player with the most Treasure Points (TP) in their profit pile at that time wins.

'Let your inner demons out to play'

A party card game of polemics and moral descent.

Suspend your moral and ethical values in this adult card game, where players argue against each other to defend a host of depraved and damnable acts.
Choose a sinful scenario from the stack of Case Cards and play devil’s advocate to fabricate a winning case, or inflict an unholy deed on your opponent and watch him squirm before the Judge!
Not for the pure or faint-hearted, this game will test the limits of the unscrupulous mind.

Features:
178 Case Cards featuring possible scenarios.
20 Clause cards.
Game Rules
Small size box suitable for travel and social gatherings

boardgame: Rail Raiders Infinite: Huckleberry's Riverboat Roll
description:
Description from the publisher:

Huckleberry's Riverboat Roll is an exciting addition to the sci-fi western board game, Rail Raiders Infinite. When players are ready to take a break from snagging booty and ducking the Lawbots in Rail Raiders Infinite, they can gather around the poker table and play to take their fellow player's double dollars.

Huckleberry's Riverboat Roll is a betting dice game, the object of the game is to win every other player's $$ tokens, using the custom poker dice. Players achieve this by playing a series of rounds, where players roll their dice and build a hand, bet on the hand, and then either win by having the best hand or by having every other player fold. Players' hands stay hidden behind the beautifully illustrated double-sided dice blinds and are only revealed once the betting is done.

Description from the publisher:

The first expansion to Expedition: The Roleplaying Card Game.

Battle against all-new Cthulhu-inspired enemies and grow your character with the new "Persona" mechanic. Will your adventurer be brave and bold in the face of danger, or will cower in fear and hopelessness?

As your persona changes through abilities, enemies and the story, your adventurer could burst forth in courage, dealing extra damage or boosting allies. Or, as you suffer from stress and fear, you could suffer roll penalties or take additional damage.

This game is the Junior Edition of The Bible Game.

From the instructions:
The Junior Bible Game will be a welcome addition to any family, Church, or Sunday School group. The game provides an opportunity to learn the Bible in a fun and challenging way.
Families will especially appreciate the 5 different levels, allowing young children to play alongside older brothers and sisters. Sunday School and Junior Church leaders will find the game to be a popular addition to their program.

There are a total of seven categories in The Bible Game:
1 Life of Christ
2 New Testament
3 Old Testament
4 W.W.W. (Who am I? Where is it? Women of the Bible)
5 Quotes
6 Alpha and Omega
7 General Knowledge

Neon Dawn is a character building, open-world, post-apocalyptic adventure board game for 1-5 players.  It is set in the Waste, a post nuclear land steeped in radiation and full of danger, adventure and treasures to be found.  The Great War happened long enough ago that no one alive remembers what life used to be like.  Those that cling to life work hard to simply survive, overcoming challenges within the Waste every single day.  This is where you intend to make a name for yourself.  Your goal is to explore and survive the Waste, while becoming the most famous adventurer in the process!

Neon Dawn is an adventure game at its core, including 400 mini stories that can be experienced by adventurers of the Waste.  Players initially build out their character through a draft, then continue to develop their character through Items, Consumables and Traits obtained during game play, as well as the Companion you choose to adventure with.  Players can explore the world, salvage materials, build reputation with settlements, trade, craft Items, build buildings (for Fame and reputation) and complete Missions.

Neon Dawn was designed to be easy to learn, yet still provide deep game play and a thematic story experience.  Due to the variance in the cards, map tiles and dice rolls, each play through of Neon Dawn will provide a unique story experience specifically for the players at the table.  No two stories will be alike! (statistically, it's nearly impossible)

In this small promotional expansion an alternate city deck is provided. 
These cards can be used instead of the ones provided with the game. This deck has 10 cards in it. These could even be shuffled in for an even longer campaign of find land mines with mad cows (however, the card backs are different from those in the base game).

The new cards are:

     Cairo (2): Take any cow from the discard pile and play it into another player's herd.
     Istanbul (3): You may take any number of Spies from the table into your hand.
     London (4): Every player with at least one card in their hand passes one card to the left.
     Los Angeles (1): Take 200 from every player.
     Moscow (1): Draw four cards. Pass one of them to the player on your left.
     New York (2): Take 400 from another player.
     Paris (4): Choose a random card from the normal city deck and follow its instructions.
     Rome (5): Every player who has at least one cow passes one cow to the left.
     Sydney (3): Swap hands with another player.
     Tokyo (5): Take 200 from the pot. Put any cow on the table into the discard pile.

This postcard sized expansion is placed 4 cards away from the starting card in such a way that it is also one card away from center goal card. 
The first player to connect to this card is immediately forced to exchange their role card. They place their card back in the box and take the one that was set aside(saboteur base game) or a new card of the leftover roles cards from the deck (Saboteur 2)

Promotional card from Renegade Game Studios offered as a reward for the Season 7 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

French edition of the card found in Plato Magazine 111.

When the card first arrives in the market, All players get +1 Clank! The card costs 5 Skill, is worth 1 point, and when played, it generates 3 gold for person who played it, and all other players get +1 Clank!

Future Sarah has fractured the timelines and it's up to you, an earlier-version Sarah, to solve missions and set things right. Bring dinosaurs to Ancient Egypt and ComiCon panelists to Feudal Japan while trying to rescue lost companions scattered in time. Put companions to work in their own times to complete missions. You've got seven time jumps to complete as many missions as possible, but watch out for paradoxes; if two or more Sarahs meet in the same time zone, everything about them will change in an instant and all your plans could go up in smoke.

In Sarah's Singularity, players select an assortment of time periods from prehistoric Pangea to ComicCon 2012 and establish a set of missions for each period. Stranded companions from far-ranging times are scattered among the chosen periods and each player gets a "Sarah Card" with special powers. Finally, players choose two secret objective cards for hidden endgame scoring and a Chronologist is selected from the gathered players.

Each round, Future Sarah visits a time period to strand another companion and the players simultaneously and secretly select a time period to jump to where they hope to solve a mission by matching icons on the mission card with icons on the stranded companion cards and the icons on their own hand of helpful companions. Typically, when the selected periods are revealed, they're resolved in chronological order. However, if two or more Sarahs (including the Future Sarah) land in the same time period, they paradox! Those player immediately turn in their Sarah Cards and get a new one at random, losing the special powers they may have planned to use to help solve the mission. The Chronologist then decides the order in which the paradoxing players gets to take their turns. The paradoxing player who goes last gets to be the new Chronologist.

Players claim solved missions as victory points. They also gain bonus points and a wild icon token for rescuing a stranded companion and hidden endgame points through their secret objective cards. After seven rounds, the points are tallied and a winner is announced.

Description from the designer:

Guildbreakers: Battle for Agawan is a two-player tower defense card game. It plays like a miniatures game, but it needs only a minimum of table space and can fit perfectly in your pocket. You assume the role of a Guild Master, scrambling to stake your claim on the valuable ore deposits of Agawan Island. You must build up your base, recruit your troops, and smash down your opponent's base before he does the same to you. This fast-paced strategy game distills concepts from miniatures games, collectible card games, and real-time strategy games into a quick and portable card game.

Battle for Agawan's streamlined game system is a card game that has no shuffling and a tactical combat game that has no board, no rulers, and no dice rolling. Instead, players can freely select cards from their own customized army pool of twenty Base and Character cards. Base cards build up your rate of production while Character cards are used for engaging in combat. Special Character abilities and stats, combined with a deterministic support-based combat system, encourage mixed forces and dynamic counterplay. Battle for Agawan uses neither grid-based maps nor measurement-based movement, instead abstracting the field of battle into four simple rows. This minimalism allows for extremely quick setup and portability while preserving the core tactical elements of movement, range, and positioning.

Description from the publisher:

This is a solo PnP ninja card game. 
Game can be played in easy and normal mode.
Easy mode is created to be accessible for the younger players (6+) and this can be a good way to keep your offspring occupied  if you need 15 minutes to do something else.
On normal difficulty this game should be challenging enough to trouble even younger players born in 1987 or before it.

Player uses Hero Cards in order to break through the horde of minions and sidekicks, trying to eliminate two villains. No book keeping, dice or counters needed.

Game is monochrome for the ease of printing. Game contains 4 sheets of card + 4 pages long rule-book.

This game haven´t got any theme.

The players have a few marbles and in their turn, they have to throw them trying to make 3 in line or more combinations. Some combinations give you differents points and the person who reach 50 points, win

Description from the publisher:

Next-Generation Role-Playing Board Game Adventure. Soul Shard is the simplest role-playing board game adventure. You can pick up to 5+ characters including mage,rogue,warrior etc.. and start your journey. You can slay enemies for gaining experience and equipment. Chat with town folk to get useful information and quests etc.. This is your game if you like Role-Playing games, simplicity. While you can play mini scenarios for shorter sessions (like mini dungeons/quest lines), also main scenario can take you away to another universe for years.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Oberth Class Card Pack
description:
The U.S.S. Grissom is a Federation Oberth class ship.  As a card pack, it comes with cards, token and a dial.

It is a part of card pack wave 1.

Description from the publisher:

Expand your studies with four powerful, new assignment cards, four new tiles, and custom-molded, plastic exhaustion tokens and gems to add new challenges to your game in the Arcane Academy: Advanced Arithmancy expansion.

boardgame: Kill Doctor Lucky: Doctor Lucky's Mansion That is Haunted
description:
Description from the publisher:

Doctor J. Robert Lucky wishes to sell his haunted house to a pair of unsuspecting newlyweds from across town — but the ghosts of Lucky Mansion have a different plan. They love Doctor Lucky so much, they want him to stay with them forever!

With the Doctor Lucky's Mansion That Is Haunted expansion board, the players are ghosts, so they can move through ceilings, walls, and floors! Any two rooms are connected as long as they share an edge, not just a corner. Players can see from their current room into all the connected rooms, but no farther. What's more, the board contains three secret portals that connect two rooms, represented by duplicate objects (the raven, globe, and portrait); you can move through these portals, but you can't see through them.

This game board can be folded in half to create different play spaces with at most 4-5 players in the game, or the entirety of it can be used for games with up to eight. Whenever the number of players is close to the upper limit of the board, you should probably play with the Cat; if the board seems too roomy, you should add the Dog instead.

boardgame: The Spooks Journey Game II: The Killer Sequel
description:
Description from the publisher:

More Spooks are awaiting to meet you... Summer Camp is open and that means Campfire Tales will be told to Spook the little ones! Each of the 8 camp counselors tell a tale about a Spook; the Evil Wizard, real-life T-Rex, the infested Gator Swamp, Maniac in the woods, Haunted Toys, the Wicked Witch, creepy Clowns and horrifying Aliens. Will YOU be able to survive each of the Campfire Tales?

Roll the die and make your way around the game board encountering a Spook. Complete the task for each Spook to win the game! Collect items along the way to help you on your Journey. Overcome obstacles along the way that hinder your path to become the winner!

The players have to move their three pawns on the track of their color from space 1 to space 50. Movement is determined by the "Verolette" in the center of the game board. The "Verolette" gives 8 different results, symbolized by an animal:

ant: 14 spaces forward
donky: 6 spaces back
cat: 10 spaces forward
snake: 8 spaces back
dog: 12 spaces forward
snail: 2 spaces back
bee: 16 spaces forward
pigs: 4 spaces back

From space 50 to 52 the pawns advance 1 space at the spinn of an ant or a bee. The first player to get his 3 pawns on the spaces 51 to 53 wins the game.

boardgame: King of Tokyo/New York: Monster Pack – King Kong
description:
He is not happy! King Kong is ready to conquer the Empire State Building and Tokyo Tower to find his love. Will you dare cross his path, or worse, capture his beloved?

The King Kong Monster Pack adds new elements to two games: Tokyo Tower for King of Tokyo and the Empire State Building for King of New York!

Evolution cards let you play King of Tokyo and King of New York with supercharged monsters! King Kong's Evolutions work like the Evolutions in King of Tokyo: Power Up! and King of New York: Power Up! To play with King Kong's Evolutions, you need the Power Up! expansion for whichever base game you have.

One of King Kong's Evolutions introduces the Beauty card, which has two sides that have different effects according to which monster owns the card.

Part of the King of Tokyo series.

Description from the publisher:

Easy as one-two-three. Whoever counts the hedgehogs and birds the fastest yells “Got it” and collects the harvest! The originally designed game set in compact packaging with bright an unusual animal drawings is a great solution for this fun game and complementary for acquiring the basics of mathematics. Play and get smarter. This game is played in background mode, so you won’t have to convince your child to sit down at the table. Not only will children learn how to count, but they will instantly learn to define the number of objects on a card as well, which definitely separates this method of learning from cramming in a positive light. Adding within ten can easily become second nature to your child. HurriCount is a great supplement for developing your child’s attentiveness, logic, thinking speed, and reaction time. HurriCount has an application called HurriCount Mathitude where you will get to solve problems that are even more challenging. The cards from this game can be gradually added into HurriCount, making it more difficult, and you can also use it as a standalone playing set. When buying the two games at the same time, you will be able to receive a cut little bag that you can carry the games in.

Description from the publisher:

Signora ordered five eighths with tomatoes!

This game will make you sweat, as you’ll be working for the famous and respected Italian pizzeria called Splittissimo. Its fussy clients love to order pizza by slices. By playing this game you'll become such an expert in fractions that you'll be second to none.

The game set is designed in a charming Italian style. It includes both rectangular cards with orders and fascinating round cards. The latter are trays with different numbers of pizza slices. Apart from cheese and olives, pizzas include tomatoes, mushrooms, and peppers in various combinations; each one can have two additional ingredients.

Splittissimo offers the fastest delivery!

There are several versions of the game.

Let’s start with the easiest one. If you’re playing with children of five and older, use only round cards. Beginner level cards are marked with one dot (to make it more challenging, you can add cards with two or three dots later). Each player takes three cards and puts them face-up in front of him; the same number of cards is placed face-up on the table (playing by the rules of a more challenging version, the players draw 4 cards and 4 cards are placed on the table) The task is to use several playing field cards to assemble a pizza , so that the number of its slices would be equal to the number of slices in one of your pizzas. Or, conversely, you can choose one of the playing field pizzas and assemble it using several of your own cards.

Playing with older kids, add the rectangular order cards. The task is to assemble the ordered pizzas using the cards on the playing field, taking into account both their size and the ingredients. To add excitement to the game, use a number of special cards, which allow you to slow down your opponents’ work and accelerate your own pace using “last minute” orders.

All versions of the game end with counting the whole pizzas that players managed to put together. In other words, the players should make whole pizzas from the slices they have, which helps them to reinforce addition of fractions once again.

Etchessera is a chess-based abstract strategy game that utilizes draft-able armies in a medieval fantasy setting.  Before each game, players build their own chess army from a collection of 17 different pieces.  All of the original pieces from chess and checkers are available as well as several new ones such as a wizard, dragon, or guard.

During a player's turn, they choose to move or activate one of their pieces.  Many pieces are similar to classic chess pieces in which they capture others by moving onto the same square.  Some capture by jumping, and others capture via rolling a certain result on a die.

As in chess, the ultimate goal of the game is for each player to capture their opponent's King.  Similar to the classic game, there are opportunities for stale-mates that result in draws.

boardgame: Trans-TK (fan expansion for Trans Europa / TransAmerica)
description:
User summary
Trans-TK is a variation map for TransAmerica, which changes the playing scene to the Tokyo, Chia, Saitama, and Kanagawa prefectures.

This variation pack only includes the map and cards. You will need either Trans America or Trans Europa to play. Contents include a game board (4 fold A4) and 36 cards.

Publisher's summary
Trans-TKは東京、千葉、埼玉、神奈川を舞台にした「トランスアメリカ」のバリエーションマップです。

Description from the publisher:

Highlands is a solo boardgame set in middle ages Scotland, in a time of rival clans, factions, and kingdoms.  Play as a king/earl/jarl striving to gain reputation and power as you build your empire in the highlands.  Along the way, you will explore, decide how to expand, and encounter rival armies and a variety of creatures from the realm of the faes.

Highlands started as an attempt to create a Scythe-like solo gaming experience, but with a smaller footprint and less upkeep.  Start with a Castle, a King, and two Workers.  Then, hire more Workers, build a Cathedral, train a Soldier and a Knight, explore, encounter Faes, complete missions, and defend your Castle from the Enemy.

Highlands is played on a custom hex map 12 chess pieces, a 27 card Encounter deck, an 18 card Mission deck, and a handful of cubes.

Highlands won 4th place in the Best Overall Game category in the 2017 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

Description from the publisher:
Operation Icarus is a simulation covering a hypothetical German campaign to occupy Iceland in World War 2.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Theme/backstory: In 587BC the Babylonian armies of Nebuchadnezzar broke through the walls of Jerusalem, burned the city and pillaged the Temple of Yahweh. Those in the city who survived the siege were deported 1500km to Babylon.

The people of Israel are bleeding. Their capital is destroyed, they are exiled from their land and Yahweh has abandoned them, or so it seems.

Nursing broken hearts, the pious of Israel gather on the banks of the Euphrates River. They comfort each other, and turn to the writings of the prophet Jeremiah. Perhaps they will gain understanding before all their hope drains away ...

Game mechanics: Co-operative card game with a CCG/LCG feel. The box comes with all the cards you need for four starter decks (and a few other cards too). The starter decks are reasonable, but as you improve you will want to craft your own. Unlike other co-op CCG/LCGs there is no 'monster deck' or 'encounter deck' instead there is some very careful shared resource management required.

User summary
仔ぶた急便 (Piglet's Delivery Service)
Once upon a time Mother Pig asked her piglets to delivery some packages. In order to deliver the packages, the piglets decided to form their own delivery companies.

When delivering a package for receipt confirmation they received a hoof print rather than a signature. Each of the piglets' companies compete to see who makes the speediest deliveries.

In this game, the goal is to move the three pig pieces to the four home bases (other than your own) and collect four hoof print chips before anyone else.

Each player controls three piglets, starting with all three piglet pieces placed on their home base. There are a total of 5 home bases, one for each player from the center.

On their turn, players also draw animal tiles and add them to their hand. After drawing a tile, the player carries out all of the following actions, in any order, during their turn: moving a piglet, placing an animal tile, and returning a piglet. Throw away tiles to ensure a player’s hand does not exceed three tiles. The game ends when a player successfully moves their piglets to the other four home bases and collects all the hoof prints to win the game.

Moving a piglet - This action, in combination with the below action (placing an animal tile), can only be carried out a maximum of three times during a single turn (e.g., place one animal tile + move 2 spaces).  A player may move their piglet one space in any direction. The player can also use the secret passage spaces to travel even further. However, except for the home base spaces, a piglet cannot move to a space that is already occupied by another piglet. In addition, if a piglet moves onto a space with an animal tile placed on it, they must obey the law of that tile. If a piglet successfully moves to a home base, they can receive the hoof print from that base.

Placing an animal tile - This action, in combination with the above action (moving a piglet), can only be carried out a maximum of three times (e.g., place two animal tiles + move 1 spaces). A player places an animal tile from their hand onto an open space. An open space is a space where no animal tiles or piglets are currently placed. However, some tiles can take the place of other tiles and some tiles cannot be placed on some spaces.

Returning a piglet - This action can be used an unlimited number of times per turn. Move one piglet that is not currently placed on your home base back to your home base. At the same time, you can add a tile (without a piglet piece placed on top) into your hand.

ある日、お母さんぶたは仔ぶたたちに荷物運びのお手伝いを頼みました。子ぶたたちは荷物を運ぶために運送会社を作りました。荷物を渡したら、サインの代わりに足跡のハンコを貰います。
さあ競争です。誰よりも早くみんなの家に荷物を運んでしまいましょう。
このゲームの目的は、3匹の仔ぶたコマを動かして自分以外の4か所の拠点を回り、他の誰よりも早く4つの足跡チップを集めることです。

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Cerberus Occultist
description:
Description from the publisher:

Known for their obsessive need to study, the students of House Cerberus have a tendency to delve in knowledge that is best left alone. Unfortunately, this causes some promising students to descend into madness. The truly gifted, however, learn to tame this dark knowledge and unleash it against the foes of Crystalia.

The adventurous girls of Takoashi have barely gotten into summer, and already they want to go back to school! Schooling monsters that is! Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Cerberus Occultist comes fully equipped, complete with a hero card, cast in luxurious resin as a multi-part kit.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Siren Enforcer
description:
Description from the publisher:

Few can resist the allure of the students of House Siren. Trained in the arts of charm, persuasion, and manipulation, graduates of House Siren go on to positions of power throughout the realms. It is a foolish adversary who dismisses a student of House Siren as a mere diplomat or sophisticate. Wielding massive hammers, these powerful students are trained to knock you out with more than their looks.

The adventurous girls of Takoashi have barely gotten into summer, and already they want to go back to school! Schooling monsters that is! Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Siren Enforcer comes fully equipped, complete with a hero card, cast in luxurious resin as a multi-part kit.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Unicorn Diviner
description:
Description from the publisher:

The adorable students of House Unicorn prefer to avoid combat whenever they are able. On the rare occasion an adventuring party can coax a House Unicorn student to join them, they are gifted with a unique and powerful ally. Staying at a safe distance from the fray, the House Unicorn student provides potent magical confections that delight the tastebuds and entice their party to unparalleled acts of heroism!

The adventurous girls of Takoashi have barely gotten into summer, and already they want to go back to school! Schooling monsters that is! Super Dungeon Explore: Takoashi University – House Unicorn Diviner comes fully equipped, complete with a hero card, cast in luxurious resin as a multi-part kit.

LOST GEMMA W is a reimplementation of the designer's earlier game Lost Gemma, but with enough changes to be a new game.

LOST GEMMA W is card game with a theme of treasure hunting. The goal of the game is to find a route that leads to a "Gemma" (which means "gem" in Latin) as soon as possible. However, there are two Gemma in the game, and the player who reaches the farther Gemma is the true winner.

Before exploration, players take turns playing a card to create a map of the labyrinth. Some cards have a special effect that changes the game in various ways.

•••

プレイヤー全員でカードで地図をつくっていき、ゲーム中に設定したスタート地点から、隠された宝石（Gemma）まで早くたどり着くのを目指すゲーム。ただし、宝石はゲームに２つあり、スタート地点からより遠くにある宝石にたどり着いたプレイヤーが真の勝者です。
地図を作る時、各カードに特殊な効果がついており、色々な展開が起こるようにもなっています。

In おにぎりさん　(Onigiri-San), players work together to make onigiri (Japanese rice balls) using cards that show seaweed, rice, or one of three different fillings.  To win, players must create one of each type of onigiri in front of each player.

On each turn, the active player must either (1) play one of their hand cards onto another player's tableau; or (2) "eat" (i.e., discard) a completed onigiri from their tableau and then discard and replace one of their hand cards.  Played cards are replaced from the draw deck.  Discarded cards are not reshuffled when the draw deck runs out.

The game ends either when a player is unable to act on their turn (a loss) or when each player has one of each nigiri in front of them (a win).

Description from the publisher:

A Party game for the rude and well-read.

Everyone knows that the only thing better than reading a book is pretending you've read it at a party. So gather some friends, some booze, and play Papercuts, a party game for the rude and well-read. To play, the Editor reads a question card, and the Writers submit their funniest answer cards for the Editor to choose from. Then, everyone complains about how no one will publish their genius novel.

Papercuts is compatible with Cards Against Humanity and other similar games.

Round and square - red, yellow, blue. Where are the square, triangle and circle hidden? 
The three friends, monkey, rabbit and cat invite you to a journey through the world of colors and shapes. With colorful toys, the child first learns the basic colors and gradually acquires the most important forms. The children sort the colors apart. In the next step, the small players assign shapes. They play on one of many differen game boards, which the child himself has chosen. Where do the circle, triangle and quadrangle belong? Now the monkey, rabbit and cat have their appearance: in the combination of color and shape knowledge, the children organize the forms on different picture boards appropriately. Depending on the development step, additional shapes or the dice will come into play. Through numerous game variations the children in Meine Farben und Formen strengthen basic basic knowledge.

For 2 players. One player is in charge of the two miners, Alf and Jack, and the other player is in charge of the three outlaws.
The cards and discs should be placed appropriately on the board. Two pyragons are used. The players throw to decide who will start and the one with the highest score begins.
The men may move forwards or backwards. One man may be moved  the total number thrown or two men may be moved according to the number of each pyragon. If a red is thrown, the player must miss a turn. If a green is thrown he has another turn.

Miners.
The object of the game for the miners is to collect as many sacks of gold from their mines as they can.

Outlaws.
The object of the game for the outlaws is to rob the miners of their gold.

Winner.
The winner is the player with most gold when the mines are empty.

This one of a series of games designed to help the beginner reader learn to read words in different situations.

Description from the publisher:

In our opinion a successful party game should be three things: hilariously entertaining, easy enough for drunk people to understand and personally revealing. There are loads of dirty party games out there, but not many that also have a "getting to know you" element. And that's where we come in.

QWDG is pretty simple. It's a deck of personal questions that range from thought-provoking to ridiculous, awkward to obscene. When a card is drawn, everyone writes their own answer to the question. Then one person tries to correctly identify who wrote each answer.

To sum up: QWDG is a dirty party game for people who want to show off their disgusting sense of humor and prove how well they know their friends.

Exclusive Kickstarter expansion adding a new boss, new stages and more.

Components:
1 Oni Miniature
1 Shin Oni Miniature
10 Yurei Ninja Miniature
5 Kitsune Miniatures
2 Double-sided map tiles
4 Stage Decks (84 cards)
1 Enemy Deck (21 cards)
Story Mode Cards
Reference Card
Tokens
Rulebook

This is the expansion you've been waiting for!
As a tribute to the anniversary of our favorite arcade games, join Axel and Rhys as they take down the Onix Gang plaguing Ransom City.

Included in the expansion:
16 Miniatures
2 Fighter Decks
1 Double-sided map tile
2 Stage Decks
Tokens
1 Enemy deck
2 Hero Story decks
Rulebook

boardgame: Dragonfire: Adventures – Chaos in the Trollclaws
description:
Chaos brews in the Trollclaws as gnolls, orcs, and goblins clash in conflicts that endanger the countryside. Face new Encounters and claim new Magic Items and Market cards in the second chapter of the Dragonfire Adventures!

Contents:

    30 Encounter Cards
    12 Market Cards
    9 Magic Item Cards
    5 Dragonfire Cards
    2 Character Screens
    18 Stickers
    1 Adventure
    1 Rules Sheet
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A cheerful introduction to reading for children 6 and over.

Lese-Memory is a memory game in which you have to find pairs made up of matched pictures, pictures and corresponding words or split syllables of a larger word.

144 cards included in the game.

Description from the publisher:

This Expansion features as primary mechanic a "Push-Your-Luck" (PYL) mechanic in a "Race"-type scenario.  Players will be racing against each other to be the first player to earn 500 credits to win this NEW scenario.  During the game, players will take on a NEW and "Special" Mission called "Smuggler's Run" and in doing so may exchange cards for various tracks to UNLOCK a given dice.  With a maximum of five (5) dice which can be UNLOCKED, plus a BONUS 1D20 when you unlock two (2) or more dice results in the possibility of Scoring 60 Credits.  But as this is a Trade Mission ... You must be wary that your Tradeship does NOT get destroyed as you collect more valuable illegal Cargo during this mission while avoiding "losing it all" in the event that your Tradeship gets destroyed.

Remember this Expansion is build upon the framework of the "Planetary Expansion" and not only the "core" game.  However at this present time we feel that it may be incompatible with the Tactical Core (Future Expansion currently not available). Both expansions add a certain depth to the game which alter the way the game is played.  Because of the contrasting winning conditions, these two expansions cannot be played simultaneously.

Additionally this Expansion offers update Player Game Pads (Horizontal) which are more compatible with the NEW "Smuggler's Cache" Game Pads which are exclusive and personalized for each Faction in the game.  This also adds a bit of variability in Game Play allowing players to TRY the various Factions and their Resources for a different experience (in terms of resources).

User summary
小人さんのやさい畑 (Kobito-san no Yasai Batake) is a game about little people growing crops. Kobito-san means 'small beings', akin to Halfling or dwarvish people in a fairy tale. Yasai Batake would be a vegetable farm or field. In this context 'Little People's Vegetable Patch' is probably a close match.

The object of the game is to plant, grow, and harvest the vegetables. Successfully harvested vegetables grant you points. The game itself has a simultaneous action choice with butting mechanics. Butting is the Japanese gaming term for matching values cancelling out, as in games like Hols der Geier.

There are 3 types of cards: Plant card (green border) / Supply card (yellow border) / Buy-in card (white border). Each turn, every player chooses a card from their hand, and reveals it simultaneously. If any players have played the same kind of Plants, those Plants are cancelled, dumped to the discard pile, and their turn is skipped.

Any remaining vegetables are played on the field, "slow growing" ones first. You place your chips around your planted vegetable, the same number as the "required turns to harvest" value.

Then the supply cards come in to action. Shovel icon cards have an one-time instant effect, resolve it and discard. Trap icon cards may be placed on the field with your chip as an identifier.

Finally, resolve the Buy-in cards. You may draw cards from the deck, but the more players that played Buy-in the same time, the fewer card you may draw. If you are the only one playing, you draw 3 cards. If 2 players are playing, you both draw 2. Otherwise, each player may draw 1 card. The played Buy-in card is returned to your hand.

After that come the Planting phase, the Saboteur phase and Grow/Harvest phase.

At the Saboteur phase, the Saboteur (some destructive animals) moves in the field. If the Saboteur runs over any of the vegetables, or chips that belong to a vegetable, that vegetable is wasted, and removed from the field.

Then the Grow/Harvest phase. For each vegetable, pick a chip that belongs to that vegetable, and stack it on the vegetable card. If there are no chips left on the field for that card, it is considered grown and may be harvested to score you points.

Publisher's summary
Place your hobbits in the vegetable patch and grow, harvest your vegetables!

Kobito-san no Yasai Batake is a game where you place your hobbits in the game field and harvest the vegetables. The first player to harvest the predetermined amount of vegetables is the winner.

The field is small. You may need to race to get a better area. Should you work steadily on a small, quick and easy-to-grow vegetable? Or a slow but huge one? It's up to you. But beware, Saboteur animals maybe lurking nearby. They maybe after your hard-grown vegetables, so watch out!

畑に小人さんを配置してやさいを収穫しよう！

「小人さんのやさい畑」はフィールドに小人さんを配置してやさいを収穫するゲームです。いち早く、規定量のやさいを収穫できた人が勝者となります。

土地は狭いため取り合いになってしまうかもしれません。小さくコツコツと育てるか、大きく大逆転を狙うかはあなた次第。

しかし、畑にはお邪魔物がいます。せっかく育てたやさいを台無しにされないように気を付けてください。

There are two sets of composer discs numbered from 1 to 36. Place numbers 1 through 18 on the board in random order. Use the set of cards numbered 1 through 18 with the composers' names on them. Place these composer cards, which correspond to the discs on the board, in a pile with the names of the composers showing. Each player takes his turn identifying the composer whose name is on the top card by calling out the number which appears on the disc with the picture of the composer whose name is on the card.

And eduactional memory game.

Play with gangsters and cops using cards.
On one hand, there are 4 gangs with a godfather leading them. On the other side, 15 cops are trying to avoid the gangsters to rob a bank.

Gangster et Compagnie is played in two teams. The dealer, the player with the bank and the player with the bomb are one team. All the others are the second team.
These teams play tricks. In a trick where the first card is a Gang or Police, other players can follow or play whatever (if they can't follow). In a trick where the first card is a Bank or Bomb, nobody can play a card. The round ends a soon a player played his last card.
The team with most points in the tricks, win the round.

Take an exiting journey across the Wetterau, a broad fertile landscape in the western German state of Hessen!

In Meine Goldene Wetterau, players guide a single hiker along the paths through the cities and communities of the Wetterau, trying to direct the hiker to locations that match the cards they hold in hand. Each player has a hand of four cards, with the remaining cards being placed next to the game board as a deck. On a turn, a player takes one or both of these actions in this order:


    Move the hiker along the path 1-3 locations in a direction of their choice.
    Play location cards from their hand that match where the hiker is located or where it started the turn.


At the end of their turn, a player refills their hand to four cards. Play continues until the deck runs out and one player has no cards left in hand. Whoever has played the most location cards wins!

From the publisher's webpage:

Combat Leader: Poland 1939 is the newest release in the Combat Leader game series, covering the Polish campaign on September, 1939, with German and Polish troops.

The game comes with two maps (#9 and #10, rural and suburban terrain), two counter sheets (J & K, which must be cut apart), two identical Reference Cards, and a 24-page rules booklet.  The rules contain all the standard, advanced, and optional rules for the system, along with five scenarios.  This game sees the entry of the Poles into the system, and special rules cover the use of the 18-man Polish squad, with their characteristic weaponry (Kb wz.98a rifles, Browning wz. 1928 BAR LMG, etc.) and organization. The German SS also are introduced into the system (a full SS squad is included), with special rules governing their use on the battlefield. Overall, three German squads, two Polish squads--plus plenty of extra men to create different formations--are included.

The core rules have been expanded to include several options originally appearing in the Combat Leader: Expansion Module.  These rules are now  considered standard, and there is no need to buy that module.

Combat Leader: Poland is a complete, stand alone game. The core rules of the game system remain the same, plus additional optional rules have been included. So, Combat Leader: Poland 1939 provides you with not only a new nationality and the SS, but the most up-to-date edition of the series.  It is compatible with all other Combat Leader games.

Dice Derbi is a dexterity dice tossing game where players form teams, casting lures (dice) onto one of two game boards, trying to hit the honey hole and catch the largest fish.  Based on fishing yet easily played by all ages.

The musical cassette game. Correctly answer questions on the provided cassettes in a race to reach the finish space first. Questions may be musical clues, spoken lyrics, multiple choice, complete the lyric, or identify the year.

Description from the publisher:

In Wraith, you must recruit champions and use your magic to reach into cross over to The Other Side and bring powerful spirits back to the land of the living. Once summoned, your wraiths may fight along side you or merge with you and your champions to give you supernatural abilities, armor, and more! Face off against your friends with competitive battles or team up to stop the encroaching darkness with both campaign and defense co-op play.

Wraith is a fast-paced deck-building game for 1-4 players that focuses on recruiting and building up champions to defeat your opponents. The game features three modes of game play: competitive versus, co-op campaign, and defensive co-op. The campaign and defense variations can also be played solo.

Description from the publisher:

Judgmental is a party game in which famous figures do battle and players decide who wins. Each player secretly chooses a contender card from their hand, and those cards are dealt into a bracket. Match-ups are decided by unique and silly contests, and a vote by all players determines the victor. You win if your chosen contender gets all the way to the finals and wins the vote.

boardgame: Palaeo Diet: Eat or be Eaten
description:
Palaeo Diet: Eat or be Eaten  is a stand-alone set of gaming rules for recreating prehistoric animal hunts on your table top. The game models a time when humans were not yet in control of the world around them, when the landscape could just as easily give succour to a struggling tribe, as it could cripple a thriving people.

Designed with solo and multiplayer games in mind, players take on the role of the hunting party while an integral response mechanism means that beasts react in different ways in different situations.
family: Series: Song of Blades and Heroes

description:
A board game for 2-4+ players themed on the Latin American revolutions 1809 - 1826.

The board is a circular track of 41 spaces.  Eight countries are represented on the track by a series of consecutive spaces of length 3, 4 or 5. Countries are separated from each other on the track by a revolution space

Players share a common deck comprising cards of two main types: Liberation cards and Royalist cards. There are 5 types of Liberation Cards: Simon Bolivar, Leader, Troops, Battle, and Ideals. All cards have a force value between 1 and 8.

Players start with two cards drawn from the deck and a set of counters and markers.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 5 Phases:

Draw Phase

     Draw 1 Card and place it in your hand. 


Move Phase

     Roll 1 die and move forward that many spaces OR discard a card from your hand and move forward a number of spaces equal to its force value. 
     If you land on a Revolution space you may do one of three actions: 
         Roll the die and move again
         Move to any other Revolution space on the board
         Draw 1 card and place it in your hand
         Play a counter onto any country space you have liberated
    


Action Phase

      If you land on a Country space that has no card on it, you may (but do not have to) play a Liberation card to that space. Tuck it partially under the board directly next to the space. Place 1 of your counters on the space if you placed a card there. If you have a card under a space you are said to have liberated it. 
     If you land on a Country space that you have already liberated (You have a card and one or more counters there already) you may do one of the following: 
         Place an additional counter on the Space
         Exchange the tucked card with one from your hand  
         Draw 1 card and discard 1 card (It may be the same card) 
    
     If you land on a Country space liberated by another player, you can either do nothing or play 1 or more Royalist cards with a combined force value equal to or greater than the number of counters your opponent has on the space. If you do so, discard all the counters on the space and the card tucked under it. Remove the Royalist cards from the game. 
     If you land on a Country space that is Independent skip over it and go to the next space and resume your move. 


Independence Phase

     If you played a Liberation card under a Country space this turn and all the other spaces for that country have already been liberated (by you or others) the Country is now said to have gained its Independence. Immediately score Victory Tokens (VT) for the country. 
         You gain 2 Victory Tokens for playing the final card to make the country go Independent. 
         The player with the highest combined total of counters and card force values gets 5 VT. 
         The player with the second highest total of counters and card force values gets 3 VT. 
         All other players with at least 1 card and counter in the country get 1 VT each. 
    


End Phase

     Max hand size is 5 cards. Discard excess cards. 


The game ends when all countries have been liberated. The player with the most VT at the end of the game wins.

A rummy style card game for 2-4+ players with a Dr Who theme focused on Doctors 9-11.

Players share a common deck comprising 15 cards in each of 6 suits: Doctors, Companions, Gadgets, Foes, Plots and Specials. Cards have a force point value ranging from 1 to 15.

Players are dealt 7 cards each and then take turns with each turn comprising 4 phases:

Draw Phase

     Draw 2 cards from the top of the deck and put them in your hand. 


Action Phase

     You may play (discard) one card from your hand to activate its special ability. 
         Discard a Doctor card to look at the next 7 cards in the deck. 
         Discard a Companion card to draw 2 cards. 
         Discard a Foe card to steal 1 random card from an opponent’s hand. 
         Discard a Plot card to look at an opponent’s hand.  
         Discard a Gadget card to draw 3 cards, keep 1 and discard the other 2. 
         Discard a Special card to cause target opponent to discard 1 random card. 
    


Episode Phase

     If possible you may play (discard) one episode from your hand. An episode consists of one card of each of the 6 card types. The total value of the FP for the Doctor, Companion, and Gadget cards must exceed the total FP value of the Foe, Plot, and Special cards (The Threat). 
     Gain Victory Points equal to the value of the Threat. 
     After completing an episode draw 4 cards from the deck. 


End Phase

     Max hand size is 7 cards. Discard excess cards. 


The first player to earn 50 Victory Points is the winner.

Grab your friends to begin this addictive, fun generating, family game, combining a splash of poker, topped with a taste of Scrabble. An exciting new poker board game for the whole family, played by 2 to 4 people.

Players take turns, arranging poker hands, either horizontally or vertically on the board.

Features a funky felt playing board
Four Double Score and four Triple Score indicators
PokaMates playing cards, 80 PokaMates chips, Score sheet and Instruction sheet

A card and board skirmish game for 2 players themed on renaissance warfare with Leonardo da Vinci style inventions. The setting is Italian City States at war circa 1490 - the Fivizzano are invading the Piombino.

Play is conducted on a 16 x 16 square grid map. Each 8 x 8 section is referred to as a quadrant – the 2 eastern quadrants are land, the 2 western quadrants are water. The 6 squares of the NE corner of the SW quadrant contain city squares -  the Port City commune of Piombino. The remainder of the map layout is determined at discretion of the players though most of the land spaces should be flat land with a few clumps of forests and mountains and a few solitary hills, villages, and swamps. 
One road connects the city to a village or two and then goes off the map. A few Islands dot the Tyrrhenian Sea.  Terrain types will affect the movement of the different unit types.

There are 11 different unit types: Infantry, Engineers, Cavalry, Artillery, War Machine, Flying Machine, Fortification, Small Warship, Large Warship, Transport and Mine Field.  Each unit type has a move rating, hits score and range, and may have a special capability.

The players start with a pre-defined army comprising a mix of the various units.  The attacker (the Fivizzano) starts with his units in the bottom half of the Southern quadrants. In addition the Fivizzano gets one roll against the leader table and 4 rolls against the invention table – these give advantages / enhancements that last for the duration of the battle. The defender (the Piombino) starts with units scattered in the Northern quadrants, one roll against the leader table and 6 against the invention table.

Players share a Tactics deck comprising cards that mostly give short duration benefits.  These cards can be used, when appropriate, to enhance your own actions or negate those of your opponent

Players take turns with each turn comprising 4 Phases:

Draw Phase

     Draw 2 Tactics cards. 


Move Phase

     Each of your Units may be moved once up to a number of spaces equal to its move rating. 


Attack Phase

      Each of your units may make 1 attack vs. a target within its range. When attacking roll 1D10. On a roll of 7 or higher the attack is successful and the target loses 1 hit. A unit with zero hits is destroyed. 


End Phase 

     Max Hand size is 5 cards. Discard excess cards. 


The Fivizzano win if they occupy 4 of the spaces of the city. 
The Piombinos win if they destroy 20 or more units.

Rento is advanced business dice game. 
In the game you roll "Wheel Of Fortune", trade lands, build houses, win auctions, roll 2 metal dices and one special dice and most importantly - have fun.
The goal of Rento game is to bankrupt the other players by taking rents and becoming the richest player.

You have to predict chances, calculate risk and make smart trades. However that is not so easy because the game has strategic game tweaks that are dynamically changing the game, so you have to be careful.

Good luck!

A card and dice game for 1-5 players based on the Spore series of videogames. Each player is control of his own race as it evolves and develops from a single cell to spacefaring.

The game plays in 5 stages (Cell stage, Creature stage, Tribal stage, Civilization stage, Space stage) with each stage having a separate deck of trait cards, and event, challenge type and challenge result tables.  Players move from one stage to the next at different speeds and do not directly interact until the last stage, the Space stage. At the beginning of each stage, each player chooses 1 characteristic for his race – these characteristics improve a particular attribute for the race.

Players share a common set of Victory Point Tokens (VT), Food (Resource) Tokens (FT), Hunger Tokens (HT) and Death Tokens (DT). Each player starts with 1 VT and 3 FT.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 5 Phases:

Mutation Phase

     Draw 1 (or, possibly, more) card from the deck (of the stage you are in). 


Event Phase

     Roll once on the event table (of the stage you are in) and resolve the event. 


Selection Phase

     Most events are challenges between your species and another species that is either weaker, equal, or stronger than you. 
     To resolve a challenge first roll on the challenge type table to determine what attribute will affect the challenge.  Next Roll 1D6 (Attribution Roll) and add your level in the indicated Attribute. This is your Attribution Total. 
     Next roll 1D6, 2D6, or 3D6 depending on the Strength of the Challenger. 


This is the Challenge Roll. 

     If the Attribution Total is greater than the Challenge Roll, you win the Challenge. 


If it is equal or less, you lose the challenge. 

     Refer to that stage’s Challenge Result list to determine the benefits or consequences of winning or losing the challenge.
     You may attempt to evade a challenge. If you do so, add +4 to your attribution roll. 


However if you win, you get no reward.

Evolution Phase

     You may gain 1 Trait by playing 1 Trait card from your hand and paying its cost in food or resource tokens. The Trait card stays in play in front of you.  


Metabolic Phase

     Discard 1 Food Token. If you have no food tokens gain 1 hunger token. If you have 2 hunger tokens, discard them and gain 1 death token. 
     Max hand size is 5 cards. Discard excess cards. 
     Determine whether your race moves to the next stage:
         If you have 6 Trait cards in Cell stage in play go on Creature stage. 
         If you have 8 Trait cards in Creature stage in play go on Tribal stage. 
         If you have won against 5 villages in Tribal stage go onto Civilization stage. 
         If you have won against 7 nations in Civilization stage go onto Space Stage. 
    
     If going on to the next stage set your old Trait cards aside in your Progress Pile. Except for and VT they might score, these do not apply to later stages. Select a new characteristic for the new stage and discard down to 2 FT or RT. 


The game ends after any player has completed his 15th turn in the Space stage at which point scoring is undertaken:

         Gain 5 VT for completing the Civilization stage and entering the Space stage. 
         Add up the VT bonuses from all the Trait cards in your Progress Pile. 
         Each Death Token counts as a -2 VT penalty. 
         Add or lose VT arising from Events and Challenges. 
    


The player with the most Victory Point Tokens wins.

A card game for 2-4 players themed around the adventures of the demigod Hercules.  Each player tries to complete the most labours and adventures.

The board is a circular track divided into 12 spaces representing 12 countries or locations visited by Hercules. Each space has a specific set of instructions such as draw 1 card or take the top card of discard, draw two cards, next turn roll 2 dice to move and pick 1 etc.

There are 3 common decks of cards:

     The Labours deck has 12 cards representing the 12 labours of Hercules. Each has 3 to 6 requirements needed to complete it. The deck is constructed so that the easier labours come into play before the more difficult ones. Only four labours are revealed at a time – every time a labour is completed the next card in the deck is flipped over as a replacement.
     The Adventure deck is similar to the Labours deck but easier. Each adventure only needs 1 or 2 requirements to complete it. The Adventure deck is kept face-up. 
     The Hero Deck - each card provides 1 or 2 of the requirements needed to complete labours and adventures. 


Players start with 2 hero cards and take turns with each turn comprising 4 Phases:

Move Phase

     Roll 1D6 and move that many spaces in either direction. If you land on an opposing player steal 1 random Hero card from his hand and skip the action and labour phases. 


Action Phase

     If you did not land on another player, follow the instructions on the board space you entered. If you land on the Mediterranean you may complete the top face up Adventure card by discarding Hero cards with the necessary requirements. If completed, place the Adventure card in your Victory Pile. 


Labour Phase

     You may complete a revealed labour by discarding Hero cards with the 


necessary requirements. When completed place the labour card in your Victory Pile. 
Each Labour can only be completed if your pawn is in a specific space on the track (e.g. the Lion of Nemea can only be conducted in Greece, the Wild Mares of Diomedes in Thrace, the Golden Apples of Hesperides in Africa etc…)

     Once one player has completed a Labour it is finished and is no longer available to other players to complete.  


End Phase

     Max hand size is 7 cards. Discard excess cards. 


The game ends when all 12 labours have been completed. The player with the most labour points, gained by completing labours and adventures, wins.

Gallia Cisalpina, a tactics game for 2 players from 10 years, is game No. 8 of the series “Die Kleinen Feinen”. 
In 396 BC, the Celts set out to conquer the lands south of the Alps, but the Romans are also fighting for supremacy. Defences are set up in a hurry to protect agriculture and ensure the supply of the warriors. But the enemy is also armed.

Only 12 cards form the landscape, on which the enemy troops stand in the form of dice. 
In his turn, each player has three opportunities. He is allowed to recruit up to 3 new warriors in his own capital, he can build (or destroy) defences in occupied areas - for this, the corresponding card is reversed and 2 warriors are reduced to pawns – and, of course, he can move the troops and attack the enemy.

The game principle is straightforward and intuitive and also suitable for casual players as an entry-level tactics game.

boardgame: The Agents: Mission Critical Expansion Set (Second Edition)
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Agents: Mission Critical, the first expansion for The Agents, consists of forty cards, with two versions of twenty new unique mission cards that can be added to the base set. To play, simply shuffle these new missions into the existing missions, then get started.

Not compatible with previous editions' expansions due to different card size and rules changes.

Description from the publisher:

The Agents: Special Agents, the second expansion for The Agents, consists of forty cards, specifically two versions of twenty new unique agent cards that can be added to the base set. To play, simply shuffle these new agents into the existing agent deck, then get started.

Not compatible with previous editions' expansions due to different card size and rules changes.

The animals have escaped from the zoo, and you want to recapture them before other zookeepers do!

Your goal in CAPCHA! (ペアっと！) is to be the first player to capture either all seven different animals or four animals of the same type. The deck consists of 56 cards, with eight copies of the seven animal types. To play, you have a 3x3 field of animal cards face down on the table. If you reveal an animal that matches a card in hand, you've made a match, can use the special effects of the card, and can keep looking.

You can also make matches on other players' turns, depending on the cards you hold. The 3x3 field of animal cards is replenished at the end of each turn, so keep track of who lies where if you want to be a capture captain.

The Adrenaline: Team Play DLC expansion allows you to add a sixth player to the Adrenaline Deathmatch mayhem. It introduces character-specific weapons and abilities bringing new effects and experiences to the game.

Take full advantage of your actions with the unique Adrenaline Rush system and boost your epic shot.

Streamlined gameplay allows you to focus on what Adrenaline is really about: quickly grabbing ammo and start shooting. Winning conditions are set at the beginning of each game for a bigger variety of how to play and win!

A new team mode introduced in this DLC requires effective communication with your team, to combine your abilities, use the position of your opponents to your advantage, and enjoy creating sophisticated strikes!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Ice Age is approaching! The Dinos must gather as much food and supplies as they can to grow and thrive. But watch out for the volcano looming over the island...it might destroy everything!

Dice Age: Eggstinction is an entry level strategy game for 2-5 young/new game players and families to introduce them to contemporary game concepts like player asymmetry, resource collection, tech trees, and victory points. Players chose from a variety of dinosaur species and move around an island gathering goods in order to produce eggs...the first to produce 6 eggs in their nest wins the game! On your turn you'll roll a bunch of giant dice to populate the island with resources like insects, ferns, ginko, meat, and fish. Then you'll move around the island (possibly using your dino's unique ability) and gather up those resources to feed and grow your broods to produce more eggs. As you nest fills up, you'll gain new and enhanced skills to help you on your journey. But be careful when you gather goods from the dangerous areas of the island, as the volcano might erupt laying waste to valuable goods and scaring you back to safety.

Double-sided components are designed to enable competitive play between gamers and non/early-gamers at the same time and let young kids grow into the game by watching and learning from older players.

A card game based on the Science Fiction novel by A.E. van Vogt. Players meld cards to complete adventures.

Players share a common deck. The deck has 10 suits: Ship, Crew, Location, Alien, Power, Politics, Trick, Equipment, Investigation, Naxialism.  There are 20-23 cards in each suit. Each suit has a unique ability which can be activated during the action phase.  For example a Ship card can be used to draw 2 cards from the deck, a Crew card allows you to search the discard for 1 card etc.

Players take turns with each turn comprising 4 phases:

Encounter Phase

     Draw 2 cards from the deck and put them in your hand.  


Action Phase

     You may play (discard 1 card from your hand) to activate its ability.  


Resolution Phase

     If you have 1 card from each of the 10 suits in your hand, you may make an adventure meld.  Place the meld in a pile beside you. 
     If you made a meld draw 10 new cards. 


Logistics Phase

     Max hand size is 10 cards. discard excess cards. 


The first player to complete 4 adventure melds wins the game.

A promo for Legends of Andor: Journey to the North.  This was available through the Dice Tower Season 13 Indegogo campaign.

Set the tower from the base game on the rocky crag space to the left of the red symbol printed on the board showing a 4th place one of the 4 eternal flames on the tower.

"No, Varatan's Lighthouse at Klippenwacht has long since collapsed and fall into the sea." That's what the sea people said. But sometimes, far away on the northern horizon, you could see a shimmering violet light. At the eternal flame of the sort created by Hadrian wizards, perhaps? King Varatan had been a friend good wizards of the. Might have given him a gift of eternal flame for his lighthouse? What it a good omen to show the heroes the way?"

Roll a white die to  determine the letters on the legend track on which the remaining 3 eternal flames should be placed.
1 = p 
2 = Q 
3 = R 
4 = S
5 = T
6 = U

The following rule modifications apply - but only when the Narrator is standing of the letters with on eternal flame on one:

1. If you uncover a new wind card, you are allowed to ignore this card and set it aside (for example, if it is a storm). Then you get to uncover a second card instead.
You must accept this second card, whether it is a wind or a storm card card.
2. When you are fighting a creature (not a final adversary), you are allowed to first roll for the creature.
Only then will the heroes roll. In this way, you can assess the strength of the opponent and decide what tools to use - or, if you are fighting together, how many of you should participate."

boardgame: T.I.M.E Stories: Santo Tomás de Aquino
description: T.I.M.E Stories: Santo Tomás de Aquino is a prologue scenario that you can play through in anticipation of T.I.M.E Stories: Brotherhood of the Coast.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Inspired by Kyoto Shogi, Renge Shogi, designed by Michael Sandeman, is played on a 7x7 board. Each player has seven pieces, from left to right along the back rank: Silver, Pawn, Gold, King, Gold, Pawn, Silver. Whenever a piece moves (except the King), it alternately promotes and demotes:

Silver - Lance
Gold - Knight
Left Pawn - Bishop
Right Pawn - Rook

As in Shogi, players may drop captured pieces on any empty space, but with either side facing up. There are no restrictions on where players may drop pieces; for example, you may mate with a pawn drop and have two pawns on the same file.

Players may also drop pieces [b]from the opponent's hand[/b]. The only restriction is that you may not mate by dropping from the opponent's hand.

The Empathy Toy is a blindfolded puzzle game where players must recreate each other's puzzle patterns without sight. Instead they must use only their words to describe abstract shapes with no name. To succeed, players must create a common language, understand each other's perspectives, and tackle the problem collaboratively.

https://twentyonetoys.com/pages/empathy-toy

Description from the publisher:

Triggers is a card game designed to provoke animated discussion among friends. Players win point by accurately predicting how others will vote on controversial subjects. Get ready to check your privilege with questions like, “Is it OK to punch a Nazi?”, “Pineapple on pizza?” and “Is climate change an urgent priority?”.

Gameplay is simple. Each player begins with a YES and a NO card. The first player draws a card from the QUESTIONS deck and reads it out loud. All other players vote silently on the question by placing the corresponding answer card face down on the table. The first player now guesses if the majority voted yes, no or if there was a tie (if mathematically possible). If they guessed correctly, they keep the question card as a point. The turn moves clockwise to the next player. First to 5 points wins.

PRIVILEGE cards add a strategic element to the game. Use them to steal points, steal a turn, challenge someone to a staring contest or force someone to give you a compliment.

Triggers forges an environment of fun and open exchange of ideas. Players often find common ground in unlikely places and gain new perspectives on familiar topics.

Kumasi blends classic abstract strategy with collector card game elements to help redefine both genres. With intense turn based decision making and deep strategical depth players are lost in the near limitless possibilities.

Kumasi is a two-player card game where players take turns drawing and placing one card each as they try to make a four-sided shape in their color, either black of purple, on which they place one of their gems. Play continues until all cards have been placed and then quadrilaterals on the "outside" also receive a gem; the player with the most gems placed wins!

A promo card originally offered through Dice Tower's Season 13 Indiegogo campaign

Text:
Take this card if you are the first to build 7 buildings.
Worth 5 vp

boardgame: Battleground Fantasy Warfare: Monsters & Mercenaries – Ancient Blue Dragon
description:
Originally offered through Dice Tower's Season 13 Indiegogo campaign

Not playable on their own as you need two starter decks to play the game.

Battleground Fantasy Warfare is a point-based tabletop tactical wargame in which units are represented by cards.

Ancient Blue Dragon - 868 points - Elite
Colossal. Terrifying
Flying: 10.5"

Ancient Blue Dragon may breathe lightning three times during a battle. Mark off a lightning icon to give it a ranged attack of (3) 7/7 this turn. This attack is unaffected by Command Cards from either player and nothing can affect the number of attack dice (3) in this attack

To play a Command Card on Ancient Blue Dragon you must first discard two other Command Cards
family: Game: Battleground Fantasy Warfare (Your Move Games)

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 14: Wishing Well
description:
Originally offered through Dice Tower's Season 13 Indiegogo campaign

This is Queenie 14

Setup:
After you have placed 2 randomly drawn chamber tiles next to the starting tile, shuffle the new chamber with the rest of the chambers.

Rules:
Once discovered, a player inside the wishing well chamber can roll 2 key symbols to draw one treasure tile, get rid of a curse he has in front of him, or reclaim a die he has lost. To use the wishing well again, the player has to leave the chamber and re-enter it

boardgame: Cry Havoc: Secret Objective Promo Pack
description:
Originally offered through Dice Tower's Season 13 Indiegogo campaign

8 different end of game scoring cards

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Wrocław, a city in Poland.

boardgame: Kekionga!: A Dark and Bloody Battleground 1790.
description:
The struggle for the Old Northwest Territory (what would become the states of Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Michigan and Wisconsin) was terribly violent. Massacres and atrocities seemed to follow one upon the other and in 1790 the new US Government authorized its first war against the Native Americans for control of the region. A force of US regulars and militia from Kentucky, Ohio and Pennsylvania gathered at Cincinnati and drove on the Miami’s main settlement of Kekionga. The resulting fight was a horrendous defeat of the US force, and ushered in a new and more violent phase of this struggle. Can you do better?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battles of the Old Northwest (High Flying Dice Games)
Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

boardgame: St. Clair's Folly: The Battle of the Wabash River, 1791
description:
St. Clair's Folly: The Battle of the Wabash River, 1791 is a Tactical level board-wargame of the worst defeat ever suffered by the U.S. Army in the whole American Indian Wars.

Determined to wrest control of the Northwest Territory from the Native American nations and tribes inhabiting the area, and concerned by the British Government’s lack of withdrawal of troops and fortifications in the northern and eastern sections of the territory (particularly in present-day Michigan and Indiana), the US Government authorized military takeover of the region. In 1790 a small force of militia and US Regulars under General Harmer were soundly defeated by the Miami at the Maumee River near their main settlement of Kekionga. Alarmed, the US Congress and President Washington authorized the mobilization of an even larger force, this time to be led by the Territory’s first Governor and General of the Army, Arthur St. Clair. This would be the first full scale conflict waged by the new nation, and President Washington was very much concerned about the risks such an undertaking involved.

Can you do as well, or better?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battles of the Old Northwest (High Flying Dice Games)

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Adonis vs. the Lich King
description:
Description from the publisher:

Expand the party with a cocky new bard and a diabolical new mage!

The Magnificent Adonis, Bard of the Ages

This bard’s pretentious title (self-given, of course) makes him the envy of other bards… Okay, not really, but he can carry a tune and he’s good in a fight. He uses his three preferred instruments, the flute, the lyre and the drum, to cast bardic magic or just bash bad guys over the head!

The Good: Adonis is a fine specimen of a man.
The Bad: … and he won’t let you forget it!

The Lich King

Look over there! It’s the Lich King! This ancient skeletal mage has transferred part of his essence and power to his severed hand, making him highly resistant to attack by the good guys. But even this near-immortal embodiment of evil enjoys a pint at the tavern once in a while!

The Bad: Good luck getting past his formidable defenses.
The Worse: He knows how to “throw a punch.” Literally.

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Adonis vs. The Lich King expands the party at the Red Dragon Inn with a new hero and a new villain. These archenemies bring new mechanics and fun to the party, and can be combined with any Red Dragon Inn base set. The Lich King includes a side deck allowing him to be played as a boss in the Boss Battle variant introduced in The Red Dragon Inn 6: Villains. As an added bonus, this set includes custom Player Mats and Deck Dividers that are compatible with The Red Dragon Inn 5: The Character Trove!

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Lucky Rabbit's Foot Ale
description: Promotional card first made available as a Slug Crew Minor Reward in 2014, and later (2017) to anyone pre-ordering The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Adonis vs. the Lich King. Shuffle or replace a card in any Red Dragon Inn Drink Deck with Lucky Rabbit's Foot Ale to add a bit more excitement to your game!

Description from the rulebook (translated from Swedish):
Invest is a game for 2-6 players. The players takes of to more southern locations, each with 30 grand, to buy properties where they can build Bungalows and Hotels, giving them rental fees when the other players visits.

User summary:
It is essentially a Monopoly-clone but with lots of 1970s Charter-craze flair to it.
It also has a quite unique plastic game board.

Component list (Inventoried from a Thrift store copy, so there might be errors)

1x  Game board (paper wrapped in soft plastic casing)
6x  Generic plastic player tokens in different colors
1x  Small brown dice cup (perhaps not included?)
